However, it's important to remember that genetics are just one factor that determines an animal's characteristics. The way an animal is raised and its environment can also play a role in its personality, habits, and other characteristics. So while a cloned animal may have many of the same characteristics as the original animal, it may also be different in some ways.

Bourbon is a type of whiskey that is made from a mixture of grains, including at least 51% corn, and aged in oak barrels for at least two years. While it is true that some bourbon can take longer to mature, especially if it is aged for a longer period of time or if it is made using a higher-quality recipe, there are a few things that distilleries can do to increase production in the short term. 
One thing that distilleries can do is to increase the number of barrels they are producing. This can be done by increasing the number of barrels that are being filled each day, or by increasing the overall capacity of the distillery. 
Another option is to use younger barrels for aging. While older barrels can contribute more complex flavors to the bourbon, younger barrels can still produce a good quality product in a shorter amount of time. 
Distilleries can also blend different bourbons of different ages to create a consistent flavor profile. This allows them to produce more bourbon in the short term while still maintaining a high quality product. 
Finally, distilleries can also release "small batch" or "limited edition" bourbons that are made using a higher-quality recipe and aged for a longer period of time. These bourbons may not be as widely available as the distillery's regular offerings, but they can still help to meet the demand for bourbon.

When you drink liquids, your throat naturally narrows slightly to help prevent liquids from going down the wrong pipe and entering your airway. This narrowing is controlled by muscles in your throat, and it happens automatically when you swallow. 
If you try to drink too much liquid too quickly, it can be difficult for the muscles in your throat to keep up with the flow of liquid. As a result, some of the liquid may end up going down the wrong pipe and entering your airway, which can cause coughing and choking. 
Gulping liquids can help prevent this from happening by allowing you to take smaller sips of liquid that are easier for your throat muscles to control. This can make it easier to swallow the liquid without coughing or choking. 
So, while it is possible to just let liquids flow down your throat without gulping, it is generally a good idea to take smaller sips and gulp them to make it easier to swallow and prevent coughing and choking.

Salt is used on roads to help melt ice and snow and improve traction during the winter months. When it's cold outside, water can freeze on the roads and make them very slippery, which can be dangerous for cars and people. Salt helps to melt the ice and snow by lowering the freezing point of water, which means that it can help keep the roads clear and safe to travel on. 
There are other options for melting ice and snow on roads, such as using chemicals like calcium chloride or magnesium chloride, or using mechanical methods like plows or sand. However, salt is often the most effective and affordable option for many communities, especially when it's used in combination with other methods. 
It's important to note that while salt can be helpful for making roads safer during the winter, it can also have negative effects on the environment and on the cars themselves. Salt can cause corrosion on metal surfaces, including cars, and it can also harm plants and animals if it washes into nearby waterways. However, despite these potential downsides, many communities continue to use salt as a way to keep roads clear and safe during the winter.

A hydrophobic treatment is a special coating that is applied to the surface of a ship to make it more water-resistant. This means that water will bead up and roll off the surface of the ship instead of sticking to it and soaking in. 
One effect of using a hydrophobic treatment on a ship is that it can help to reduce drag and improve the ship's performance in the water. This is because water will not stick to the surface of the ship and create resistance, which can slow the ship down. 
There are a few reasons why a ship might not use a hydrophobic treatment. One reason is cost: applying a hydrophobic treatment can be expensive, especially if the ship is very large. Another reason is that the treatment may wear off over time, so it would need to be reapplied regularly to maintain its effectiveness. Finally, some hydrophobic treatments may have negative effects on the environment, so they may not be allowed in certain areas.

The CIA torture program, also known as the enhanced interrogation program, was a program run by the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) in the years following the 9/11 attacks in which terrorism suspects were subjected to various forms of torture and other forms of abuse. The program has been the subject of controversy because many people believe that torture is wrong, regardless of the circumstances. 
There are several reasons why people are angry about the CIA torture program: 
1. Many people believe that torture is wrong because it goes against fundamental human rights and dignity. The United States has long held that all people have certain inalienable rights, including the right to be free from torture and other forms of cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment. By torturing terrorism suspects, the CIA was violating these fundamental rights. 
2. The effectiveness of torture as an interrogation technique has been called into question. Many experts believe that torture is not an effective way to gather reliable information, as people who are being tortured will often say anything to make the torture stop, even if it is not true. In addition, the use of torture can create a climate of fear and mistrust, which can make it more difficult to gather accurate intelligence. 
3. The CIA torture program was carried out in secret, and many people believe that it was done in an effort to cover up the program and avoid accountability. When governments operate in secret, it can be difficult for citizens to hold them accountable for their actions, and this can undermine trust in government and the rule of law. 
4. The CIA torture program has also been criticized because it was carried out at detention facilities in other countries, including countries with a history of human rights abuses. This has led some people to question whether the United States is upholding its own values and principles when it comes to human rights. 
Overall, the CIA torture program has been a source of controversy because it involves the use of torture and other forms of abuse, which many people believe is wrong, and because it was carried out in secret and may have violated fundamental human rights.

Most of it is historical reasoning : America was founded as a representative democracy , primarily because the Founding Fathers did n't exactly trust the general public on being educated enough on every issue to vote . Those representatives are then the ones who directly vote for laws . Second reasoning is also important : under what system would people vote directly on laws ? Switzerland does it , more or less , because their country is the size of postage stamp ( apologies to any Swiss people ) compared to the United States . This was more or less impossible for the better part of American history , considering the founding of America predated the internet , or even telegraph lines . It 's difficult even now ; yes the internet may provide an avenue , but considering how we had trouble building a healthcare website , do you really think the government can build a website for voting ? And then we run into the problem of actual legislation voting : legislation is more than the things we hear about in news ( like the Affordable Care Act ) . There is important federal laws , but then there are state laws , but then there are individual counties within states too . Considering the dense amount of material that needs to be read and decided upon ( ordinances , laws , taxes , amendments ) , it 's simpler to divide each level by different government levels and have ( informed and trained ) representatives to decide for us .

Anesthesia is a medical treatment that is used to help people fall asleep or lose consciousness during a medical procedure. When a person is under anesthesia, their body is temporarily unable to feel pain or move. 
While being under anesthesia can be similar to sleeping in some ways, it is not the same as sleep. During sleep, the body naturally relaxes and becomes less aware of its surroundings. The brain and body also go through a series of changes as they rest and repair themselves. 
Anesthesia, on the other hand, is induced by medications that are given to a person before a medical procedure. These medications work to block pain signals and cause the person to lose consciousness. The person remains under the effects of the anesthesia until the medications wear off, which can take a few hours. 
In short, being under anesthesia is a temporary state that is induced by medications, while sleep is a natural process that occurs during certain times of the day and night.

It is true that no two snowflakes are exactly the same. Snowflakes form when water vapor in the air cools and condenses into ice crystals. As these ice crystals fall through the air, they can pick up additional layers of ice and water vapor, which can cause them to take on a variety of shapes and patterns. The exact shape and pattern of a snowflake is determined by the temperature, humidity, and other conditions in the air as it falls. 
Because there are so many variables that can affect the shape of a snowflake, it is practically impossible for any two snowflakes to be exactly the same. In fact, it is estimated that there are more possible snowflake shapes than there are atoms in the observable universe, so it is unlikely that we will ever run out of possible snowflake designs.

-1 + 1 = 0
-2 + 3 = 1 
So, in this case, the expression "-1 - (-1)" is equal to 1.

Businessmen can be personally liable for certain crimes even if their business has limited liability. Limited liability means that the business itself is legally separate from the owners, and the owners are generally not personally responsible for the debts and legal issues of the business. Evolution selects for successful reproduction . The entire goal of a species is to create as many successful offspring of itself and to get those offspring to a point that they can procreate on their own . Depending on the species and their breeding habits , it may take days or decades to reach sexual maturity . Once they reproduce , certain species no longer care for their young . In these cases having the mature adult compete with the offspring for food is disadvantageous . Other species require care until the young can fend for themselves . In these cases , the additional resources needed to sustain the parent for a longer period of time is outweighed by the protection / training they can afford the next generation . Species that can remain sexually active through longer periods of their life will also have less invested in each individual reproduction cycle , so evolution might select for trading caring for their young for multiple reproductive cycles throughout their lifespan . Repeat this selection process over billions of years and billions of species and you end up with the spectrum of life - spans that we see today .

Essentially Flash is a product , whereas HTML5 is an open standard agreed upon by the internet 's " governing body " . Flash is a separate program that is run embedded within the browser , whereas HTML5 is a new version of the HTML markup language used to lay out web pages that is simply rendered by the browser natively . HTML5 is a W3C standard ( it is defined by the World - Wide - Web Committee , a group that defines common standards for Internet protocols and technologies ) whereas Flash is a proprietary product , owned by Adobe . IMO ( as a Solution Architect - a person who designs large scale software systems ) and former software engineer and web developer HTML5 is far superior . The open standard thing is important as it means that all vendors * should * implement the way they render it in the same way as opposed to Flash which requires you to download and keep updated an extra program as well as your browser . HTML5 is very capable in terms of animation and etc which * used to * require Flash ( or Microsoft Silverlight , a similar product ) and performs better in every case I have seen , although as it has only just been ratified as a standard I have n't yet seen any HTML5 games , for example . I 'm sure they 're out there though !

Generally the legislature decides , and when they do , it 's generally based on geography . And yes , the absolutely can change , it 's just not as common nowadays . For example , I 'll take you through the history of Georgia 's capitals ( since I live here ) . Savannah , on the coast , was the first city established in Georgia , and it was the de facto " capital " during the time when we were still a colony ( since we were technically governed by England , but the royal governor sat in Savannah ) . When we became a state , Savannah was named the state capital mostly because it always had been . As the state expanded , the population started shifting west and so Augusta , a little further up and west , was chosen as the next capital partially because it was nearer to the new center of the population and partially because it had served as a rotating capital with Savannah whenever the British were constantly laying siege to it during the Revolution . Augusta was the capital for a little bit before they moved it to Louisville , which did n't amount to much . In 1802 , governor John Milledge sent out a party to find the new geographic center of the state and there they would establish the new capital . The surveyors found a natural spring bubbling up close enough to the center , and so decided to establish Milledgeville as the new capital ( and eventually my home town :D ) . Milledgeville served as capital through the Civil War , until 1868 . During Reconstruction , the military governor of Georgia ordered new delegates to meet in Atlanta . Atlanta became the official capital for a few reasons . First , it was a rebirth from being burned down by General Sherman ( even open - minded , progressive liberals in Georgia gag at the mention of his name ) , and it gave Georgia a new start away from its Confederate capital . Also , Milledgeville is surrounded by hills , which made it hard as hell for trains to get in . Atlanta , on the other hand , is relatively flat and actually began life as a train hub called Terminus . It also was a bigger , more modern city on the rise . So the decision was pretty easy to make , and Atlanta 's served as capital of the state ever since . TL;DR : It 's the legislature deciding where to put the capital , and they can change relatively simply . They just do n't tend to anymore .

Bit depth refers to the number of bits used to represent the amplitude (loudness) of a audio signal at a given point in time. The higher the bit depth, the more accurately the signal can be represented, which can result in a higher quality audio recording. 
A bit depth of 16 bits means that there are 16 bits available to represent the amplitude of the audio signal at each point in time. This allows for a total of 65,536 possible amplitude values, which is a relatively fine level of resolution. 
A bit depth of 24 bits means that there are 24 bits available to represent the amplitude of the audio signal at each point in time. This allows for a total of 16,777,216 possible amplitude values, which is a much finer level of resolution than 16 bits. 
In general, recordings made at a higher bit depth will have a higher quality and more dynamic range (the difference between the loudest and quietest parts of the recording). However, it's important to note that the benefits of higher bit depths may not be audible to most listeners, especially if the recording is played back on equipment with a lower bit depth or quality.

When a person receives a blood transfusion, it is very important that the blood type of the donor and the recipient are compatible. If a person receives a blood transfusion with incompatible blood, it can cause serious problems and can even be life-threatening. 
There are different blood types, and each person has one of these types: A, B, AB, or O. In addition to these blood types, there is also a factor called the Rh factor, which can be positive or negative. This means that there are eight possible blood types: A+, A-, B+, B-, AB+, AB-, O+, and O-. 
When a person receives a blood transfusion, their immune system may recognize the donated blood as foreign and attack it. This can cause a reaction called transfusion reaction, which can lead to serious complications such as anemia, kidney damage, and even death. 
To avoid transfusion reactions, doctors and hospitals carefully match the blood type of the donor and recipient before a transfusion is given. They do this by testing the blood of both the donor and recipient to determine their blood type and Rh factor. This way, they can make sure that the donated blood is compatible with the recipient's blood, and that the transfusion will be safe. 
In the past, it was common for doctors to try different blood types on patients to see which one worked best. However, this practice was very risky and led to many deaths. Today, doctors and hospitals use careful blood typing and testing to ensure that transfusions are as safe as possible.

The concept of time has been around for as long as humans have been able to think and communicate. People have always needed to keep track of time in order to know when to do things, like when to plant crops, when to go to bed, and when to meet up with friends. 
One way that people have measured time is by using the sun. They noticed that the sun rises in the east and sets in the west, and that it takes about a day for the sun to make one full trip across the sky. So, people started using the sun to measure time in days. 
Another way that people have measured time is by using the moon. The moon goes through phases, like a full moon and a new moon, and it takes about a month for the moon to go through all of its phases. So, people started using the moon to measure time in months. 
People have also used other things to measure time, like water clocks, sand clocks, and clocks with hands. Today, we use all kinds of different clocks and watches to keep track of time. 
In short, the concept of time has been around for a very long time, and people have used many different things to measure it.

Milk teeth, also known as baby teeth, are the first set of teeth that a child gets. These teeth are important because they help children learn to chew and speak properly, and they also hold space in the jaws for the permanent teeth that will come in later. 
Milk teeth are different from permanent teeth in a few ways. For one thing, they are smaller and more delicate than permanent teeth. They also have different shapes and are arranged differently in the mouth. Milk teeth also have thinner enamel, the hard outer layer that protects teeth, which makes them more prone to cavities. 
One of the reasons that children lose their milk teeth is because they are simply not strong enough to last a lifetime. They were only meant to be temporary, and once the permanent teeth start to come in, the milk teeth are no longer needed. The permanent teeth are much stronger and will be able to do a better job of chewing and biting food. 
So, in short, milk teeth are important for helping children learn to eat and speak, but they are eventually replaced by stronger, permanent teeth that are better able to do these tasks.

This meant it was the perfect weapon for developing countries without a huge industrial base - most of the third world . You did n't need huge factories and skilled workforce . The second reason was political . AK was licensed for close to nothing by the USSR to every country which wanted to get on anti - imperialist bandwagon . Combine that with the general approach to licenses in the Soviet Union ( capitalist nonsense ! ) and you essentially get an * * Open Source Assault Rifle * * . Anyone anywhere with access to basic metalworking tools could build an AK47 . That would not be possible with Western mode of arms production which was proprietary and made for profit and if someone started knocking off an M16 or FN Fal there would be court cases and other similar stuff . So if you needed cheap gun that you can manufacture at home when the need arises AK47 was your pick ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- As for the reasons why it was n't adopted by Western armies there are again two reasons which mirror the causes of Ak47 's huge * non*commercial success First was that AKs robustness , simplicity and reliability came at the expense of accuracy and weight . AK47s were barely accurate beyond 100 - 200 m and they were heavy and heavier still if made from poor quality materials . They were also relatively uncomfortable to use . An AK is a bit clunky in handling which is why they were constantly improved . Ak74 which was the 5.45 mm variant was one such improvement . The Israelis had modified the AK to produce their standard rifle IAI Galil . The main advantage of AKs were numbers and low cost . Many countries went the opposite route and manufactured more sophisticated and accurate weapons that tried to improve on the design . While Americans worked through more classic M16 to M4 the Brits , French and some other countries tried [ bullpup ] ( URL_0 ) configuration in their SA80 , AUG , FAMAS etc . Second reason was political naturally . Apart from the awkwardness of using an enemy design in the West selling arms was a huge and profitable * business * . Large European countries had arms manufacturers which wanted to do something more than produce guns on American license because a ) it is less profitable with license fees b ) less prospective for future operations . A stampede is a situation where a large group of people are rushing or running in a panicked way, often because they are trying to escape from something or get to somewhere quickly. In a stampede, people may be trampled or crushed by the weight of the crowd, which can lead to serious injuries or death. 
The cause of death in a stampede is usually due to suffocation or trauma, which can occur when people are pushed or squeezed too hard in a confined space. For example, if a large group of people are trying to get through a narrow doorway or passageway, and there is not enough room for everyone to go through at the same time, the pressure of the crowd can become very intense. People may fall down or be pushed to the ground, and if they are unable to get up or move out of the way, they may be trampled by other people who are still trying to move forward. 
It is important to stay calm and avoid panicking in a stampede situation. If you find yourself in a stampede, try to find a safe place to take cover, such as a wall or a doorway. If you are able, try to help others who may be in danger or who are in need of assistance. It is also important to follow the instructions of authorities and emergency responders, who are trained to handle stampede situations and can help to keep people safe.

Doctors can use several different methods to find out if a person has allergies. One common method is to do a skin prick test. During this test, a small amount of an allergen (such as a substance that a person might be allergic to) is placed on the skin, and then a needle is used to prick the skin through the allergen. If the person is allergic to the allergen, they will develop a raised bump or rash on their skin. 
Another way to test for allergies is to do a blood test. During this test, a blood sample is taken from the person and sent to a laboratory, where it is tested for the presence of antibodies to specific allergens. Antibodies are proteins produced by the body's immune system in response to an allergen. If the person has allergies, their blood will contain high levels of these antibodies. 
It's important to note that doctors do not usually test for allergies by exposing a person to the allergen directly. This is because allergies can cause serious reactions, such as difficulty breathing or a drop in blood pressure, and it would be dangerous to intentionally trigger these reactions. Instead, doctors use safer methods, such as skin prick tests and blood tests, to test for allergies.

During this process, your eyes become more sensitive to low levels of light, which makes it easier to see in the dark. 
Your eyes have two types of cells called rods and cones that help you see. Rods are more sensitive to low levels of light and are responsible for your night vision. Cones are more sensitive to bright light and are responsible for your color vision. When you're in a dark room, your rods are more active and your cones are less active, which is why it can be harder to see colors in the dark. 
So when you're in a dark room, it can be easier to see objects if you don't look directly at them because your rods are more sensitive to low levels of light, and looking directly at something can cause your cones to become more active, which can make it harder to see.

The Big Bang theory is the most widely accepted scientific theory for the origin of the universe. It suggests that about 13.8 billion years ago, the universe began as a singularity, a point of infinite density and temperature. At this moment, all the matter and energy in the universe were concentrated in a single, infinitely small and hot point. 
Then, for reasons that are not yet fully understood, this singularity underwent a tremendous expansion, causing the universe to rapidly cool and expand. As it expanded, the universe cooled, allowing for the formation of particles and eventually atoms. These atoms eventually coalesced into stars, galaxies, and everything else we see in the universe today. 
Before the Big Bang, it is not clear what happened or if time itself had a meaning. Some theories suggest that the Big Bang was not the beginning of the universe, but rather the result of a previous contraction or collapse of a previous universe. Others propose that the Big Bang was the beginning of our universe, but that there may be other universes with their own origins and histories. 
Overall, the Big Bang theory provides a framework for understanding the evolution of the universe, but there is still much that scientists do not understand about the early universe and what may have happened before the Big Bang.

It 's true that nuclear reactions tend to output greater energy over time than chemical ones , but chemical ones are far easier to engineer . When it comes to getting a payload into orbit , you need enough thrust to not only push your cargo into space , but also the engines and the fuel . This is the paradox of rocket science . More fuel and bigger engines mean you can go further , but also means you need more fuel and bigger engines to lift them in the first place . A nuclear reactor of any kind is a complex , heavy and cumbersome thing , certainly when compared to a ( relatively ) simple oxygen / hydrogen / kerosene ignition system . Ultimately , it 's just easier to get the required lift and speed from burning some chemicals together than it is from heating hydrogen and expelling it at high speed as would be done in a nuclear rocket . The alternative , a nuclear pulse rocket , has huge drawbacks in terms of its waste products ( see below ) . Also , consider the safety aspects . When a chemical rocket explodes - as they have a tendancy to do now and then - you 're left with a big fire , some debris and maybe some unpleasant and hazardous materials from burning plastics / metals . If a nuclear reactor expodes mid - air , you 've then got radioactive material being dispersed over a wide area . ( Consider that the Columbus disaster had wrekage spread over hundreds of square miles ) . There would n't be any nuclear detonations , the design of a nucelar reactor does n't work on the same principle as a bomb , but you would have the same or comparable fallout effects .

Atoms are made up of three types of subatomic particles: protons, neutrons, and electrons. The number of protons in an atom's nucleus determines what element the atom is. For example, all atoms with 6 protons are carbon atoms, all atoms with 8 protons are oxygen atoms, and so on. 
The properties of an atom are determined by the arrangement of its electrons. The electrons are arranged in shells around the nucleus, and each shell can hold a certain number of electrons. The outermost shell is called the valence shell, and the number of electrons in the valence shell determines how the atom will behave. 
If an atom has a full valence shell, it is stable and not very reactive. Atoms that have only a few electrons in their valence shell, on the other hand, are more reactive because they want to gain more electrons to fill their valence shell. This is why some elements, like sodium and chlorine, are very reactive: they have only one or a few electrons in their valence shells and are eager to gain or lose those electrons to become more stable. 
So to sum up: the number of protons in an atom's nucleus determines what element it is, and the arrangement of electrons around the nucleus determines the atom's properties.

When you 're reading you 're doing two things . The first thing you do is recognizing word images and stringing them up into sentences . Recognizing word images is ' mechanical ' in the sense that any skill you 've learned to do is mechanical , like driving a car or riding a bike or playing tennis or playing tetris or eating with a knife and fork and so on . You 're just going through the motions , basically , and because you 've practiced those skills so much simply by doing them often you can exercise them even without being aware of them . This is why you can drive home from work and think about that way too short skirt Stacy was wearing and what a horrible skank she is at the same time . The second thing you do when you 're reading is interpreting or imagining what happens in the story . This requires your attention , and is not something you can do on auto - pilot . When your attention drifts from what you were imagining , what you were imagining goes away , and you start imagining whatever it is your attention drifted to . When you 're reading and you suddenly find you 've not retained anything for the last couple of lines / paragraphs / pages , you 've been executing the mechanical skill of reading but not the interpretative or imaginative skill of reading . Usually this does n't matter , because most tasks only require one or the other , but when it happens when you 're reading you do notice it because reading requires both . edit : what happens in your brain is that neurons fire based on ion levels on different sides of membranes and not much else . Because of the way the brain has evolved , contemporary neuroscience has a really hard time pinning any phenomenological ( basically , something we notice in our experience ) effect to a specific pattern of neural activation . That does n't stop them from trying ( just look at the enormity of the neural correlates of consciousness literature for example ) but it does mean that a real answer to your question in terms of brain ( rather than mind ) processes is still a way off , scientifically speaking .

People can be shy for a variety of reasons. Some people may be shy because they are naturally introverted, which means that they tend to feel more comfortable being alone or with a small group of close friends, rather than being in large groups or around new people. Others may be shy because they are anxious about social situations, and may worry about what others think of them or about making mistakes. 
Still others may be shy because of past negative experiences, such as being teased or bullied, which can make them feel self-conscious or afraid of being rejected. Some people may also be shy because of cultural or social norms that discourage them from speaking up or expressing themselves. 
Overall, shyness is a normal and common human emotion, and it's something that many people experience at some point in their lives. It's important to remember that everyone is different and that it's okay to be shy. With time and practice, many people are able to overcome their shyness and become more confident in social situations.

By measuring the strength of the signals from all of these networks, it can triangulate its position and figure out exactly where it is. This is much more accurate than just using the cellular network, which can only give a rough estimate of your location. 
So, when an app asks you to turn on your WiFi, it's because it needs to use these nearby WiFi networks to help determine your location more accurately.

There are a few reasons why consumers might not be able to collectively refuse to pay their debts and why lenders might be able to take action if they do. 
First, when you take out a loan or credit card, you typically agree to a contract that says you will repay the debt according to certain terms. This contract is legally binding, which means that if you don't pay your debt as agreed, the lender can take action to try to get their money back. This might include sending your account to collections, suing you in court, or garnishing your wages (which means they can take a portion of your paycheck to pay off your debt). 
Second, even if a large group of people all decided not to pay their debts at the same time, it's not clear what the outcome would be. It's possible that lenders would be unable to collect on their debts and might have to write them off as losses, which could cause them financial difficulties. This could lead to higher interest rates or stricter lending requirements for everyone in the future, as lenders try to mitigate the risk of default. 
Finally, there might be consequences for individual consumers who don't pay their debts. This could include damage to their credit score, which could make it harder for them to get approved for loans or credit cards in the future, as well as legal action taken against them by lenders. 
It's important to remember that taking on debt is a serious responsibility, and it's important to be sure you can afford to repay your debts before you borrow money. If you're having trouble repaying your debts, it's usually a better idea to work with your lenders to try to find a solution rather than simply refusing to pay. There are often options available, such as refinancing or restructuring your loans, that can help you get back on track.

The official internet domains for the United Kingdom, Australia, and other countries do not end with ".uk" or ".au." Instead, they use the top-level domains (TLDs) ".co.uk" and ".com.au," respectively. These TLDs are part of the Domain Name System (DNS), which is a system that translates domain names (like google.com) into numerical IP addresses that computers can understand and use to communicate with each other over the internet. 
The TLDs ".co.uk" and ".com.au" are used to indicate the country where a website is registered or based. In the case of the United Kingdom, the ".co.uk" TLD is used for commercial websites, while the ".uk" TLD is used for other types of websites, such as government, education, and personal websites. G force suits, also known as anti-g suits, are specially designed clothing that helps pilots to withstand the intense forces they experience when flying in high-performance aircraft, such as fighter jets. These suits work by using inflatable bladders that are built into the clothing, which can be inflated or deflated as needed to help counteract the effects of gravity on the pilot's body. 
When a pilot flies an aircraft at high speeds or makes sharp turns, the force of gravity (also known as G-forces) can put a lot of strain on the pilot's body. This can cause blood to pool in the pilot's lower extremities, leading to a condition called "g-induced loss of consciousness" (G-LOC). To prevent this from happening, pilots wear G force suits that are designed to help keep the blood flowing to the pilot's head and brain, even when the body is subjected to intense G-forces. 
The way the suit works is fairly simple. When the pilot experiences high G-forces, the inflatable bladders in the suit inflate, applying pressure to the pilot's legs and abdomen. This helps to keep the blood from pooling in the lower part of the body and instead keeps it flowing to the brain, which is essential for maintaining consciousness. When the G-forces are reduced, the bladders deflate, allowing the blood to flow normally again. 
G force suits are an essential piece of equipment for pilots who fly high-performance aircraft, as they help to keep them safe and able to perform their duties even in the most demanding conditions. I hope this helps to explain how G force suits work!

A grand jury is a group of people who are chosen to decide whether there is enough evidence to charge someone with a crime. If the grand jury decides that there is enough evidence, they will issue a "true bill" of indictment, which means that the person will be charged with a crime and will have to go to trial. 
Grand juries are different from normal juries in a few ways. One difference is that grand juries don't decide whether someone is guilty or not guilty. Their job is only to decide if there is enough evidence to charge someone with a crime. Grand juries also don't have to follow the same rules as a normal trial. For example, the person being investigated by the grand jury doesn't have the right to be present or to have a lawyer present during the grand jury proceedings. 
It's not uncommon for police officers to appear before a grand jury when they are being investigated for a crime. This might happen if someone has accused the police officer of wrongdoing, or if there is evidence that the officer broke the law. If the grand jury decides that there is enough evidence to charge the police officer with a crime, then the officer will have to go to trial to face those charges.

Flash and HTML5 are both technologies that are used to create and display content on the internet. Flash is a software platform that was developed by Adobe and is used to create animations, games, and other interactive content. HTML5 is a version of the HTML programming language that is used to create the structure and layout of websites and web applications. 
As a user, it's important to know that Flash and HTML5 are not directly competing technologies. They are simply two different ways of creating and displaying content on the internet, and each has its own strengths and weaknesses. 
Here are some key differences between Flash and HTML5: 
Compatibility: Flash requires a separate plug-in to be installed in your web browser in order to work, while HTML5 is built into most modern browsers and does not require any additional software. This means that HTML5 content is generally more widely compatible and can be accessed on a wider range of devices, including smartphones and tablets.
Performance: HTML5 content tends to perform better than Flash content, especially on mobile devices. This is because HTML5 content is designed to be lightweight and efficient, while Flash content can be more resource-intensive and may not run as smoothly on some devices.
Security: Flash has a reputation for being less secure than HTML5, as it is more susceptible to malware and other types of online threats. HTML5, on the other hand, has built-in security features that help to protect against these types of threats. 
Ultimately, which technology is "better" for you as a user will depend on your specific needs and preferences. If you need to access interactive content or games on the internet, then Flash may be a good option for you. If you are primarily interested in accessing static content (such as text and images) or using web-based applications, then HTML5 may be a better choice.

ANY cell can get cancer . Your professor is likely citing a statistic that suggests that breastfeeding lowers chances of cancer significantly , but I think it 's naive to say milk production completely stops cancer development . Cancer is a specific mutation of your cells that basically means your cells are n't told to die anymore . They grow uncontrollably . I 'm not sure of the correlation between breastfeeding ( or milk production ) and cancer growth , but * all * cells can mutate in this way . It 's just that ; like smoking increases the risk of this mutation , so can breastfeeding reduce that risk . Frighteningly , * anyone * can develop cancer . But , to bring this full circle , I do n't see how producing milk necessarily would stop cancer development on its own .

Of all the things that mutations cause ( that is , mutations that happen * after * a person has been born ) , most of them probably just end up with the mutated DNA getting repaired or removed , or the cell that contains it being destroyed through programmed cell death . Cancer is the result of a mutation that removes the cell 's ability to repair or quarantine mutations . It is n't that so much of DNA is devoted to reproduction that any one mutation is likely to damage that section of the DNA . It 's that , of all the mutations that happen , the ones that can survive and reproduce are necessarily the ones that alter the normal reproduction / cell death processes ( and these are the mutations that cause cancer . )

In Western music, the traditional musical alphabet consists of the letters A, B, C, D, E, F, and G. These letters represent the seven "natural" notes of the diatonic scale, which is a scale that consists of seven notes in an octave. 
In the diatonic scale, the notes are spaced apart by specific intervals, or distances, between the pitches of the notes. These intervals are called whole steps and half steps. A whole step is an interval that consists of two half steps, and a half step is the smallest interval used in Western music. 
In the diatonic scale, the notes are spaced apart as follows: 
A to B: whole step
B to C: half step
C to D: whole step
D to E: whole step
E to F: half step
F to G: whole step
G to A: whole step 
As you can see, there is only a half step between the notes B and C, and between the notes E and F. This means that there is no room for a B sharp or an E sharp in the diatonic scale. 
However, it is possible to add accidentals, which are symbols that raise or lower the pitch of a note by a half step. For example, you can use a sharp symbol (#) to raise the pitch of a note by a half step, or a flat symbol (b) to lower the pitch of a note by a half step. This is how B# and E# (or Fb, which is the same thing) can be represented in music notation.

For example, if you want to play a song that has DRM on it, you may need to enter a special code or password in order to unlock it. 
DRM can be controversial because it can make it more difficult for people to use the content that they have purchased in the way that they want to. For example, if you buy a song from a store and it has DRM on it, you may not be able to play it on all of your devices, or you may have to jump through some hoops to get it to work. This can be frustrating for users, especially if they feel like they are paying for something that they can't fully use. 
There are also concerns about DRM being used to track users or limit their freedom to use the content in certain ways. Some people believe that DRM is unnecessary and that it does more harm than good, while others argue that it is necessary to protect the rights of content creators and prevent piracy.

Uninstalling an application and deleting it are two different things. When you delete an application, you are simply removing the app from your device. The files that the app created on your device will also be deleted. This means that the app will no longer be visible on your device and you won't be able to use it. 
On the other hand, when you uninstall an application, the app is not only removed from your device, but any system files and settings associated with the app are also removed. This includes things like registry entries and configuration files. An uninstaller is a program that helps you uninstall an application from your device by removing all of these associated files and settings. 
Many apps come with an uninstaller because they create system files and settings on your device when they are installed. These files and settings need to be removed when the app is uninstalled in order to completely remove the app from your device and restore your system to its original state. 
In short, deleting an app simply removes the app from your device, while uninstalling an app removes the app and any associated files and settings from your device.

When you fall asleep, your body goes through a series of changes. Here's what happens: 
1. Your muscles start to relax: As you start to fall asleep, your muscles begin to relax, especially in your face, neck, and limbs. You might notice that your arms and legs feel heavy and you might feel a tingling sensation in your hands and feet. 
2. Your breathing and heart rate slow down: As you fall deeper into sleep, your breathing and heart rate slow down. You might notice that your breaths become slower and deeper, and your heart rate becomes more regular. 
3. Your brain waves change: There are different types of brain waves, and the type of brain wave you have depends on what you're doing. When you're awake, your brain waves are fast and active. But as you fall asleep, your brain waves slow down and become more regular. 
4. Your body temperature drops: As you fall asleep, your body temperature drops slightly. This is why you might feel cold when you first go to bed, but then warm up as you fall asleep. 
5. Your body enters different stages of sleep: There are two main types of sleep: REM (rapid eye movement) sleep and non-REM sleep. Non-REM sleep is divided into three stages: light sleep, deep sleep, and very deep sleep. As you fall asleep, you move through these stages of sleep in a cycle that lasts about 90 minutes.

Vanilla is a type of flavoring that is made from the seed pods of a tropical climbing orchid plant called Vanilla planifolia. These seed pods are green when they are picked from the plant, but they turn black when they are dried and cured. 
The vanilla flavoring that we use in cooking and baking is made by extracting the flavor from the cured, black seed pods. The extract is a dark brown, almost black, color. However, when we use vanilla extract in our recipes, it is usually only a small amount, and it gets mixed in with other ingredients, so it doesn't have a noticeable effect on the overall color of the finished dish.

Movies that are not originally filmed in 3D can be converted into 3D in post-production, which means after the movie has already been filmed and is being prepared for release. To do this, the movie is first divided into two separate images, one for the left eye and one for the right eye. These images are then slightly offset from each other to create the illusion of depth when viewed through special glasses that have one red lens and one blue lens (or one green and one magenta). 
The process of converting a 2D movie into 3D is complex and time-consuming, and it involves using computer software to analyze the images and assign different levels of depth to different objects in the frame. This allows the 3D version of the movie to have a sense of depth and perspective that makes it feel more immersive and realistic. 
So, to sum it up, movies that are not originally filmed in 3D can be made to look like they were filmed in 3D by using special computer software to create two slightly offset images and adding depth to different objects in the frame. When these images are viewed through special glasses, they create the illusion of 3D.

A touchscreen works by using an electric current to detect when and where you touch the screen. The screen itself is made of a material that conducts electricity, and your finger is also a conductor of electricity. When you touch the screen with your finger, you interrupt the flow of electricity, and the phone's touchscreen can detect this interruption and figure out where on the screen you touched. 
A screen protector is made of a material that does not conduct electricity, so it does not interrupt the flow of electricity when you touch the screen. This is why you can still use a touchscreen with a screen protector on it. 
Gloves, on the other hand, are made of insulating materials that do not conduct electricity. This means that when you touch the screen with a gloved finger, the flow of electricity is not interrupted, and the touchscreen cannot detect your touch. 
A stylus is a special pen-like tool that you can use to write or draw on a touchscreen. It has a small, conductive tip that can interrupt the flow of electricity when you touch the screen with it. This allows the touchscreen to detect your touch even if you are using a stylus instead of your finger. 
Conductive thread is a special type of thread that is made of a material that conducts electricity. It is often used in clothing or other fabric items to create touch-sensitive surfaces or to connect electronic components to each other. Copper wire is also a conductor of electricity, and it is often used in electronics and electrical systems for this reason.

Allergies are a type of immune system reaction that occurs when the body mistakes a normally harmless substance, such as pollen or peanuts, for a harmful invader. When this happens, the body releases chemicals called histamines in an attempt to protect itself, which can cause symptoms like sneezing, itchy eyes, and trouble breathing. 
It's not clear why some people develop allergies and others don't. It's thought that a combination of genetic and environmental factors may play a role. For example, people who have a family history of allergies may be more likely to develop them, and certain environmental exposures (such as being exposed to cigarette smoke or pollution) may also increase the risk. 
It's also not clear whether allergies are beneficial or not. Some scientists believe that allergies may have evolved as a way to protect against certain parasites or infections, but this is still an area of active research and debate. 
There are some potential benefits to having allergies, such as increased sensitivity to certain substances or a heightened immune response. However, allergies can also be very disruptive to daily life and can cause significant discomfort or even be life-threatening in severe cases.

Private banks, also known as commercial banks, do not have the ability to create money out of thin air. Instead, they are regulated financial institutions that must follow certain rules and regulations set by government agencies and central banks. 
When a person or business deposits money into a private bank, the bank is required to hold a certain amount of that money in reserve, which can be used to cover any withdrawals that the depositor may request. The bank can then lend out the remaining money to other people or businesses in the form of loans. This is how private banks make most of their profits. 
Private banks are not allowed to simply create new money and add it to their own accounts or the accounts of their customers. This would be considered fraud and is illegal. 
Electronic transactions between private banks are tracked through a centralized system called the Automated Clearing House (ACH), which is operated by the National Automated Clearing House Association (NACHA). This system allows banks to securely transfer money between each other and keep track of all the transactions that have taken place. 
In summary, private banks are regulated financial institutions that must follow certain rules and regulations to prevent them from creating money out of thin air. They make most of their profits by lending out the money that their customers deposit with them, and electronic transactions between private banks are tracked through a centralized system called the ACH.

There are a few reasons why veterinarians usually prefer to neuter animals (i.e., remove their testicles) rather than performing a vasectomy (i.e., cutting the vas deferens to block the flow of sperm). 
First, a vasectomy is a more complicated and invasive surgery than neutering. It requires a higher level of surgical skill and specialized equipment, and the recovery period is typically longer for the animal. In contrast, neutering is a relatively straightforward procedure that can often be performed under general anesthesia in a veterinary clinic setting. 
Second, neutering is more effective at reducing unwanted behaviors in animals, such as aggression, marking territory, and roaming. This is because neutering removes the source of hormones (testosterone) that contribute to these behaviors. A vasectomy, on the other hand, only blocks the flow of sperm and does not significantly reduce the production of hormones. 
Finally, neutering is a more cost-effective option for pet owners, as it is generally less expensive than a vasectomy. 
Overall, while a vasectomy might seem more humane in theory, in practice it is usually a less practical and effective option for preventing unwanted pregnancies and modifying behavior in animals.

There isn't anything inherently "right" about the number 4 when it comes to music. Ultimately, what makes a rhythm appealing is how it feels to the listener and how it fits with the other elements of the music.

I 'm actually taking some fire investigation classes for work in the near future . It 's fascinating stuff really . The biggest variance is the extent of the damage from a fire . A single wide that is burnt to the foundation will be very difficult to determine a starting point or cause , but can still be done based on the extent of charring on the surviving beams . A home that is gutted by a fire is much easier to work with , things such as the charring pattern of the beams can be used to trace where the fire was hottest or coolest , and samples of the materials in that area can be collected to determine if an accelerant is present . The presence of melted or discolored metals can also be used to trace the path of a fire , using the melting and oxidizing points of the various metals to determine where the fire is hottest . Another interesting fact , evidence collected from a fire scene is placed in an airtight container , and the fumes that are released from the material are used to determine if an accelerant is present , and even tell you what sort of accelerant it is . As far as the cause of the fire , once you determine the starting point of the fire , you can deduce the cause of the fire , even without any actual evidence present . I.E. the fire started in the center of the living room - interview the occupants to determine what would have been in that area , work from there . Samuel Johnson was British , and compiled the first dictionary of the language in Britain . He believed that the spelling of a word should reflect its origin , so he left the " u " in words like colour and flavour , along with other spellings like programme . Daniel Webster was American and compiled the first dictionary of the language in the U.S. He felt that spelling should be simplified , so left out extra letters in color and flavor , shortened program , etc . When you breathe heavily in cold weather, the cold air can irritate the lining of your throat, causing it to feel sore or burn. This is especially likely if the air is dry, as dry air can further irritate the throat. 
To help prevent this from happening, you can try the following things: 
1. Wear a scarf or other face covering over your mouth and nose when you go jogging in cold weather. This will help warm the air before you breathe it in. 
2. Stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water before and after your jog. This will help keep your throat moist, which can reduce irritation. 
3. Try to breathe through your nose instead of your mouth. Your nose has tiny hairs called cilia that help filter out irritants, so breathing through your nose can help protect your throat. 
4. Take breaks to warm up and drink something warm, like tea or broth. This can help soothe your throat and keep it from getting too dry or irritated. 
5. If your throat is already feeling sore, you can try sucking on a throat lozenge or drinking something warm with honey to help soothe the irritation.

There are a few reasons why rotisserie chickens might cost less than raw whole chickens at the grocery store. 
One reason is that it costs money to raise, process, and package raw chickens for sale, and these costs are reflected in the price of the raw chicken. When people drink alcohol, it can affect their brain and change the way they think and behave. Alcohol is a depressant, which means it slows down the central nervous system and can make people feel more relaxed and less inhibited. This can make it easier for people to say things they might not normally say when they are sober, or to show emotions they might normally try to hide. 
Alcohol can also affect the part of the brain that controls judgment and decision-making, which can make it harder for people to think clearly and make good decisions. This can lead to people saying or doing things that they might regret later. 
It's important to remember that it's never okay to drink and drive, and that alcohol can also have serious negative effects on people's health if they drink too much. It's always a good idea to drink responsibly and to be aware of how alcohol might affect you.

Honey does not spoil in the sense that it will not rot or go bad in the same way that many other food products do. However, honey can still change over time, and the expiration date on the bottle is there to give you an idea of how long the honey is likely to remain at its best quality.

However, honey can still change in other ways. For example, it can crystallize or thicken over time, and the flavor and color of honey can also change. These changes are generally not harmful, but they can affect the quality and taste of the honey. 
The expiration date on the bottle is there to give you an idea of how long the honey is likely to remain at its best quality. After the expiration date, the honey may still be safe to eat, but it may not taste or look as good as it did when it was first bottled. If you have tasted honey past the expiration date on the bottle and noticed a strange taste, it is likely that the honey has changed in some way and is no longer at its best quality.

Existentialism is a philosophical way of thinking about the world and our place in it. It is concerned with questions about the meaning of life, and what it means to be human. 
One of the main ideas of existentialism is that each person is responsible for creating their own meaning in life. This means that we have the freedom to choose how we want to live, and that we are responsible for the choices we make. 
Existentialism also emphasizes the importance of individual experience and subjective perspective. It suggests that each person's experience of the world is unique, and that it is up to each individual to decide what is important and meaningful to them. 
Existentialism can be a difficult concept to understand, but one way to think about it is that it is a way of looking at the world that emphasizes the importance of being true to oneself and living a life that is meaningful to you.

The American War of Independence, also known as the American Revolutionary War, was a conflict fought between Great Britain and the 13 colonies that later became the United States of America. It was not a British civil war because it did not involve people within Britain fighting against each other. Rather, it was a war between a sovereign nation (Great Britain) and a group of colonies that were seeking to break away from British rule and become an independent nation. 
The colonies were not happy with being ruled by Great Britain and wanted to have more control over their own affairs. They formed an alliance and began fighting against the British military, which was trying to maintain control over the colonies. Eventually, after many years of fighting, the colonies were successful in gaining their independence and became the United States of America. 
So, while the conflict may have involved people from Britain fighting against people from the colonies, it was not a civil war within Britain itself. It was a war between two separate groups who had different goals and desires.

People who have the A antigen on their red blood cells are said to have type A blood, while people who have the B antigen on their red blood cells are said to have type B blood. People who have both A and B antigens on their red blood cells are said to have type AB blood, and people who have neither A nor B antigens on their red blood cells are said to have type O blood. 
Now, why do we have different blood types in the first place? Well, that's because different blood types have different functions in the body. For example, type A blood is good at protecting against certain diseases, while type B blood is good at carrying oxygen to the body's tissues. 2. Depth: Lakes are typically not very deep, so if a spacecraft were to land in one, it would have to do so at a very low altitude. This would not give the spacecraft enough time to slow down and make a safe landing. 
3. Control: When a spacecraft returns to Earth, it needs to be able to control its descent and land in a specific location. This is not possible when landing in a lake, as the spacecraft would be at the mercy of the wind and currents. 
4. Recovery: Finally, it would be very difficult to recover a spacecraft that has landed in a lake. Specialized equipment would be needed to lift it out of the water, which could be time-consuming and costly. 
In summary, landing a spacecraft in a lake is not a safe or practical option. Instead, spacecraft are designed to land on solid ground, where they can touch down at a controlled speed and be easily recovered.

A transformer is a device that uses electricity to transfer energy from one circuit to another through a process called electrical induction. It does this by using coils of wire and a magnetic field. 
Inside a transformer, there are two coils of wire called the primary coil and the secondary coil. The primary coil is connected to a source of electricity, such as a power outlet, and the secondary coil is connected to a device that needs electricity, such as a lamp. 
When electricity flows through the primary coil, it creates a magnetic field around the coil. This magnetic field then causes a current to flow in the secondary coil, which creates its own magnetic field. The two magnetic fields interact with each other, causing energy to be transferred from the primary coil to the secondary coil. 
The amount of energy that is transferred depends on the number of coils in each of the primary and secondary coils. If the secondary coil has fewer coils than the primary coil, it will receive less energy, which means the voltage of the electricity in the secondary circuit will be lower than the voltage in the primary circuit. On the other hand, if the secondary coil has more coils than the primary coil, it will receive more energy, which means the voltage of the electricity in the secondary circuit will be higher than the voltage in the primary circuit.

The GPA system is a way of measuring academic achievement in the United States that assigns a numerical value to a student's grades. The 4-point GPA scale is the most commonly used scale in the United States, and it assigns a numerical value to each letter grade, with 4.0 being the highest possible GPA and 0.0 being the lowest. 
There are a few reasons why the GPA system is used in the United States instead of percentage grades. First, the GPA system allows for a more nuanced understanding of a student's grades. While a percentage grade gives a general idea of how well a student has performed, the GPA system allows for more precise comparisons between students. 
Second, the GPA system is more flexible than percentage grades. For example, if a student receives a 90% on one assignment and an 80% on another, their average percentage grade would be 85%. However, if the first assignment was worth twice as much as the second assignment, the student's grades would be weighted differently, and the GPA would reflect that. 
Finally, the GPA system is more widely used in the United States, so it is easier for universities to compare the grades of students from different schools. This can be particularly helpful when evaluating the academic achievements of international students, as different countries may use different grading systems. 
Overall, the GPA system is a convenient and widely accepted way of measuring academic achievement in the United States. It allows for more precise comparisons between students and is more flexible than percentage grades.

It is possible that allergies could eventually be phased out by evolution through natural selection, but it is not a straightforward process. 
Allergies are a type of immune system response to certain substances that are normally harmless to most people. The substances that can trigger an allergic reaction are called allergens. In some people, the immune system mistakes these allergens as harmful and mounts an excessive response to them. This can cause symptoms like sneezing, itchy eyes, and difficulty breathing. 
Genetics play a role in whether or not a person develops allergies. If a person has a family history of allergies, they are more likely to develop allergies themselves. However, genetics are not the only factor. Environmental factors, such as exposure to allergens and other substances, can also affect the development of allergies. 
Natural selection is the process by which certain traits become more or less common in a population over time. The traits that are most beneficial to survival and reproduction are more likely to be passed on to future generations. If a trait is harmful or disadvantageous, it is less likely to be passed on. 
In theory, if a trait like allergies was consistently harmful and caused people to have a lower chance of surviving and reproducing, it could eventually be phased out through natural selection. However, this process can take a long time and is not guaranteed. 
There is also the possibility that allergies could be beneficial to some people in certain situations. For example, allergies to certain substances could help protect against certain diseases or parasites. In these cases, the trait might not be selected against, and could even be passed on to future generations. 
So, to answer your question, it is possible that allergies could eventually be phased out through natural selection, but it is not a simple process and is influenced by many factors.

The grand jury is a preliminary proceeding for the purpose of deciding whether there is a good enough reason to have a trial . The difference between a grand jury and a normal jury ( i.e. , a trial ) is that a grand jury is a closed , one - sided hearing where the prosecutor is tasked with demonstrating that there is probable cause to believe that a defendant committed the particular crime . Usually , a two - thirds supermajority agreement among the jurors is required to " indict " the defendant ( i.e. , two - thirds of the jurors must agree that there exists probable cause to believe the defendant committed a crime , thus warranting a full trial ) . If two - thirds of the jurors indict the defendant , it does not mean he is convicted . It means there is enough evidence that there should be a full trial . Unlike during the grand jury , at a full trial the defendant enjoys his sixth amendment rights to counsel , to confront his accusers , and to a public proceeding . What I mean is that during a grand jury the defendant has no right to call his own witnesses , nor cross - examine witnesses that testify against him ( or more accurately have a lawyer do those things ) , and he has no right to public oversight . During a trial the defendant has a right to a lawyer , who can introduce evidence and call witnesses on his behalf and cross - examine opposing witnesses in an a hearing opened to public oversight . Also , the trial requires the jury to unanimously vote that he committed the crime beyond a reasonable doubt in order to convict him . This is a much harder standard than the grand jury 's 2/3 's majority vote for probable cause . It 's also worth noting that not all states employ grand juries . For example , in my state a preliminary hearing substitutes for the grand jury . During my state 's preliminary hearings , the prosecutor is tasked with introducing evidence before a judge ( without a jury ) , and the defendant has the right to have a lawyer argue against that evidence and cross examine the prosecutor 's witnesses . In this scenario the judge ( and not a jury ) decides whether there is enough probable cause for the case to go to trial . This is not to say that some states only do grand juries , and other states only do preliminary hearings . My state has certain policies where it will use a grand jury rather than a preliminary hearing , e.g. , where there is a danger of witness intimidation . I do n't know whether my state would use a grand jury for a capital case against a cop . Technically it would be at the District Attorney 's discretion , but it 's probably really just politics . Also , it should be noted that the rules regarding whether or not there must be a grand jury is different in federal courts . The fifth amendment preserves a defendant 's right to a grand jury for all " capital , or otherwise infamous crime[s ] " in federal courts . To sum it up , a grand jury is a process with a very low burden of proof for the purpose of deciding whether a full trial is necessary . Some states use preliminary hearings more often . Serious federal crimes require grand juries . And , some states courts use grand juries rather than preliminary hearings for specific cases .

Imagine the fertilised egg cells are actually just two regular chicken eggs . The yolk is the nucleus of the cell , this contains the combined DNA of the mother and father of the child . The vast majority of the child 's DNA is in this yolk , but there is some DNA in the egg white . This is called mitochondrial DNA . Mitochondria are the powerplants of cells that provide them with the energy they need for their various processes . If there 's a problem with this mitochondrial DNA this can cause all sorts of horrible birth defects , including severe brain damage . So all they are doing is scooping up the egg yolk ( nucleus ) and putting it in the egg white from a different egg ( which has healthy mitochondrial DNA ) . This donated egg comes from a separate woman all together , which is where the third person comes from . But only a tiny amount of this persons DNA will wind up in the child . The great thing about this kind of treatment is that once the child is born , there 's no chance of them passing on the mitochondrial problems to their children . This technology has the potential to wipe out this horrifically tragic illness and i 'm incredibly proud my country is the first to allow this to become a reality .

In the United States, churches and other religious organizations are generally exempt from paying federal income tax. This tax exemption is based on the principle of separation of church and state, which is reflected in the First Amendment to the Constitution. 
The First Amendment protects the freedom of religion, and one way it does this is by preventing the government from interfering with the operations of religious organizations. This includes not requiring them to pay taxes on their income. 
However, it's important to note that religious organizations are still subject to other taxes, such as property taxes on any buildings or land they own. They are also required to follow all other laws and regulations, just like any other organization. 
It's also worth noting that not all religious organizations are exempt from paying taxes. For example, churches and other religious organizations may lose their tax-exempt status if they engage in political activities or if they do not meet certain requirements, such as filing annual tax returns or disclosing certain financial information.

Correlation refers to a relationship between two things, where one thing (called a "variable") might have an effect on the other. For example, if you eat more ice cream, you might get more cavities. In this case, there is a correlation between eating ice cream and getting cavities, because one thing (eating ice cream) seems to be related to the other (getting cavities). 
Causation refers to a relationship where one thing (the cause) directly causes another thing (the effect). In the example of eating ice cream and getting cavities, the causation would be that eating ice cream directly causes you to get cavities. 
However, just because two things are correlated does not mean that one thing is causing the other. There might be other factors that are causing both things to happen. For example, if you eat more ice cream and also brush your teeth less often, you might get more cavities. In this case, the lack of tooth brushing is causing the cavities, not the ice cream. 
So, to sum up: correlation means that two things seem to be related, but causation means that one thing directly causes the other. Just because two things are correlated does not necessarily mean that one thing is causing the other.

The sensation of falling in dreams is actually a very common experience and can be caused by a variety of factors. One reason people may experience this sensation is due to changes in their body position while they are sleeping. When you are lying down, your body is in a state of relative stillness. However, if you move or shift positions while you are sleeping, your body may perceive this movement as falling. This can trigger a reflex that causes you to feel like you are falling or to experience a sensation of vertigo. 
Another reason people may experience the sensation of falling in their dreams is due to changes in their brain activity while they are sleeping. During the dreaming stage of sleep, known as rapid eye movement (REM) sleep, the brain is more active and may produce vivid and sometimes strange or surreal dreams. In some cases, the sensation of falling may be part of the dream content and may be related to the dreamer's feelings or emotions. 
Finally, the sensation of falling in dreams may be related to the way our brains process and interpret sensory information while we are asleep. During sleep, our brains are still receiving and processing sensory information from our surroundings, even though we are not consciously aware of it. This sensory information may be incorporated into our dreams and may contribute to the vividness and realism of the dream experience. 
Overall, the sensation of falling in dreams is a normal and common experience that is caused by a combination of factors related to body position, brain activity, and sensory processing during sleep.

The short answer is , because it gives the information the Universities care about . A longer version ... for grades , generally a University cares about 2 things , which are closely related . How well a student has mastered material , and how capable a student is of mastering material . If a student is about average , they should be receiving a C , good , B , amazing , A. So why does n't this translate directly to percentages ? In short , because not all professors are good at translating an assessment of their knowledge to standard percentages . Take something like Quantum Physics ; I 've had a class where the average on a midterm was ~20 - 30 % , with the amazing students getting 40 % . Now , that may speak to the professor being terrible ( for some definition of terrible ) , it may speak to terrible students , or it may be because the test was designed to be so difficult that a student could only complete 2 - 3 out of 5 problems in the time given , but half the test was in recognizing which of those problems a student could complete best . If the whole class is designed like this , and " percentages " are used , someone outside looking at the scores from the class would simply only view a student 's score in isolation , see it as depressingly low , and view it as a mark against the student , even if they had the highest score in the class . Grades , then , are a way for the professor of the class to say " Well , I was using a teaching / grading method that does n't match up exactly to your expectation of 90%+ = Great student , anything below 60 is awful , but here 's a way to view my evaluations in a standardized way . "
