Because smart , educated people are often very , very good at over - thinking things . They question everything . They do n't accept authority as the final word on anything . And they 're fantastic at coming up with " logical " justifications for purely emotional reactions . No one 's more susceptible to confirmation bias , than a person who is already very good at collecting and assembling data . This guy actually wrote a book about it : [ Smart People Believe Weird Things ] ( URL_0 ) As for how this relates to vaccines in particular ... Parents usually have a gut aversion to harming their own child . Giving a child a vaccine involves - at the very least ! - tears , fever , and a swollen arm . Parents need to be fairly committed to want to jab their kid with a needle in the first place . Now toss into the mix heart - wrenching stories of dead babies and disabled children , along with a soupcon of conspiracy theorizing ( Big Pharma , Big Brother ) , and a general fear of the unknown ( it 's not natural ! ) . And you 've got the perfect recipe for an educated anti - vaxxer . I completely understand where they 're coming from . When I got pregnant with my first child , I read everything I could get my hands on . I had to sort through solid science and crunchy granola pseudo science , and a General Arts degree really does n't equip you to do that well . There were so many questions ! Should I have my child in hospital , where I have access to better equipment and specialists but also a higher risk of iatrogenic infections , or do I have my child at home , where there 's less risk of infection but a higher chance I wo n't get immediate help if something goes wrong ? I compromised on a midwife - attended homebirth . Do I get the neonatal heel prick to test for Phenylketonuria , even though it means my child 's first experience after birth is one of pain ( and I 'd read that newborn nervous systems are super sensitive ) ? Yes . Welcome to the world , little girl , this wo n't be the most painful thing you experience in your life ! Do I have my son circumcised ? No , the benefits do n't seem compelling enough . And what about vaccination ? I have to admit I waffled at first . Some of the anti - vaxxers stories made me cry . I talked to my doctor and he said it was my decision . I went to a Public Health info session , and I listened , and then I looked around the room . There were a LOT of parents in there , swearing off vaccinating their kids . And I thought , " Wow , there goes any hope of herd immunity . I 'd better get my kids vaccinated . " Now , of course , I 'm a huge proponent of vaccination . I 've even started insisting the family get their flu shot every year , even though the last time I got it done it messed up my shoulder . It 's worth it , to protect my mother - in - law ( who 's had cancer ) . But I was n't so sure when I was 24 , committed to protecting my unborn child , and questioning everything anyone told me .

Roosters are male chickens, and they have a natural tendency to crow or make a loud noise, called "cock-a-doodle-doo," at dawn. This behavior is controlled by a biological clock called the circadian rhythm, which helps regulate their sleep patterns and other daily activities. 
Crowing is a way for roosters to communicate with other chickens and to establish their territory. It is also a way for them to attract females, or hens, to mate with. When a rooster crows, it is telling other roosters to stay away from its territory and letting the hens know that it is ready to mate. 
So, roosters crow in the morning because it is a way for them to communicate and establish their territory, and this behavior is controlled by their internal biological clock.

Before space travel, map makers didn't have the ability to see the Earth from a bird's eye perspective like we do today with satellites. But even without this kind of view, they were still able to create very accurate maps of the continents using the methods I described above.

Bison are a type of large, hoofed animal that are native to North America. They are related to cows, but they are generally bigger and can produce more meat per animal. Bison are also known for being hardy animals that can survive in a variety of climates and environments. 
However, there are a few reasons why bison are not raised as widely as cows for meat production. One reason is that bison are generally slower to mature than cows, which means that it takes longer for them to reach slaughter weight. This means that it takes longer for farmers to raise bison for meat compared to cows. 
Another reason is that bison can be more challenging to raise than cows. They are generally more independent and can be more difficult to handle, which can make it harder for farmers to manage them. In addition, bison require more space to roam and graze, which can be a challenge for farmers who have limited land available. 
Finally, bison can also consume more feed than cows, which can be more expensive for farmers. Overall, while bison have some advantages as a source of meat, they are generally not as practical to raise as cows for a variety of reasons.

Hey there ! Totally not my field , but I minored in physics in my undergrad years ! Think of electricity as water in a hose . In this analogy , the current ( measured in amperes ) would be analogous to current of the water through the pipe- as in , how much water moves through a given section in a period of time . Voltage would be the speed of the water both in the hose and coming out the end of the hose . How does this help us understand transformers ? Let 's say you want to increase the voltage of the electricity . This can be done without having to raise the power supply at all , using what 's called a transformer . Put your thumb over the end of the hose- suddenly the water starts shooting out , and you can feel the increase of pressure under your thumb . The amount of water passing through any given section of the hose at any point in time has not changed at all , but by shrinking the opening with your thumb , in order to keep the current constant , the water has to speed up coming out the end , which lets you [ spray your backyard enemies ] ( URL_1 ) with a more effective water laser . How does a transformer actually do this , since there 's no real " hole " to shrink ? Easy ! Take a piece of metal ( your transformer 's " core " ) and wrap your wires around it like [ this . ] ( URL_0 ) ( Please note that the transformer setup in this diagram would do the opposite , and * decrease * the voltage ) All moving electric charges ( in this case , the electrons moving through the wire wrapped around the left side of the core ) create a magnetic field . As you can see , the left side has fewer turns than the right side . The electrons in the wire on the right are influenced by the magnetic field created by the ones on the left , and they begin moving . This creates an " induced " current in the right side . Energy must be conserved ! Because there are more coils on the right , there are more electrons under the influence than you started with ! So the voltage decreases ( or increases ) based on how many turns of wire are wrapped around each side . Unfortunately , there is one discrepancy with the hose analogy- the current in the wire has to increase if the voltage decreases , ( so that energy is conserved across the transformer ) and vise versa . The core plays a pretty important role , too ! It magnifies the electromagnetic effects because it is full of electrons that mimic the ones in the wire . Does that help ? ( It 's been a looong time since undergrad so if I 've bungled it anyone is welcome to correct me )

Your little car might take be R15 160/180 wide , a BMW Z4 ( for example ) will take R19s 225/255 tyres . * Profile - how ' fat ' is the side - profile ? Most sports cars have low - profile tyres which require different properties than tyres with higher profile ( which are often more economical and a softer ride ! ) . In the UK tyres are rated for fuel efficiency , wet grip and external noise ( [ see here ] ( URL_1 ) ) . Typically cars that score high ( A - rated ) in each of these categories are more expensive . Whether the costs are justifiable is hard to be certain ( as in how much more it costs the manufacturers to make them is not something I know ) but there 's a lot more to tires than just simply all being the same but slightly different sizes .

John F. Kennedy, also known as JFK, was the 35th President of the United States, serving from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. He was a popular and charismatic leader, and is remembered for his energetic and innovative approach to governing. 
One of the things that JFK is most remembered for is his leadership during the Cuban Missile Crisis, a tense 13-day period in which the United States and the Soviet Union came close to nuclear war. JFK was able to negotiate with the Soviet Union and reach a peaceful resolution to the crisis, which helped to prevent a major global conflict. 
JFK was also known for his commitment to civil rights and equality. He signed the Civil Rights Act of 1964, which helped to end segregation and discrimination against African Americans. He also worked to improve relations between the United States and other countries, and was instrumental in establishing the Peace Corps, a program that sends young people to work on development projects in other countries. 
Overall, JFK is remembered as a brave and visionary leader who worked to make the world a better place.

There are a few different ways that music artists can make money these days, even though there is a problem with illegal downloads on the internet. Here are some of the main ways:

2. Streaming: Many people listen to music through streaming services like Spotify, Apple Music, or Pandora. These services pay the artists a small amount of money every time one of their songs is played. 
3. Touring: Many artists make a lot of their money by going on tour and performing in front of live audiences. They sell tickets to their concerts and also make money from merchandise like t-shirts and hats. 
4. Licensing: Artists can also make money by allowing their music to be used in TV shows, movies, commercials, and video games. They get paid a fee for this use of their music. 
5. Sponsorships: Some artists make money by partnering with brands and promoting their products. For example, an artist might make a deal to use a certain brand of headphones in their music video or wear a certain brand of clothes on stage. 
Overall, there are many different ways that artists can make money, but it can be challenging because of the problem with illegal downloads. It's important for artists to find a mix of different sources of income to help support their careers.

Before personal glucometers, diabetics had to visit their healthcare provider to have their blood sugar levels checked. This typically involved taking a small sample of blood from the finger using a needle and sending it to a laboratory to be analyzed. This process was not only inconvenient, but it also required a lot of time and effort, as the individual had to make a trip to the healthcare provider's office or clinic. 
Personal glucometers have made it much easier for diabetics to monitor their blood sugar levels on their own, as they can be used at home or on the go. With a glucometer, a person with diabetes can simply prick their finger, place a drop of blood on a test strip, and get a reading of their blood sugar level within seconds. This allows them to make adjustments to their insulin doses or other treatments as needed to help keep their blood sugar levels within a healthy range. 
Overall, personal glucometers have made it much more convenient for diabetics to manage their condition and have likely had a significant impact on improving the health outcomes of people with diabetes.

If you find an undiscovered island in international waters, it does not automatically become yours. International waters are not owned by any particular country, so the island would not belong to you just because you found it. Instead, the legal status of the island would depend on what country has the strongest claim to it. 
To make a claim to an island, a country must be able to show that it has exercised effective control over the island for a long period of time. This might include things like building settlements, planting flags, or using the island for military purposes. If a country can prove that it has exercised effective control over an island, it may be able to claim ownership of the island under international law. 
In order to determine the legal status of an undiscovered island, it would be necessary to investigate the history of the island and see if any countries have a strong claim to it. If no country has a strong claim to the island, it may be possible for the island to become part of the territory of the country that first discovers it and establishes effective control over it. However, this would have to be determined by international law and would likely involve negotiations with other countries.

Before the Berlin Wall was built , it functioned as simply another part of East Germany , albeit one where the East German government had no authority . In other words people were free to cross from east to west Berlin to meet friends , buy goods and services , and so on . Once the Berlin Wall was built everything changed . In fact one of the main purposes of the Berlin Wall 's creation was to basically besiege west Berlin by preventing supplies getting in . That problem was overcome by the western powers launching a non - stop 24 - hour - a - day airdrop of supplies to west Berliners , continuing for weeks . Once the East German government relented a little and allowed supplies into west Berlin , it then functioned just as any other small and relatively isolated city did .

This is a frequent phenomenon with technology : something is considered very hard or even impossible for a long , long time , and then one day , somebody figures it out . That opens a whole FLOODGATE of discovery leading to discovery , and the technology leaps forward quickly . Eventually , those chains of discovery start to play out , and the rate of advancement slows down , and may even come to a halt , perhaps starting up again years later when somebody discovers something new . We saw the same phenomenon with the building of the first transistor in 1947 . The * theory * of transistors had actually been known since the early 20th century , but nobody had figured out a way to actually build one . That led to an explosion in electronics , and when somebody figured out how to put a shitload of teensy transistors on a silicon wafer , the explosion exploded .

Freshwater fish species such as the Northern Pike can end up in non-connected lakes across huge regions and sometimes even on different continents through a process called "dispersal." Dispersal is when an organism (in this case, a fish) moves from one place to another and establishes a new population there. There are a few different ways that freshwater fish species can disperse to new areas: 
1. Natural dispersal: Some fish are good at swimming and can travel long distances to reach new bodies of water. For example, Northern Pike are strong swimmers and can travel downstream to reach new lakes or rivers. 
2. Human-aided dispersal: Humans can also play a role in the dispersal of fish species. For example, people may introduce a new species of fish into a body of water for recreational fishing, or they may accidentally release a fish into a new area while transporting it for some other reason. 
3. Historical dispersal: In some cases, fish species may have dispersed to new areas in the past, and then become established there. For example, during the last ice age, many parts of the world were covered in ice, and some fish species may have survived in isolated pockets of water. When the ice melted, these fish were able to spread out and establish populations in new areas. 
Overall, the process of dispersal helps to explain how freshwater fish species like the Northern Pike can end up in non-connected lakes across huge regions and even on different continents.

Border control is a way for countries to try to keep track of who is coming in and out of the country and to try to prevent people from entering the country illegally. There are many different ways that countries can enforce border control, and the methods they use will depend on the resources they have available and the specific challenges they face. 
One way that countries can enforce border control is by using physical barriers, such as fences or walls, to block people from crossing into the country illegally. These barriers can be very effective at preventing people from crossing the border, but they can also be expensive to build and maintain. 
Another way that countries can enforce border control is by using technology, such as cameras, drones, and sensors, to monitor the border and detect when people are trying to cross illegally. This can be a more cost-effective way to enforce border control, but it may not be as effective as physical barriers at preventing people from crossing the border. 
Finally, countries can also enforce border control by using trained personnel, such as border patrol agents or immigration officers, to patrol the border and intercept people who are trying to cross illegally. This can be a very effective way to enforce border control, but it can also be expensive and resource-intensive. 
Overall, it is not possible to completely prevent people from crossing the border illegally, but by using a combination of different methods, countries can try to make it more difficult and discourage people from attempting to cross the border illegally.

As for lipids ( fats ) , that 's a broad and nebulously defined group of molecules , but the usual dietary form of fatty acids and triglycerides are not easily vaporized as far as I know . However , ethanol ( and maybe other alcohols ) are easily vaporized , easily absorbed into the blood through the lungs , and can be metabolized by the liver to yield ATP ( alcohol has calories ) . I think that technically qualifies as an affirmative to your question , though I ca n't see any practical application of it .

PTSD, or post-traumatic stress disorder, is a mental health condition that can develop after a person experiences or witnesses a traumatic event. This can include a wide range of events, such as combat, natural disasters, car accidents, sexual assault, or other violent incidents. 
Soldiers who do not participate in combat can still be exposed to traumatic events while serving in the military. For example, they might witness other soldiers being injured or killed, or they might be involved in dangerous situations that could threaten their own lives. They might also experience other types of stress, such as being away from home, being in a high-pressure environment, or seeing or hearing about disturbing events. 
All of these experiences can be traumatic, and they can lead to the development of PTSD in some people. It's important to remember that everyone responds differently to traumatic events, and not everyone who experiences trauma will develop PTSD. However, if someone is experiencing symptoms of PTSD, it's important to seek help from a mental health professional to get the support and treatment they need.

So while it is possible for an organism to be made up of gas, it is not able to sustain life because it lacks the necessary organization and structure.

Insider trading is illegal because it involves buying or selling a company's stock based on information that is not publicly available. This is unfair to other investors who don't have access to this information and can't make informed decisions about whether to buy or sell the stock. 
Imagine that you and your friend both want to buy a toy at the store, but your friend knows that the store is about to have a big sale and the toy will be much cheaper tomorrow. If your friend buys the toy today and sells it to you tomorrow at the sale price, they will make a profit while you will have paid full price. This isn't fair, because your friend had access to information that you didn't have, and they used that information to their advantage. 
In the stock market, insider trading is illegal because it's similar to this situation. When someone has inside information about a company and they buy or sell the company's stock based on that information, they have an unfair advantage over other investors. This can harm the integrity of the stock market and undermine public trust in it. That's why insider trading is illegal.

Standoffs are used to physically separate the motherboard from the case of a computer. This separation is important for a number of reasons. 
First, the motherboard and the case are made of different materials, and they can have different electrical charges. If the motherboard is in contact with the case, these charges can flow between the two, causing a short circuit. Standoffs help to prevent this by providing a physical barrier between the motherboard and the case. 
Second, standoffs help to support the motherboard and keep it level within the case. This can help to prevent the motherboard from bending or warping due to the weight of the components that are attached to it. 
Finally, standoffs also help to provide airflow within the case by creating space between the motherboard and the case. This can help to keep the components on the motherboard cooler, which can help to extend their lifespan. 
In short, standoffs are an important component of a computer case because they help to protect the motherboard from damage, support it, and improve airflow.

Self-esteem is a person's overall sense of self-worth or how they value themselves. It is not solely based on chemical processes in the body, but rather is influenced by a variety of factors including life experiences, thoughts, and beliefs, and social and cultural influences. 
It is normal for self-esteem to fluctuate and change over time, and it can be affected by both positive and negative experiences. For example, a person who receives positive feedback and recognition for their accomplishments may have a higher sense of self-esteem, while a person who experiences failure or criticism may have a lower sense of self-esteem. 
While chemical processes in the body, such as the release of certain hormones or neurotransmitters, can influence a person's mood and emotions, they do not directly determine self-esteem. However, mental health conditions such as depression and anxiety can affect self-esteem, and these conditions may be influenced by chemical imbalances in the body. 
It is important to remember that self-esteem is not fixed and can be improved with effort and self-care. It is important to focus on building self-esteem by setting and achieving goals, practicing self-compassion and self-acceptance, and surrounding oneself with supportive and positive people.

Prions are tiny particles that can cause serious and often fatal diseases in people and animals. They are made up of proteins, which are important building blocks in the body. Normally, proteins have a specific shape that helps them do their job. But prions are abnormal proteins that have a different shape, and they can cause other proteins around them to change shape too. When this happens, it can cause problems in the body because the proteins are not able to work properly. 
Prions are not viruses, and they do not contain any genetic material (like DNA or RNA) like viruses do. Instead, they are thought to be able to change the shape of proteins by acting as a kind of template for other proteins to copy. 
Prion diseases can be very serious and are often fatal. Some examples of prion diseases include Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (CJD) in humans, and bovine spongiform encephalopathy (BSE, also known as "mad cow disease") in cows. These diseases can be transmitted through contaminated food or medical equipment, but they can also occur spontaneously in some people. 
There is currently no cure for prion diseases, and they can be difficult to diagnose. Researchers are working to better understand how prions cause disease and to find ways to prevent and treat these conditions.

There are two basic ways a sail can drive a boat onward . 1 . If the wind is coming from behind , or at not too big an angle behind , the boat , then the sail is trimmed ( basically , aimed ) so that the wind fills the sail from behind . The wind pushes the boat along , as you 'd expect . If the boat were to reach the speed of the wind , any further increase in the boat 's speed would cause the wind to start pushing it * back * . So , in that configuration , the boat ca n't go faster than the wind . 2 . Once the wind is more than about 45 degrees off from the stern ( rear ) of the boat , though , the sail is trimmed such that the * front edge * of the sail is pointing into the wind . The wind is now flowing * along * the sail on both sides of it . As the boat turns further and further towards the wind , the sail gets pulled in further and further , so that its front edge is aimed into the wind and the wind is flowing along both sides . This works until the boat is heading about 45 degrees upwind - that is , the wind is coming in at a 45 degree angle off the bow ( front ) of the boat . How the sail works in this case , I 'll explain a bit further down . 3 . When the boat is pointing closer than 45 degrees into the wind , that will stop working . You can no longer get the right sail shape , and the wind will just push against the boat and slow it down and eventually stop it . So the people in other comments saying you can sail almost directly upwind are mistaken . You ca n't practically sail very close to directly upwind , you have to be a significant angle off . This is why , when boats are trying to get directly upwind , you 'll see the zigzag , and you 'll notice each zig and zag is approximately a 90 degree turn - because they 're close to 45 degrees off the wind in one direction , and then the other , as they work their way upwind . So , how does the sail work when the boat is pointing somewhere between about 45 degrees upwind and 45 degrees downwind ? It 's all about the shape of the sail . Imagine yourself looking down on the boat from directly above the mast . The wind is blowing the sail into a sort of billow form . If it could , the wind would blow the sail all the way straight in whatever direction the wind is going , like a flag , but it ca n't . A sail is not like a flag in a very important way - the bottom of it is attached to a big straight bar , called the boom . So the sail must stay straight at the bottom , all the way to the rear corner . But , and this is just as important , the sail is n't a plain flat triangle either . It has a bunch of extra fabric ; it 's bigger in the middle than is needed to fill the space between the lines if it were just flat . So there 's extra material to billow out , and the wind , trying to blow the sail off to the side , turns it into a curve . Because of the shape of the sail and how much extra material is in which parts , the sail forms a shape with a sharper ballooning out right at the front , and a shallower curve towards the rear . If you 're looking at this from above , this shape looks very much like what the cross - section of an airplane wing looks like from the side . It works in the same way . I 'm not going to fully ELI5 how that works right here , but the simplest way to imagine it is that the wind creates * suction * on the rear side of the sail . Think of it like this , sort of : Along the inside of the sail 's curve , the side in the direction the wind is coming from , the wind is directly hitting the sail and being curved by it . So here , the wind is partly pushing against the sail . But on the outside of the sail 's curve , the side away from the direction the wind is coming from , the wind going past the mast would just keep on moving past where the sail is , except that direction of motion of the air would pull some air away from the sail . It causes the wind to curve in towards the sail after it has passed it , but it 's not actually blowing against the sail , it 's still blowing kind of away from the sail . It 's pulling air away from the sail . It 's creating a kind of suction , sucking the sail in that direction . It 's pulling the sail towards the direction in which it 's billowing . Now , you might 've noticing , looking down at the boat from above , that this direction the wind is sucking the sail towards is n't actually directly towards the front of the boat . And you 're right - if the boat were flat on the bottom , it would start sliding partly sideways , partly forward . But sailboats have something called a keel ( if it 's permanently fixed ) or centerboard ( if it 's made to slide into and out of the water ) , which sticks out from the bottom of the boat deep into the water . This keel is a thing but wide blade shape that allows the boat to easily slide forward or backward , but presents a lot of resistance against sliding sideways . So all of the sideways component of the force coming from the sail gets wasted pushing water against the side of the keel / centerboard , but the forward component of the force from the sail moves the boat forward . Even though the boat is actually being pushed at an angle sideways - forward , it only moves forward . Now here 's the most important part for answering your question : This motion of the wind along the sail on both sides , creating a force that sucks the sail towards where it 's billowing ... this force is going to keep on being there as long as the wind is flowing along the sail . It does n't matter how fast the boat is moving . This force is stronger when the wind is faster , and this force weakens when the wind is slower , but this force is not affected at all by how fast the boat is moving ! It 's the same amount of suction - force on a boat standing still , or on a boat slowly , or a boat moving fast . If the boat happens to be moving at a speed that happens to be higher than the speed of the wind ? So what ? There 's no relationship , per se , between the speed of the wind and the speed of the boat in that sense . The only relationship is that the speed of the wind determines how much suction - force acceleration is being applied to the boat . In theory , this could eventually accelerate the boat to " infinite " speed ( well , eventually relativity and speed of light limitations start to matter :) , as long as the wind kept flowing along the sides of the sail like that . It does n't , of course , because there 's drag on the boat . That is , resistance to its forward motion , mainly from the water , though somewhat from air too . For any specific amount of forward suction - force , there 's a maximum speed it can get the boat to , where the amount of force from water and air resistance is equal to amount of forward force from the sail 's suction , and the boat stops getting any faster . Obviously , the stronger the wind , the faster the boat can go before reaching that point , because the stronger the wind , the stronger the suction - force from the sail . But the point is , that maximum speed is * not * determined by a comparison of the boat 's speed and the wind 's speed . Rather , it 's determined by the relationship between the amount of suction - force you can get from that particular size & shape of sail at whatever the current speed of the wind is , vs. the amount of resistance on the boat ( determined by its mass and design ) . Of course skill matters too , because the boat wo n't actually get the true maximum force from the sail . How close it is to the maximum possible , depends on how well the sailors trim the sail to just the right point , and keep up with changes in wind direction , with a combination of subtly steering the boat and moving the sail in or out .

Insects have many different ways of surviving the winter, depending on the species and the conditions in their environment. Some insects, like ladybugs and monarch butterflies, migrate to a warmer place for the winter. They fly to a new location where they can find food and shelter until the weather gets warmer again. 
Other insects, like bees and ants, stay in their nests or hives and huddle together to keep warm. They may also store food to eat during the winter when there isn't as much to find outside. 
Some insects, like moths and butterflies, lay their eggs in the fall, and the eggs hatch into larvae or caterpillars in the spring. The larvae then grow into adult insects and emerge when the weather gets warmer. 
Other insects, like ladybugs and praying mantises, go into a state of dormancy during the winter. This means that they stop moving and become very still, almost like they are sleeping. They are able to do this because their bodies are able to slow down their metabolism and use very little energy until the weather gets warm again. 
So, insects have many different ways of surviving the winter, but they all have to find a way to stay warm, find food, and avoid predators until the weather gets warm again.

Lightning is caused by the buildup of static electricity in the clouds. This can occur during a snowstorm, but it is less common because snow is made up of small, lightweight ice crystals that do not produce as much static electricity as other types of precipitation, such as rain or hail. 
When it is cold and dry outside, the air is less able to hold moisture, which can cause snowflakes to form. As these snowflakes fall through the air, they rub against each other and can build up a static charge. However, this charge is not strong enough to produce lightning, which requires a much larger buildup of static electricity. 
In contrast, rain and hail are made up of larger, heavier water droplets that are more effective at producing static electricity. When it is warm and humid outside, the air can hold more moisture, which can cause rain and hail to form. These larger water droplets are more effective at rubbing against each other and building up a static charge, which can lead to lightning. 
So, while it is possible for lightning to occur during a snowstorm, it is less common because snow is not as effective at producing static electricity as other types of precipitation.

TOR, or The Onion Router, is a network that allows users to browse the internet anonymously by routing their traffic through multiple layers of servers, or "nodes," before it reaches its destination. This makes it difficult for anyone to trace the traffic back to the original user.

One way this could happen is if the attacker is able to compromise a node in the TOR network and monitor the traffic passing through it. If the victim is using a vulnerable device or operating system, the attacker may be able to exploit a security flaw to reveal the victim's IP address or other identifying information. 
Another way an attacker could potentially deanonymize a victim using TOR is by setting up a "fake" node on the TOR network and enticing the victim to connect to it. The attacker could then monitor the victim's traffic and potentially reveal their identity. 
It's important to remember that while TOR can help protect your anonymity online, it is not a guarantee of complete anonymity. It's always a good idea to use caution and take steps to secure your device and protect your personal information when using the internet, whether you're using TOR or not.

The "half-life" of a substance is the amount of time it takes for half of the substance to decay, or break down, into something else. For example, if a substance has a half-life of 10 years, then after 10 years, half of the substance will have decayed, and after 20 years, half of the remaining half will have decayed, and so on. 
Measuring the "full" life of a substance would involve waiting until all of the substance has decayed, which could take a very long time. The half-life is a useful measure because it tells us how quickly the substance is decaying, and we can use this information to predict how much of the substance will remain after a certain amount of time. 
It's also worth noting that the half-life of a substance is always the same, no matter how much of the substance you have. This means that if you have a small amount of a substance with a long half-life, it will take a long time for it to decay, but if you have a large amount of the same substance, it will still take the same amount of time for half of it to decay.

Most electronic devices that tell the time, like phones and computers, have a small battery inside them that powers a tiny chip called a "real-time clock" (RTC). The RTC chip is responsible for keeping track of the time even when the device is turned off. 
When the device is turned on, it uses the time stored on the RTC chip to set the time on the device's display. The device may also connect to the internet and check with an online server to get the most accurate time possible. This helps to ensure that the time displayed on the device is accurate, even if the device has been off for a long time. 
So, even when an electronic device is turned off, it still knows the time because it has a special chip inside it that keeps track of the time. When the device is turned on, it uses the time stored on this chip to set the time on the display.

When we cook protein, the heat causes the protein molecules to unravel and rearrange into new shapes. This is called denaturation. Even though the protein has changed shape, it still contains the same amino acids as before and can still be used by the body. 
When you eat a cooked chicken breast, your body breaks down the denatured protein into smaller pieces called amino acids. These amino acids are then absorbed into your bloodstream and taken to cells throughout your body. 
Inside your cells, the amino acids are used to make new proteins that your body needs. For example, they can be used to repair tissues, make enzymes, or form hormones. 
So, even though the protein in a cooked chicken breast has been denatured, your body is still able to use it to make new proteins that are important for your body to function properly.

When you go to a higher altitude, the air pressure around you decreases. The pressure inside your ears is usually the same as the pressure outside your ears, so when you go to a higher altitude, the pressure inside your ears is greater than the pressure outside your ears. This can cause your ears to feel "plugged" or "full." 
To equalize the pressure, your body has a system that allows air to flow in and out of your middle ear, which is the space behind your eardrum. There's a small tube called the Eustachian tube that connects your middle ear to the back of your throat. When you swallow, yawn, or blow your nose, the muscles in the walls of the Eustachian tube open and close, allowing air to flow in and out of the middle ear and equalize the pressure. 
When you're at a higher altitude and the pressure inside your ears is greater than the pressure outside, your body will try to equalize the pressure by opening the Eustachian tube. When the tube opens, you may feel a "popping" sensation in your ears as the air flows in and the pressure equalizes. This is completely normal and nothing to be concerned about. It's just your body's way of adapting to the change in altitude.

There are a few reasons why the audio on two-way radio systems used by emergency services might not always be crystal clear. Here are a few possible explanations: 
1. Interference: Two-way radio systems operate on specific frequencies, but these frequencies can sometimes be affected by other electronic devices or even physical obstacles, such as buildings or mountains. This can cause static or other interference that makes the audio sound fuzzy or unclear. 
2. Distance: The further away a radio user is from the transmitter, the weaker the signal will be. This can lead to poor audio quality, especially in rural or remote areas where there might be less infrastructure to support strong radio signals. 
3. Equipment: The quality of the equipment being used can also affect the audio quality. If the radios or other equipment being used are old or not well-maintained, it can lead to poor audio quality.

A union is essentially a representative that sits between you and your employer when it comes to matters such as hiring ( workplace agreements and wages ) , termination ( fair dismissal , severance ) , strikes and so on . For workers unions are usually a good thing , because the union represents a large number of people in an industry they 're in a good position to negotiate better wages and working conditions than any individual would be able to get . The union will also represent you in workplace disputes , and can sometimes provide legal representation . Unions do have some downsides . For example if there are non - unioned workers in an industry it effectively weakens the union 's position ( why would an employer agree to the union 's demands when they can just hire non - union people instead ) . Therefore in some industries there is a lot of pressure on workers to join unions . If you look around you 'll find stories of strong - arm tactics or even open hostility . Employers also find unions difficult to work with . An employer needs to consider the financial situation of their company when considering things like wages , the union does n't have the same concern and so could make demands greater than the employer can cover , if the employer does n't meet the demands or negotiate them down then they risk their staff going on strike . The pro - union counterargument is that without unions the employers will have minimal pressure to actually raise wages , and that factors such as workplace favouritism could end up with two employees doing the same work for different amounts of pay , therefore it is better to have the employer and the union negotiate the wage for everyone .

Acne is a common skin condition that occurs when the hair follicles on your skin become clogged with oil and dead skin cells. The clogged pores can become infected with bacteria, and this can cause a pimple or zit to form. Pimples are also called zits, spots, or blemishes.

Hormonal changes: Pimples are common during puberty when the body's hormones are changing. Hormonal changes can also cause pimples to form in adults.

Diet: Some people find that certain foods, such as chocolate or fried foods, can trigger pimples.
Skin care products: Some skincare products can irritate the skin and cause pimples to form.
Stress: Stress can affect the body's hormone levels and can cause pimples to form. 
Pimples can be treated with over-the-counter or prescription medications, such as creams or gels that contain benzoyl peroxide or salicylic acid. It is important to keep the skin clean and to avoid picking or squeezing pimples, as this can cause them to become infected or leave scars.

VHS tapes, also known as VHS cassette tapes, are a type of magnetic tape that was commonly used for recording and playing back video and audio in the 20th century. They work by using a magnetic head to read and write magnetic signals on the tape. 
When you insert a VHS tape into a VHS player, the player's drive motor moves the tape past a series of spinning heads. These heads are magnetically charged and can read the magnetic signals on the tape. As the tape moves past the heads, the signals are converted into an electrical current, which is then amplified and played through the speakers of the VHS player. 
The audio on a VHS tape is typically recorded in a format called "analog audio," which means that the audio is stored as a continuous, wave-like signal on the tape. This is different from "digital audio," which stores audio as a series of 1s and 0s. When you play a VHS tape, the audio is read from the tape and converted into sound waves that you can hear through the speakers.

A swing state is a state that does not consistently vote for the same political party in presidential elections. These states are considered important because the outcome of the election can be influenced by the results in these states.

Another reason is that these states have a large number of electoral votes, which are used to determine the winner of the presidential election. The more electoral votes a state has, the more influence it has on the outcome of the election. 
Finally, swing states are often targeted by both political parties because they can be won over with targeted campaign efforts. This means that both parties will spend a lot of time and resources trying to get people in these states to vote for their candidate.

Postmodernism is a broad term that refers to a style or approach to art, culture, and society that emerged in the latter half of the 20th century. It is characterized by a rejection of traditional values and beliefs, and a focus on individual experience and interpretation rather than objective truth. 
Postmodernism often emphasizes the role of power and language in shaping our understanding of the world. It suggests that knowledge is not fixed or objective, but rather is constructed and shaped by the cultural and historical context in which it exists. 
In postmodernism, there is a skepticism towards grand narratives or universal explanations for events and phenomena. Instead, postmodernism suggests that truth and meaning are subjective and open to interpretation. 
Postmodernism has influenced many different fields, including literature, art, philosophy, sociology, and cultural studies. It has also had a significant impact on the way we think about and approach various issues, such as identity, race, gender, and power.

I would say that it is pandemic as opposed to being epidemic , that is , it is n't just happening in NYC or Ferguson or Chicago . While the vast majority of police officers in every department are not guilty of brutality or even of looking the other way , it is , and has been , happening in isolated cases across the country . Further , it also seems to be indicative of police deference and entitlement . Little things like officers not following departmental protocols time and time again after reported incidents of the use of extreme force ( e.g. not getting / recording post - incident interviews , failing to collect evidence ) without action taken by the chief or a political system have led to people simply losing faith in the police . Many feel that the entitlement is so rampant that the officers ca n't be trusted as they once were . It goes deeper than this too . There is a very real and deeply seated distrust between non - whites ( and sympathetic / empathetic whites ) and the justice system . Exacerbated by a failed war on drugs and the still tender wounds of the civil rights movement . ( That 's my explanation of the Police side of the issue but , like everything , there are two sides here ) As mentioned above , ours is a generation born of several decades of political strife , or at least our privileged " first world problems " version of it . From Vietnam and the Civil Rights movement to Rodney King and Katrina , you could say that protest and civil disobedience is in our blood . This has led to a public refusal to capitulate to authority and , for many , the automatic assumption that those with power are disinterested in the common good or even actively opressive . Law enforcement agencies , as a result , feel they are now in an arms race of sorts . Additional firepower and more agressive tactics have been , they feel , necessitated by a progressively more uncooperative ( and often better armed ) public . This in turn reenforces the public 's notions of opression . ( That is my ELI5 of the situation , the rest is my personal opinion on it ) You will hear often ( in fact a couple comments here reference it ) that these incidents are so infrequent that they hardly deserve attention , the current furor being nothing more than the direct result of news media sensationalization . Worse , you will hear people arguing that Police are people too , they make mistakes and we have to be able to forgive and forget . These ideas are ignorant and ineffectual at best and dishonest and dangerous at worst . We hold our police officers up to be the beacons of justice in this country . Undoubtedly it is a high standard perhaps unachievable but law enforcement is the tool through which we shape a better more idealized society . I firmly believe that we are only ever as righteous as the least righteous amongst us . Police departments have a responsibility to weed out the offending officers . Instead they too often close ranks , look the other way or simply ignore the issue . There is an unfair level of distrust from the public but unfortunately that is not going to change over night . We are many and old grudges die hard , even ill - informed ones . If , however , Law Enforcement decides to take it upon themselves to address the public 's concerns we could arrive at a better level of safety and mutual respect within a couple decades at most . Yes it means being the bigger " person " but is n't that exactly what we should expect from our police departments ? TL ; DR Do n't bother getting into the issue if you do n't have time to read eight paragraphs . This is a complicated issue with no room for the lazy and uninformed .

Sailboats can go faster than the wind because they use the wind to propel themselves through the water. The wind pushes against the sails, which causes the boat to move forward. 
Imagine you are holding a piece of paper in your hand and you blow on it. The paper will move in the direction that you are blowing. Now imagine you are holding a cardboard box in your hand and you blow on it. The box will not move as easily as the paper because it is much heavier and more difficult to push. 
Sailboats work in a similar way. The wind pushes against the sails, but the boat is much heavier than the paper, so it doesn't move as easily. However, the shape of the sails is designed to catch the wind and use its energy to propel the boat forward. The keel, which is a long, heavy fin that extends from the bottom of the boat, helps to keep the boat upright and moving in a straight line. 
So, to summarize, sailboats go faster than the wind because they use the wind to propel themselves through the water. The shape of the sails and the weight of the boat work together to harness the wind's energy and turn it into forward motion.

Drug tests work by looking for specific chemicals or markers in a person's body that indicate the presence of a particular drug. There are several different types of drug tests, and each one is designed to detect a different set of drugs. 
One common type of drug test is a urine test, which looks for drugs that have been metabolized (broken down) by the body and passed out of the system through the urine. Urine tests can detect a wide variety of drugs, including marijuana, cocaine, amphetamines, and opiates like heroin. 
Another type of drug test is a saliva test, which looks for drugs that are present in a person's saliva. Saliva tests are often used to detect recent drug use, because drugs can show up in saliva within minutes of being used. 
There are also blood tests and hair tests that can be used to detect drugs. Blood tests are generally more accurate than other types of drug tests, but they are also more invasive and are usually only used in certain circumstances. Hair tests can detect drugs that have been used over a longer period of time, but they are not as sensitive as other types of tests and may not be able to detect very recent drug use. 
Overall, drug tests work by looking for specific chemicals in a person's body that are produced when drugs are metabolized. There are different tests for different drugs, and the type of test used will depend on the specific drug being tested for and the circumstances under which the test is being conducted.

I had a friend who did not believe in evolution , and we discussed this . It comes down to this : they ( or at least some people ) believe in " microevolution , " but not " macroevolution . " To them , microevolution means changes within a species , or " kind " . A wolf to a dog , or one type of bird into another , for example . Macroevolution , to them , means one species or " kind " changing into another . Like fish into things that walk on land . The idea of " kinds " comes from the Bible , for example in the story of Noah : > In the selfsame day entered Noah , and Shem , and Ham , and Japheth , the sons of Noah , and Noah 's wife , and the three wives of his sons with them , into the ark ; They , and every beast after his kind , and all the cattle after their kind , and every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind , and every fowl after his kind , every bird of every sort . ( Gen 7:13 - 14 ) This idea does n't necessarily line up with the scientific idea of " species . " For example , in this view , all hoofed animals might belong to the same " kind . " It does allow them to say that maybe Noah only brought a few animals onto the Ark , representing all of the different " kinds , " and then after that , they changed into all the animals we have today ( while still staying within their " kind " ) . This , at least , was the explanation that I got from my friend . What he was really opposed to was the idea that " a lizard could change into a mammal , " or similar .

In the movies you mentioned, the characters are not actually sleeping for long periods of time. Instead, they are in a state of suspended animation, which means that their bodies are in a state of reduced metabolic activity and they do not experience the normal physical effects of time passing. In this state, their bodies are not moving, so they are not at risk of developing bed sores. 
Hibernation is a natural process that some animals go through in order to conserve energy during times when food is scarce. It involves a reduction in body temperature, heart rate, and other metabolic processes. Humans do not have the ability to naturally hibernate, but scientists are studying the possibility of artificially inducing a state of suspended animation in humans for medical purposes, such as during surgery or for long-term space travel. However, this is still an area of research and it is not currently possible for humans to enter a state of hibernation.

All of our senses can decline to some extent as we age. Our senses of hearing, vision, and smell are particularly prone to changes as we get older because they depend on cells in our body that can wear out over time. 
For example, our eyes have tiny light-sensitive cells called rods and cones that help us see. As we get older, these cells can break down, which can make it harder for us to see clearly and distinguish different colors. Similarly, our ears have tiny hair cells that help us hear. These cells can also break down as we age, which can make it harder for us to hear high-pitched sounds or hear in noisy environments. 
Our sense of smell is also connected to cells in our body, specifically cells in our nose that help us detect different odors. These cells can also break down as we age, which can make it harder for us to smell certain things as well as we used to. 
Our sense of touch is less likely to decline as we age, although it can be affected by certain medical conditions or injuries. 
Our sense of taste can also change as we get older. Some people may find that they are more sensitive to certain tastes or that certain foods don't taste as good as they used to. This can be due to changes in the cells in our mouth that help us taste, or it can be due to changes in our sense of smell, which can also affect how things taste. 
Overall, it's normal for our senses to change as we age, and there are things we can do to help preserve them, such as protecting our eyes and ears from damage and taking care of our overall health.

Piracy is n't ok , but it is a consequence of marketing and pricing . There is no justifying it , but there is some explanation of it . With saturation advertising , there 's literally almost no direction I can look , right now , where I 'm sitting , and not see an advertisement for digital media . The whole point is the advertisers want you to feel that you want this product and you want it * now * , in order to induce a purchase . And they 're successful , at large , too successful , actually . The value of media is inflated , music and video is n't actually worth what they 're charging , as evidenced by the rampant piracy . The working class has n't had a pay raise in 22 years and has less buying power now than they did in the 1980s , cost of living increases , and the price of media has always been ~$15 for an album . And you get less today than ever . You used to get a cover , an insert , and art , bundled with the album . Today , you get an mp3 download . It costs fractions of a penny to digitize the final product and distribute it on the internet , you 'd think that would drop the price . It costs $ 0.017 to produce a CD , just as a point of reference , and digital distribution is orders of magnitude cheaper . So here you have a market who wants your product and wants it now , and ca n't or wo n't afford it . I 'm not saying it 's ok , but this is the catalyst that allows people to justify it . The way the industry works is an artist is contracted to produce content for the publisher , they do n't own their own songs , and they 're literally lucky if they get any money , at all , from the sale of their media . Artists make money by performing , and even then , they 're responsible for the cost of the production , which cuts into their profits . So publishers are seen as big , bad , rich , faceless corporations - profiteering gluttons who do n't need any more money for something they own but they did n't make , so fuck em ' , right ? The world is such a big place and so many people do buy the album that they wo n't even notice I stole a copy ... Or so the mindset goes . There are scenarios where people do pay , when they feel the price is fair and when they feel the profits are distributed to those whom they see entitled .

The terms "first degree" and "third degree" are used to describe the severity of different types of injuries. They are not used to describe the severity of crimes. 
In the context of burns, "first degree burns" are the least severe. They usually affect only the outer layer of skin and cause redness, pain, and swelling. "Second degree burns" are more severe and affect the outer layer of skin and the layer underneath. They can cause blistering and are more painful than first degree burns. "Third degree burns" are the most severe. They damage all layers of the skin and can even damage the tissues underneath. Third degree burns can be very serious and require medical attention. 
In the context of murder, "first degree murder" is considered the most serious because it involves premeditation and planning. It is the intentional and premeditated killing of another person. "Second degree murder" is also a serious crime, but it does not involve the same level of planning and intent as first degree murder. It is the intentional killing of another person, but without premeditation. "Third degree murder" is generally considered the least serious of the three degrees of murder. It is the unintentional killing of another person while committing a non-violent crime. 
So, the terms "first degree" and "third degree" are used to describe different levels of severity, but they are used in opposite ways depending on the context.

No-knock warrants are warrants that allow law enforcement officers to enter a property without first announcing their presence or purpose. In the United States, the Fourth Amendment to the Constitution protects citizens from unreasonable searches and seizures by the government. This means that law enforcement officers generally need a warrant to search a person's home or property, and they must follow certain procedures when doing so. 
One of these procedures is the "knock and announce" rule, which requires officers to announce their presence and purpose before entering a property. However, in some cases, officers may be able to get a no-knock warrant, which allows them to enter a property without first announcing their presence. 
No-knock warrants are generally only granted in cases where there is a risk of danger to the officers or the public, or a risk that evidence might be destroyed if the officers announce their presence. In order to get a no-knock warrant, law enforcement officers must provide a judge with evidence that there is a good reason to bypass the knock and announce rule. 
The Supreme Court has ruled that no-knock warrants are constitutional as long as they are used in a reasonable manner. This means that officers must have a good reason for using a no-knock warrant, and they must follow all other rules and procedures when executing the warrant. However, there have been cases where no-knock warrants have been used improperly, leading to controversy and calls for reform.

It has to do with one - to - one correspondences . So for example , with every natural number we can relate exactly one even number , therefore we say the set of naturals and the set of even numbers have the same " cardinality " . This is true even thought the set of even numbers is a " proper subset " of the natural numbers . It can be shown , however , that there exists * no * one - to - one correspondence between the naturals and the real numbers , therefore their cardinalities differ . And also with every natural number you can relate * at least * one real number ( namely the number itself , e.g. 0 with 0 , 1 with 1 , etc . ) , so we say that the set of real numbers has greater cardinality than the set of natural numbers . What it is n't true is that you can relate every real number with a natural number . To get a better understanding I suggest you learn what an " injection " , a " surjection " , and a " bijection " is . ( A bijection is a function which is in a one - to - one correspondence . And also you can show a function is a bijection if and only if it is a surjection and an injection . )

Thunder and lightning are caused by the movement of electrical charges within a cloud or between a cloud and the ground. Snow is made up of frozen water vapor, while rain is made up of liquid water. During a snowstorm, the air is typically too cold for the snow to melt and become rain, so there is no movement of electrical charges and therefore no thunder or lightning. 
To understand this better, think about the difference between ice and water. Ice is a solid, while water is a liquid. If you put ice in a warm room, it will eventually melt and become water. Similarly, if the air is warm enough, snow will melt and become rain. But if the air is cold, the snow will stay frozen and there won't be any thunder or lightning.

Inside your phone, there is a component called a battery that stores electrical energy and provides power to the phone when it is turned on. The battery is made up of a group of small cells that are connected together and contain chemical substances that can produce electricity. 
Inside the battery, there are positive and negative electrodes, and a chemical called an electrolyte. When the phone is turned on, the battery produces an electrical current by moving electrons from the negative electrode to the positive electrode through the electrolyte. 
As the battery is used, the chemical reactions inside the battery start to run down and produce less and less electrical current. When the battery is almost empty, it will produce very little electrical current, and your phone will eventually turn off. 
To measure how much charge is left in the battery, your phone has a small chip called a "battery management system" (BMS) that keeps track of the amount of electrical current flowing in and out of the battery. The BMS sends this information to the phone's operating system, which then displays the remaining battery charge as a percentage on the screen. 
So, in short, your phone knows how much charge is left in the battery by measuring the electrical current flowing in and out of the battery and using a special chip to keep track of this information.

The United States is considered the wealthiest country in the world because it has the largest economy, measured by its Gross Domestic Product (GDP). GDP is the total value of all the goods and services produced in a country in a given year. The US has a large and diverse economy, with many different industries and businesses that contribute to its GDP. 
However, the US also has a large national debt, which is the total amount of money that the federal government owes to creditors. The national debt is different from GDP because it measures the total amount of money that the government owes, rather than the total value of goods and services produced in the country. 
The national debt can be thought of as a kind of "borrowing" by the government. Just like an individual might borrow money from a bank to buy a house or a car, the government might borrow money to pay for things like infrastructure projects, military spending, and social programs. 
It's important to note that having a large national debt does not necessarily mean that a country is not wealthy. In fact, many wealthy countries have large national debts. However, it is important for the government to manage its debt responsibly and to have a plan in place to pay it back over time.

A strawman theory or strawman argument is a type of misrepresentation of an opposing position. It involves presenting a distorted or exaggerated version of someone's argument in order to make it easier to attack or refute. This can be done intentionally, in an attempt to deceive or mislead, or it can be done unintentionally, due to a lack of understanding or knowledge about the opposing position. 
For example, imagine that there is a debate about whether or not to build a new highway through a particular region. One side of the argument might be in favor of building the highway, while the other side is opposed. If the side in favor of the highway presents a strawman argument, they might exaggerate or distort the arguments of the opposing side, making it seem like they are opposed to all forms of transportation or development, when in reality their objections may be more nuanced or limited in scope. This allows the side in favor of the highway to more easily attack or refute the opposing position, even though they have not accurately represented it. 
It's important to be aware of strawman arguments and to try to avoid using them in discussions or debates. It's better to accurately represent the opposing position and engage with it on its own terms, rather than trying to deceive or mislead others by distorting it.

Food cost in a restaurant refers to the amount of money that a restaurant spends to purchase the ingredients needed to prepare the dishes on its menu. This cost can fluctuate for a variety of reasons. 
One reason is the changing purchase prices of ingredients. Just like the prices of other goods and services, the prices of ingredients can go up or down depending on various factors such as supply and demand, transportation costs, and other market forces. When the price of an ingredient goes up, it can increase the overall cost of the dish, which can affect the restaurant's profitability. 
Another reason that food cost can fluctuate is due to waste or spoilage. If a restaurant orders too much of an ingredient and it goes bad before it can be used, that can add to the restaurant's food cost. Similarly, if an ingredient is prepared or served in a way that results in a lot of waste, that can also increase the food cost. 
Finally, food cost can be affected by the efficiency of the kitchen and the service staff. If the kitchen is able to prepare dishes quickly and efficiently, it can help to reduce food costs by cutting down on the amount of ingredients that are used or spoiled. Similarly, if the service staff is able to correctly predict the number of customers that will be coming in and order the appropriate amount of ingredients, it can help to reduce waste and keep food costs under control. 
In summary, food cost in a restaurant can fluctuate due to changes in the prices of ingredients, waste or spoilage, and the efficiency of the kitchen and service staff. It is important for restaurants to try to keep their food costs as low as possible in order to remain profitable.

There different kinds of taxes and it is very difficult to find a country without a single one of the major categories . The most common types of taxes are corporate * ( paid by large companies ) * , income * ( paid by people earning salaries depending on the specifications in every country ) * and sales * ( also called VAT , charged by the govt . on most products that people buy ) * tax . although there are other types of taxes like import duty , the three I 've listed are the ones that most people talk about when they refer to a no - tax state . There are currently [ 7 countries without income tax ] ( URL_0 ) and [ nine without corporate tax ] ( URL_1 ) . Each of these countries benefit fro their policies in some way . Most of the countries without income tax are oil - rich gulf states . Qatar is an excellent example with no income and sales taxes and not that much corporate tax . But it is also the richest country in the world per capita ( this means that if every govt . in the world divided its money equally to all of its citizens , the people from Qatar would be the wealthiest ) . Put simply , the government generates so much revenue through oil exports that it does not need to tax its citizens . This is the same case for most other oil - rich gulf states . Another group of countries with no - tax policies are tiny island nations like the Bahamas . for these countries , the populations are small , easing the government 's expenditure and they rely a lot on foreign investment , making it necessary for them to lure investors with favorable tax policies . To place this into context , the Bahamas actually sells citizenship for about $ 200,000 . To attract foreigners , it removes taxes so that they can run their businesses in the country with a lot of ease . By attracting these foreign investors , the govt . generates employment for the citizens and improves the socio - economic conditions in the country . Apart from the oil - rich countries and those trying to attract foreign investors , you also have Brunei , which is a very unique case as it tries to keep its citizens as happy as possible . The country actually calculates success through Gross Happiness rather than the usual GDP . AS such , the country keeps taxes at a bare minimum to increase happiness . In short , that is how a no tax policy operates in most countries that people usually refer to . However , all countries collect some form of taxes from different places .
