Well , if we 're going to leave behind Hollywood in our explanation ( Terminator just is n't going to happen , sorry . AIs will never be in control of large scale systems without a human in the loop ) , the real danger is that we get to the point where the evolution of artificial intelligence is faster than the evolution of our meaty knowledge . We can lose control of the ends to which these tools are being made . Think of it this way : before there was speech , all we could do to learn was to watch others . Before there was writing , humans essentially had to re - learn everything by ( at best ) being taught by someone else . Before there was computers , we were limited to the speed of data lookup ( how fast you could retrieve the information ) . Before there was the Internet , we were limited by data volume ( how much data your library or computer stored ) . As we made incremental progress , we accelerated our pace of learning , until now we use computers and AI as a sort of " almost thinking " tool : we train computers to infer what we will be interested in seeing . In fact , we use computers to design new processors , so you can sort of see how our tools are becoming necessary to make more of their kind . No one person knows where all the transistors are on a CPU anymore , rather we 're using software to ensure that the thing that runs the next generation of software will do a good job . At some point , it 's not inconceivable that this role of computers can pass a point known as " the singularity " : imagine someone is designing an AI , but the design of the algorithm gets too complicated to understand . If we get to the point that advanced algorithms are being used to create AI that designs algorithms , and on down the chain , it 's possible that our AI development will proceed _ without us _ . Now , we still have control of this process , but there are some dangers : what if the AIs we develop get too complicated for us to understand ? Then they become , best case scenario , inaccessible or worthless to us . This is likely to happen very soon , actually : multi - layer perceptrons ( [ neural networks ] ( URL_0 ) ) are _ specifically designed _ so that they are not understandable by humans in the goal of being more accurate at predicting the desired output . They are part of a class of supervised learning algorithms termed " black box " : you train them up , give them an input , and just have to trust the output , because you ca n't really understand what 's happening in the middle . In the worst case scenario , poorly designed algorithms find ways to prevent us from exerting control . While we will still likely be able to starve them for some time ( cut the cord ) , the deeper we get with AI algorithms , the more powerful they get - but the harder they get to understand . Perhaps , if the figure of merit in their design is " do stuff as efficiently as possible " , human - in - the - loop interactions can be so inefficient as to carry less weight than the algorithm itself does , which would cut us out of the decision making . Of course , well designed algorithms would optimize more for " do stuff as efficiently as possible , with human tuning , and the ability to quit if something goes wrong " . Since humans decide what the " goal " of a well - constructed AI is ( and will for some time to come ) , computers lack their own volition . They are n't going to " decide " that they are smarter than us . This means we still understand at least what the AIs we develop are attempting to optimize for . When we let them optimize themselves , it 's not inconceivable , however unlikely , that they choose to optimize for something we do n't think is important . This is n't really going to lead to war against the machines , but rather some economic disaster : when we rely on an AI for decision making ( like is very common in today 's stock market ) , one slip of the algorithm can [ cause a lot of economic havok ] ( URL_1 ) . tl;dr : the more layers of AI we use to design and build AI , the harder it is to ensure that the result is exactly what we were asking for .

Water can conduct electricity because it contains ions, which are atoms that have gained or lost electrons and have a positive or negative charge. When water contains a lot of dissolved minerals, it can conduct electricity because the ions in the minerals allow electricity to flow through the water. 
However, pure water does not conduct electricity very well because it does not have many ions. This is because the hydrogen atoms in water are bonded to the oxygen atoms through covalent bonds, which do not allow electrons to flow freely. As a result, pure water is a very poor conductor of electricity. 
So, when you touched a slightly damp iPad charger, the water on your skin may have conducted electricity, which is why you got a bit of a jolt. But if the water on your skin was pure, you probably would not have felt anything.

When a person sells a house and the mortgage is not fully paid off, the process is called a "short sale." In a short sale, the lender agrees to allow the homeowner to sell the house for less than the amount that is still owed on the mortgage. 
Here's how it works: 
1. The homeowner contacts their lender and explains that they need to sell their house but owe more on the mortgage than the house is worth. 
2. The lender reviews the homeowner's financial situation and determines whether they are eligible for a short sale. The lender will look at factors such as the homeowner's income, the amount of equity in the house, and the homeowner's ability to make the mortgage payments. 
3. If the lender agrees to the short sale, the homeowner can then try to sell the house. The lender will review any offers that the homeowner receives and decide whether to accept or reject them. 
4. If the lender accepts an offer, the buyer will need to pay the amount agreed upon in the offer to the lender. The lender will then use this money to pay off the remaining balance on the mortgage. 
5. Once the mortgage has been paid off, the lender will release the lien on the house and transfer the title to the new owner. 
In a short sale, the homeowner is responsible for paying any difference between the amount they owe on the mortgage and the amount that the house sells for. This is called a "deficiency balance." The lender may ask the homeowner to pay the deficiency balance, or they may agree to forgive it.

The news article you posted does n't do a great deal of explaining , but the [ published paper ] ( URL_0 ;) is quite useful . Particularly , the attack summarised through the following steps : 1 ) Client downloads a file from the server that colludes with the adversary . 2 ) While the download progresses , the server injects a repeating traffic pattern in the TCP connection it sees originating from the exit node . 3 ) After sometime the download process is stopped and the server halts the traffic shaping procedure . 4 ) The adversary obtains network statistics from flow records corresponding to the server to exit traffic and for the various clients that used the victim entry node for the duration of the attack experiment . 5 ) If the flows are not correctly aligned , equally long and evenly separated , then the adversary applies the approximation strategy to correctly align them . 6 ) The adversary thereafter computes correlation coefficient for the server to exit traffic network statistics and for all the individual clients that used the victim entry node during the attack experiment . 7 ) The victim client ’s statistics is expected to be most correlated to the server to exit node traffic . Thus the client , whose traffic statistics are most correlated to the exit node traffic are chosen as the victim and verified . Basically , if the NSA controlled a service you were trying to access via Tor , they could send specially shaped packets to act as a sort of label . Even though the data is encrypted and anonymised through Tor ( between the entry and exit nodes ) , if the NSA controlled a lot of routers or major Internet exchange points , they could still track the special packets from the server to the first exit - node , through the middlemen , and finally ( and imperatively ) back through the original entry node & back to your original connection . But that 's not nearly ELI5 enough so I 'll do a * really simplified * analogy which gets the idea across : * * TL;DR * * - say Tor was n't actually an Internet thing and instead was a way of anonymously sending physical mail . If the sender of the parcel was malicious ( eg , an NSA controlled honeypot site ) they could send out really heavy parcels instead of normal , small , letters . So even if the parcel gets repackaged in different boxes or wrapping paper with new labels on ( the middlemen trying to anonymise the post ) , at the end of the day , the client is still going to be receiving a really heavy ass parcel . Even though it may look different , the weight of the packet indicates it 's the same packet which the NSA sent out . Which , in and of itself is pretty useless . But if the NSA controlled most of the postmen , they can just ask them if anyone got a really heavy parcel delivered that day . If they 're lucky , one of the postmen they control remembers delivering a super heavy parcel to 23 Jump Road , Michigan , USA . And then you 're fucked . But critically this relies on two things : having control of the end server , and being able to monitor major Internet exchange points in near real - time , and we 're talking hundreds if not thousands of gigabytes per second here . Theoretically yes Tor is vulnerable but this paper does n't prove it can actually be exploited in any meaningful manner to intercept communications between two end users that want to remain anonymous .

CPUs and GPUs are designed to perform different types of tasks, so they are optimized for different things. CPUs (central processing units) are generally used for tasks that require a lot of sequential processing, such as running software programs and handling input/output operations. They are able to perform a wide range of tasks, but they are not as efficient at tasks that require parallel processing, such as rendering graphics or performing calculations for scientific simulations. 
GPUs (graphics processing units) are specialized processors that are designed specifically for tasks that require parallel processing, such as rendering graphics and performing calculations for scientific simulations. Because they are optimized for these types of tasks, they are able to perform them much faster than CPUs. However, they are not as good at tasks that require sequential processing, such as running software programs and handling input/output operations. 
So, even though the clock speed of a GPU might be lower than that of a CPU, it is still able to perform certain tasks much faster because it is designed specifically for those tasks. Hope this helps!

The Hubble Space Telescope takes pictures of the Milky Way by pointing its camera at different parts of the galaxy and taking long exposures, which means it takes a picture for a longer amount of time. This helps the camera capture a lot of light from the stars and other objects in the galaxy, which makes the picture very detailed and clear. 
The Hubble Space Telescope is able to take pictures of the Milky Way from its orbit around Earth because it is far away from the Earth's atmosphere. The Earth's atmosphere can make it difficult to see objects in space clearly, because it can cause the light from those objects to become distorted. But the Hubble Space Telescope is above the Earth's atmosphere, so it can see objects in space much more clearly. This is why it is able to take such detailed pictures of the Milky Way and other objects in space.

Inflation is the rate at which the general price level of goods and services is rising, and subsequently, purchasing power is falling. Central banks attempt to limit inflation and avoid deflation in order to keep the economy running smoothly. 
A little bit of inflation can be a good thing because it can encourage people to spend money rather than saving it, which can help keep the economy going. When prices are stable, people may be more likely to save their money, which can lead to less spending and a weaker economy. 
On the other hand, if there is too much inflation, it can be harmful because it can lead to instability and uncertainty. If people expect prices to keep rising, they may be less likely to make long-term plans, which can hurt the economy. 
Therefore, central banks try to strike a balance by keeping a moderate level of inflation, usually around 2%. This allows for some price flexibility and can help encourage spending, while still keeping prices stable enough to support long-term planning.

It's generally better for your teeth to have sugar in small amounts rather than all at once. That's because sugar can cause tooth decay, which is a process that happens when bacteria in your mouth use sugar from the foods you eat to make acids. These acids can attack your tooth enamel, which is the hard outer layer of your teeth. If the enamel gets weak or wears away, it can lead to cavities. 
When you eat or drink something with sugar in it, the bacteria in your mouth start making acid right away. The more sugar you eat or drink, the more acid they can make. So if you eat or drink a lot of sugar all at once, your teeth will be exposed to a lot of acid at once, which can be harmful. 
On the other hand, if you have small amounts of sugar throughout the day, your teeth will be exposed to acid for a longer period of time, but the amount of acid they're exposed to at any given moment will be smaller. This can be less harmful to your teeth. 
It's also important to remember to brush your teeth twice a day and floss once a day to help remove sugar and plaque from your teeth. Plaque is a sticky film of bacteria that can build up on your teeth and cause tooth decay. 
So, to sum up: it's better for your teeth to have sugar in small amounts rather than all at once, and it's important to brush and floss your teeth to help protect them from sugar and plaque.

Overall, the cost of a death sentence can be several times more expensive than the cost of keeping someone in prison for a long period of time. Depends on the cameras . In some cases , absolutely . In others , definitely not . Most of the time though , the answer is ' sort of . ' A Hasselblad Stellar is entirely identical to a Sony RX-100 except for the case , but when it came out , sold for about five times the price . In this situation , the markup was entirely based on the cachet of owning a Hasselblad . ( Note : It did n't work . The design studio has been shut down , and the Stellar is selling for about 70 % off now . ) In general , a big part of the cost of a camera is derived from the sensor - specifically , the size of it . The Nikon D7100 is quite similar in design and features to the D600 , but the sensor in the D600 is substantially bigger ( full - frame ) , and the camera sells for about twice the price . Does the sensor cost that much more ? No , not really - but building a camera around it requires more engineering as well . Now if you have a built - in lens , your optics are going to get better as the price increases . That 's a HUGE factor in the price of a camera . Ultimately , cameras follow the same curve as almost every other consumer product : As you move from the low end to the high end , the improvement / price starts out high , then moves to a fairly steady value , then drops off . Say that to make a ( separate ) camera at all , you 'll have to sell it for $ 75 . That 's the base cost of making a body , shutter , electronics , and lens . Double the price , and you get MORE than twice as good of a camera - higher precision , better autofocus , more features , bigger and better lens , bigger sensor , the works . Double it again to $ 300 , and you get about twice as good of a camera as the $ 150 was . Another doubling , and you get about 1.6x as good of a camera . Now we 're getting into the diminishing returns area . The key here is n't whether a $ 1200 camera is twice as good as a $ 600 one , but whether it 's enough of an improvement that you are willing to pay an extra $ 600 for it . Is a $ 2400 camera twice as good as a $ 1200 ? Nope , definitely not - but it 's significantly better in a few ways , and those factors might be enough to get you to shell out the difference . Also , if you have $ 30k wrapped up in lenses , is it really outrageous to buy a camera body for $ 4k that is modestly better than a $ 1k one ? Ultimately , what do you want from your camera , and how much are you willing to spend on it ?

Electricity is the flow of electrons, which are tiny particles that are part of atoms. The speed of electricity is not a constant value and can vary depending on the material that it is traveling through. In general, electricity travels at a speed of about 1/100th the speed of light through most materials. 
The speed of light, on the other hand, is a constant value and is very fast - about 186,282 miles per second (299,792,458 meters per second). This is the fastest speed that anything can travel. 
Cables and other mediums, such as wires and conductors, can affect the speed of electricity. For example, if the material that the electricity is traveling through has a lot of resistance, it can slow down the flow of electricity. On the other hand, if the material has a low resistance, it can allow the electricity to flow more quickly. 
So, to sum it up: the speed of electricity is not as fast as the speed of light, and it can be affected by the materials that it is traveling through.

So in short, our eyes are able to move independently to some extent, but they also have the ability to move together in the same direction. This helps us to see more clearly and judge the distance of objects more accurately.

The first order of business is to seal the chest , to stop air from getting in or out . However , this can also cause a problem called tension pneumothorax . This is when you 're breathing with a hole in the actual lung itself . The air goes in when you breathe in , but escapes into the chest cavity . Pressure builds , and collapses the lung . If too much pressure builds , it puts pressure on the heart . To fix this issue , you need to puncture the chest again , with something small . In the military , we use a 14 - guage needle . Dispatcher here . I dispatch for a sheriffs office in a ski county . Our dispatch center dispatches for the county deputies , the state troopers , the park rangers , the wildlife officers , the US forest service officers , and local fire and EMS . As far dispatching law enforcement goes , each agency has a certain jurisdiction , and if a call happens in their jurisdiction we usually send one ( or multiple ) units depending on the call . For example the state troopers take all of the calls on the state roads and interstates ( accidents , traffic violations , road hazards , suspicious vehicles on the interstate , etc ) . The county deputies take all of the calls on county roads , and county property . These calls may include domestic violence calls at a citizens houses , burglary alarms , citizen complains against each other , welfare checks , and civil problems . They also patrol the area proactively trying to prevent crime . They have a job very similar to a city police officer . The park rangers take care of all of the problems in state park jurisdiction , and the wildlife officers generally ride around on ATVS and look for poachers , and take care of the wildlife people encounter on their property . The US forest officer takes the calls in the national forest in our jurisdiction and patrols the highway the passes through it . He may also receive help from our deputies if need be because we have a deputy whose zone passes through his national forest . We also have a local police department in the area , but their dispatch is in another location and we occasionally coordinate . We can hear each others radio traffic , but do n't dispatch each others officers . Now , there are about 6 troopers ( Highway patrol ) on air in the busiest time , and they are separated into 3 zones . Each trooper is assigned a zone , and if we receive a call in their zone we generally ask them to respond . If all the troopers in their zone are unavailable , we ask the officer in charge to advise . The deputies have 4 zones with 6 deputies on duty . 4 deputies have their own zone ( so one in each zone ) and there is a float who will respond to any call if an officer is busy , and another call comes in in their zone . There is also a SGT on duty who is the boss of the crew . If a call comes in their zone , they are dispatched to it , and if deputies from another zone come to assist , the officer who is assigned to that zone will be in charge of the call ( write the big report , and if needed , take the prisoner into the jail ) . When we take a call our system automatically recognizes the address and maps it for us , and we can see whose zone it is in , and we send that unit . Generally , there is a unit available to assist if they need backup , and all officers in every agency will back each other if they feel they are in a dangerous situation . We have a computer aided dispatch system that tracks all of the calls , and what every unit is doing , so we know if they are available , unavailable , their location and how long they have been on that call so we can security check them if need be . I 'm not going into detail with any other agency besides the highway patrol and Sherrifs deputies because they all pretty much have one zone . I hope this covers at least a small chunk of curiosity . Feel free to ask more questions , because I 'm sure I 've probably made no sense . Oh , and they know the address because it shows up on their mobile dispatch center on their computer in their vehicle , and we always tell them when we dispatch them on the call .

Speakers produce sound by vibrating a flexible membrane called a diaphragm. When an electrical signal is sent to the speaker, it causes the diaphragm to move back and forth rapidly, creating sound waves in the air. 
The shape and movement of the diaphragm determines the type of sound that the speaker produces. For example, if the diaphragm moves in a simple, regular pattern, it will produce a pure tone or sine wave. However, if the diaphragm moves in a more complex pattern, it can produce more complex waveforms, such as those found in music and other sounds. 
To create these complex waveforms, the speaker uses multiple small electrical signals that are combined together. Each signal causes a specific part of the diaphragm to vibrate in a certain way, and when all of these vibrations are combined, they create the overall movement of the diaphragm. 
Think of it like a group of people all clapping together to make a beat. Each person is making a simple clapping motion, but when they all clap together, they create a more complex and varied sound. Similarly, the different signals sent to the speaker's diaphragm work together to create a more complex sound wave.

In general, it is safer to stay away from any electrical sources that are labeled with high voltage warning labels, as these can be very dangerous and can cause serious injury or even death.

In the early days of the internet, when it was still a relatively small and specialized network, each country was assigned a two-letter code called a "top-level domain" (TLD) to identify the country where a website was based. This made it easy for people to know which websites were from which countries. 
For example, the TLD for Italy is ".it", so websites with this TLD are likely to be based in Italy. Similarly, the TLD for Brazil is ".br", so websites with this TLD are likely to be based in Brazil. 
However, as the internet has grown and become more widely used, more and more people and organizations outside of these countries have wanted to use these TLDs for their own websites. To meet this demand, the number of TLDs has been expanded to include many more options beyond just the two-letter codes for countries. 
For example, in addition to the TLDs for countries like ".it" and ".br", there are now also TLDs for specific types of organizations, such as ".edu" for educational institutions, ".gov" for government agencies, and ".com" for commercial businesses. There are also TLDs for specific communities, such as ".org" for non-profit organizations, and ".net" for internet service providers. 
As a result, it is no longer necessary for websites in the United States to have a specific TLD to identify their country of origin. Instead, they can choose from a wide range of TLDs that are more specific to their purpose or community.

Lucid dreaming is a type of dream where you are aware that you are dreaming and can sometimes even control what happens in the dream. Some people believe that it is possible to train yourself to have lucid dreams, and there are a few different techniques that you can try to increase your chances of having one. 
One way to train yourself to have a lucid dream is to keep a dream journal. Every morning after you wake up, write down everything you can remember about your dreams from the night before. This can help you become more aware of your dreams and may increase your chances of having a lucid dream. 
Another technique is to do something called "reality checking." This means that you regularly check during the day to see if you are awake or dreaming. For example, you can try to push your finger through your palm or look at a clock and then look away and look back to see if the time has changed. If you do this enough, you may start doing it in your dreams and realize that you are dreaming. 
There are also some other techniques that you can try, such as setting an intention to have a lucid dream before you go to sleep, or trying to stay as awake as possible just before you fall asleep. 
Overall, whether or not it is possible to train yourself to have lucid dreams is still a topic of debate, and different people may have different experiences. However, if you are interested in trying to have a lucid dream, it can be fun to experiment with different techniques and see what works for you.

Another reason is that smoking is much more prevalent and addictive than drinking. Many people who smoke become addicted to cigarettes, which makes it difficult for them to quit and can lead to serious health problems. The United States national debt is the total amount of money that the federal government owes to its creditors. The debt is currently about $28 trillion and it is possible for it to be repaid, but it would require the government to make regular payments to its creditors to reduce the balance. 
There are a few ways that the government could make these payments: 
1. By running budget surpluses: This means that the government would bring in more money through taxes and other revenues than it spends on things like defense, education, and healthcare. The extra money could be used to pay down the debt. 
2. By selling assets: The government could sell things like land, buildings, or other assets to raise money to pay off the debt. 
3. By growing the economy: If the economy grows, the government will bring in more money through taxes, which could be used to pay down the debt. 
It is important to note that the national debt is not necessarily a bad thing. In fact, it can be a useful tool for the government to fund important projects and stimulate the economy. However, if the debt gets too large, it can become difficult for the government to make the necessary payments, which could lead to financial problems.

The Rosetta mission was a big deal because it was the first time that a spacecraft had landed on a comet. A comet is a small, icy body that orbits the sun. They are made up of dust and ice and are often called "dirty snowballs." 
Comets are interesting to scientists because they are thought to contain clues about the formation and early history of the solar system. The Rosetta spacecraft was launched in 2004 and it spent more than 10 years traveling through space before it reached the comet it was going to land on. 
Landing on a comet is different from landing on a planet like the moon or Mars because comets are much smaller and have a very weak gravity. This makes it difficult for a spacecraft to land on a comet and stay there. The Rosetta mission was able to land on the comet using a special landing module called the Philae lander. The Philae lander was able to anchor itself to the surface of the comet and take measurements and collect samples of the comet's surface. 
Overall, the Rosetta mission was a big deal because it allowed scientists to learn more about comets and the early history of the solar system. It also showed that it is possible to land a spacecraft on a comet, which could open up new possibilities for future space missions.

When you exercise, your body uses stored energy in the form of fat to fuel your muscles. Your body has a limited amount of energy stored in the form of glycogen (a type of carbohydrate) in your muscles and liver, and when that runs out, it turns to fat as an energy source. 
Here's how it works: 
1. When you start exercising, your body breaks down glycogen stored in your muscles and liver into glucose (a type of sugar) to use for energy. 
2. As you continue to exercise, your body uses up more and more glycogen and starts to use fat as an energy source. 
3. When your body uses fat for energy, it breaks down the fat molecules into smaller parts, including glycerol and fatty acids. 
4. The glycerol and fatty acids are then taken to your cells, where they are used to produce energy through a process called cellular respiration. 
5. During cellular respiration, oxygen is used to convert the glycerol and fatty acids into energy, and carbon dioxide (CO2) and water are produced as byproducts. 
6. The CO2 is expelled from your body when you breathe out, and the water is expelled through urine and sweat. 
So, to answer your question, when you exercise, your body burns fat by using it for energy and releasing the byproducts (CO2 and water) through your breath, urine, and sweat.

Storm clouds are gray because they contain a lot of water droplets that are very close together. When light from the sun or other sources passes through these droplets, it scatters in all directions. This is what makes the clouds appear white. 
However, storm clouds are often much denser and contain a lot more water droplets than normal clouds. This means that more light is scattered in all directions, which can make the clouds appear darker. This is why storm clouds are often gray. 
Normal clouds are white because they also contain water droplets, but these droplets are farther apart from each other. This means that less light is scattered in all directions, which makes the clouds appear white. 
Overall, the color of clouds is determined by the amount of water droplets they contain and how these droplets scatter light. Storm clouds appear gray because they contain a lot of water droplets that scatter a lot of light in all directions, while normal clouds appear white because they contain fewer water droplets that scatter less light.

In Morse code, there is a short pause between dots and dashes within a single letter, and a longer pause between letters in a word. The length of the pause between words is even longer. 
For example, if you wanted to spell the word "hello" in Morse code, it would be: 
H - dot dash dot dot
E - dot
L - dash dot dot dot
L - dash dot dot dot
O - dash dash dash 
Each letter is separated by a short pause, and the word is separated by a longer pause. 
So, if you are listening to someone sending Morse code, you can tell when a letter ends and another one begins by listening for the pause between the dots and dashes. And you can tell when a word ends and another one begins by listening for the longer pause between the letters.

No one really knows what causes depression . There 's debate about whether depression is a single disease or several diseases with similar symptoms . Most antidepressants work on the neurotransmitters serotonin , norepinephrine , and/or dopamine . There is also evidence that drugs targeting the NMDA receptor can also be effective . People respond to all sorts of different drugs . Even more confusingly , people who do not respond to one serotonin drug may do well on a different serotonin drug . People do have physical symptoms such as fatigue , weakness , appetite changes , etc . If these symptoms are driven by the brain or if they come from some other mechanism is n't really known either . There are some diseases like Irritable Bowel Syndrome and Fibromyalgia which have a strong statistical relationship with mental health disorders such as depression , but we do n't understand the root cause enough to say for sure how they are related .

EDIT , Nov 21 : By popular demand , now includes Midrange decks and Limited formats . Also , a new section on where players get their cards . Magic : The Gathering is a collectable card game . It can be played in several different formats , but in the most common , two players create decks out of the cards they own and play against each other . Generally , the cards in a deck are either lands or spells . Lands provide the energy needed to cast spells , and spells usually do one of the following : - Summon a creature that can fight for you or an artifact that you can use . ( * * Creatures * * and * * Artifacts * * ) - Create a lasting effect that affects the game as a whole or one or more cards in play . ( * * Enchantments * * ) - Create an instantaneous effect that affects one or more cards in play or an effect that lasts a single turn . ( * * Sorceries * * and * * Instants * * ) - Summon a sort of " teammate " that has its own abilities . ( * * Planeswalkers * * ) The primary goal is to run your opponent out of life points by attacking him with creatures or damaging him directly with spells . There are other ways to win , such as running your opponent out of cards in his deck . The gameplay is complex . The players take turns , but many types of spells can be cast on an opponent 's turn , or in response to a spell that they are trying to cast . A critical play by one player may be countered by the other , and that counter itself countered by the first . There are many thousands of cards out there , and you are not expected to know about them all . The Comprehensive Rules ( * not * including the text of cards ) are nearly two hundred pages , although a lot of that consists of old mechanics that are rarely used or highly - specified details that are rarely relevant . The learning curve is pretty steep , but a few games suffice to learn the basic rules . The complexity is more similar to Dungeons and Dragons than to most card games . A key feature of M : tG is the concept of * * color * * . There are five colors : Red , Green , White , Blue , Black . Each is " allied " to two others and " opposed " to the two others . Most spells require mana of a certain color to cast , which is produced by different types of land cards . Each color has distinct strengths and weaknesses – for example , blue has the best counterspells , but it 's not very good at simply hitting things . A given deck will usually use from one to three colors of card , although in some situations decks with four or five colors can work . In addition , it is often considered that there are three general archetypes of deck ( with a * lot * of variation within each category ) : - * * Aggro * * decks focus on dealing damage ASAP to win ASAP . They usually have cheap creatures ( " weenies " ) that can be used right away and " buff"-type spells to eke out a few more points of damage . They usually win fast or not at all , because they often ca n't stand up to the more powerful creatures and spells that are played later in the game . - * * Control * * decks focus on preventing the opponent from acting until the player is ready to win with powerful spells or abilities . They run a lot of counterspells , destruction spells , and things that make action more expensive for the opponent , along with a few creatures strong on defense and disruption . They usually win by using cheap defenses against expensive attacks until they are ready to overwhelm the opponent with their accumulated strength , and lose if they just ca n't stay ahead of the enemy 's attacks . - * * Combo * * decks rely on a few combinations of cards that can lead to a near - instant victory . There * are * infinite combos in M : tG , but they 're designed to be difficult or resource - intensive to pull off . A combo deck will usually run ( in addition to the all - important combo pieces ) " tutor " cards that let them search their deck , protection cards to guard the combo pieces , and just enough stall cards to prevent a quick defeat . A combo deck wins when it gets its combo in place before losing , and loses if it ca n't get the combo out in time or ca n't protect it long enough to use it . - * * Midrange * * decks are a common intermediate archetype between Aggro and Control . Midrange tries to beat down the opponent with damage from powerful creatures ( colloquially , " fatties " ) rather than with cheap ones ( " weenies " ) . These decks have to run enough control to stay alive until the big hitters come out . They win when they get the hitters out early enough to do lethal damage , and lose when they ca n't get the big guys out at all ( being out - controlled ) or when they just take too much of a beating too early ( being out - aggro'd ) . --- Many different kinds of people play M : tG. There are professional players who win large sums at tournaments , groups of friends who play casually on the weekends , and everything in between . If you 're playing competitively , it can be absurdly expensive , as you can not compromise on getting the perfect cards for your deck , but if you and your opponents are playing casually , it 's a lot cheaper . The most common formats are as follows . Two - player games are the norm for tournaments , but casual matches are often multiplayer . * * Constructed * * formats call for pre - constructed decks built from each player 's personal collection : - * * Standard * * : Decks are made of cards printed in about the last 18 months . - * * Modern * * : Decks are made of cards printed since about 2003 ( minus a ban list ) . - * * Legacy * * : Decks are made of * any * cards ( minus a ban list ) . - * * Vintage * * : Decks are made of * any * cards ( minus a * very small * ban list ) . - * * Commander * * ( or " EDH " ): Decks are made of * any * cards ( minus a ban list ) , but limited to one of each card . One creature card is designated as your " commander " and is nearly always available for summoning . A popular multiplayer format . * * Limited * * formats feature decks made on the spot from a limited pool of cards : - * * Sealed * * : Each player opens several randomized booster packs and makes a deck from the contents . - * * Draft * * : A group of players open many packs and use a system to pick their cards from the contents , then build a deck from what they have . These formats cost money each time to play , because you need to buy booster packs . However , you can play them without having any of your own cards beforehand . Limited play can be a good way to pick up more cards . A relatively new casual format is * * Cube * * , in which you and your group put together a large pile of cards beforehand and use them in place of booster packs . This eliminates the recurring expense , although you need to have enough cards beforehand . There are others formats , including Conspiracy , Planechase , Pauper ( use no rare cards ) . Two - Headed Giant ( play with a partner ) , and many more . Formats that allow older cards tend to be more expensive , because the cards have been out of print for a long time . Commander is a bit cheaper than other formats that allow old cards , because you only can have one of each card ( other formats usually allow up to four of each card ) . I play Commander with my D & D group . None of us puts that much money into it , and we try to keep it more fun than competitive . Some of the players also play in local mini - tournaments on Friday nights ( " Friday Night Magic " ) , usually breaking about even . --- So , where does one get cards ? Most cards come from randomized booster packs with 15 cards each . Cards from a given set are designated either common , uncommon , rare , or " mythic rare " . You can buy booster packs for around $ 3.50 USD from retailers , or you can buy them as part of a limited tournament . There are also pre - made decks that you can buy directly from Wizards of the Coast . Each one has a theme and cards intended to work together . You wo n't win any tournaments with a pre - made deck , but it can be a good starting point . In addition , there are a couple of products intended for beginners , such as the " Deck - builder 's toolkit " with a semi - randomized selection of cards and a big stack of common lands . ( Common lands are dirt cheap , but it would take quite a few booster packs to get enough to build a deck . ) Most M : tG players trade cards with their friends or at events to get the specific cards they need . In addition , there is a thriving market of single cards at game stores and online . Some very , very old cards are very , very rare and expensive . A single [ Black Lotus ] ( URL_0 ) can cost many thousands of dollars . But these cards are not legal in most formats , and most players will never have seen one in person . When playing casually , your group may allow the use of " proxy " cards to substitute for the pricey ones . Myself , I 've only been playing for a year or so , and I do n't actually own my own cards ! ( My friends have a ton of extra decks to lend out . ) I 'm planning to build my own Commander deck soon , and I 'll be starting from a pre - made deck from Wizards and supplementing it primarily with trades with friends and singles from the internet .

It is generally illegal for politicians to accept bribes. A bribe is a payment or gift given to someone in a position of power in exchange for them doing something specific or for them not doing something that they are supposed to do. In many countries, it is a crime for politicians to accept bribes because it undermines the integrity of the political system and can lead to corruption. 
For example, if a politician accepts a bribe from a company in exchange for supporting a particular piece of legislation, they may be breaking the law. This is because they are using their position of power to influence the outcome of a decision in a way that benefits the company that paid them the bribe, rather than acting in the best interests of the people they represent. 
If a politician accepts a bribe and then does nothing in return, they may still be breaking the law because they are accepting something of value in exchange for the promise of doing something. Even if they do not follow through on the promise, the fact that they accepted the bribe can be illegal. 
In short, it is generally illegal for politicians to accept bribes, and doing so can have serious consequences, including fines, imprisonment, and the loss of their job.

Pinyin is actually a standardized system for writing Chinese using the Roman alphabet, and it is widely used in China for teaching Chinese as a second language, transcribing proper nouns and place names, and as a tool for inputting Chinese characters on computers and phones. 
However, Pinyin is not used as the primary written form of Chinese. Chinese characters (also known as Hanzi or Kanji in Japanese) are the primary written form of Chinese and have a long history dating back over 3,000 years. They are still used today because they are an important part of Chinese culture and history, and they are also very efficient for written communication. 
One of the main reasons that Pinyin is not used as the primary written form of Chinese is that it cannot fully represent the sounds and meanings of Chinese words. Chinese characters, on the other hand, can convey the full meaning and pronunciation of a word in a single character. For example, the Chinese character for "water" is 水, which is pronounced "shui" in Mandarin. Pinyin cannot represent the meaning of the character, only the pronunciation. 
Additionally, replacing Chinese characters with Pinyin as the primary written form of Chinese would be a massive undertaking that would involve changing the way that Chinese is taught in schools, updating dictionaries and other reference materials, and potentially requiring people to learn a new writing system. It would also be difficult to determine a standard way to represent the many different regional accents and dialects of Chinese using the Roman alphabet. 
In summary, while Pinyin is a useful tool for learning Chinese and transcribing proper nouns, it is not used as the primary written form of Chinese because it cannot fully represent the sounds and meanings of Chinese words, and replacing Chinese characters with Pinyin would be a major undertaking.

Freckles and other skin imperfections, like moles and scars, can remain because they are caused by changes in the skin cells or the skin's structure. 
When skin cells grow and divide, they sometimes produce too much pigment, which can cause freckles. Freckles are usually small, flat, round spots on the skin that are a little darker in color than the surrounding skin. They are more common in people with lighter skin and tend to appear on areas of the skin that are exposed to the sun, like the face, arms, and legs. 
Moles are also caused by pigment-producing cells in the skin. They are usually small, round or oval-shaped spots on the skin that can be a little darker or lighter than the surrounding skin. Moles can appear anywhere on the body and can be a variety of colors, including pink, red, brown, or black. 
Scars are areas of skin that have been damaged and have healed over. They can be caused by injuries, surgeries, or certain skin conditions. Scars are usually flat and smoother than the surrounding skin and can be a different color or texture than the surrounding skin. 
While the skin is constantly shedding dead cells and regenerating new ones, these skin imperfections can remain because they are caused by changes in the skin cells or the skin's structure, which are not necessarily affected by the normal process of cell turnover.

There are several factors I can think of . Some more prohibiting than others . 1 . Its dangerous . Its just as dangerous as going to space . Think if space was like the ocean , and you could only see a few feet in front of you . We would have virtually no information on our own solar system let alone the stars . 3 . It requires a lot of energy . Unlike a space probe which will continue to move and explore long after its fuel runs out , a deep sea submersible will not . It requires a constant source of propulsion , which means constantly returning to the surface to refuel or recharge . 4 . Its time consuming . We acquire information from space literally at the speed of light . As for the oceans , the exploration happens at the speed of the deep sea vehicle . These vehicles are n't designed to move very fast . Even if their speed was increased , it would n't matter . Its like trying to explore all of Siberia at night with a flashlight and a disposable camera while driving a golf cart . It would take many years , possibly many decades to explore the entire ocean . 5 . Its expensive . Space is much more expensive over a single mission . But compare to the amount of money it would take to launch hundreds of expeditions over many decades , it 's not . With space exploration you get more bang for your buck . A single voyage to the ocean floor yields very little information compared to a single space mission . Its not impossible , but its definitely a challenge for the future . It really is the final frontier for Earthly exploration .

I 'm going to go off the assumption that you do n't know what tor is , if you do then feel free to skip the next paragraph . The entire idea behind tor is that by bouncing your information through different relay points , each of which is secured and the data transfers between them are encrypted , thus making your communications much harder to trace back to you . If I turn on my computer and use a regular browser , lets say Firefox , my computer requests that a specific bit of information from , lets say , reddit . My request is sent from my computer , to my ISP 's servers , to the servers of the website and then takes the little bit of information , lets say a specific webpage , and then does that same process in reverse . Taking the information from the servers of the website to the ISP 's servers and then back to my computer . Tor complicates this process by , rather then doing a straight line of data from computer to ISP to site , first encrypting all the communication and then secondly bouncing that signal / request for data around before submitting the request for information . This has the effect of making you appear like your in another place and posses a different IP address then you actually do . Simply put , a Tor node is one of the small parts of the jump that obfuscates your physical location and IP address by bouncing your information . An exit node is a tor node that serves as the ' exit ' point from the tor network and sends the actual request for information from the site . Hopefully that clears this up , if you have anymore questions just ask . :)

A panic attack is a sudden and intense feeling of fear or anxiety that can be accompanied by physical symptoms such as a racing heart, shortness of breath, dizziness, and chest pain. These symptoms can be very frightening and can make someone feel like they are in danger, even if there is no real danger present. 
If you come home and see your housemate suffering from a panic attack, there are some things you can do to help: 
1. Stay calm and try to stay with them. Panic attacks can be scary, and your housemate may feel very alone and overwhelmed. By staying with them, you can provide support and comfort. 
2. Encourage them to take slow, deep breaths. This can help to calm their body and mind. 
3. Talk to them in a soothing and reassuring voice. Let them know that they are not alone and that you are there to help. 
4. Help them find a comfortable and safe place to sit or lie down. 
5. Offer them a glass of water or a soothing drink. 
6. If the panic attack continues for a long time or if your housemate is in severe distress, consider calling for medical help. 
Remember, panic attacks are not dangerous, and they will usually pass on their own after a few minutes. It's important to reassure your housemate and let them know that they are safe and that the panic attack will eventually end.

Hi ! I 'm from New York . You 've gotten some good answers , but maybe I can help go more in - depth . The very first thing to remember is that in more expensive cities , wages rise with cost inflation . Now , it 's not a perfect scale-- most of the cost inflation comes because many people are * very * rich , not necessarily because the poor are less poor . However , it 's still a factor-- there are fewer minimum wage jobs , and/or you 're more likely to get a job whose wages are above the federal minimum . In other words , chances are the same job netting you $ 7.25 in Nebraska will get you around $ 10 in NYC . Now that 's not 100 % true . There are still * some * minimum wage jobs here . How do people get by ? Well , about like anywhere else , I suppose . They do n't get to enjoy many aspects of the city where inflation has taken hold . They do n't get to go out to dinner at the nice restaurants or go drinking in NYC bars . They ca n't go to Whole Foods , they have to go to cheaper grocery stores . They have to curb themselves more , and * * they get government assistance * * like anywhere else . Remember , the farther you go from NYC the cheaper things get , so many times people will live far away from the city if they do n't make a lot . And if they 're living far away from the city , and working a minimum wage job ... they can just as easily find one near where they live . Hence , like I said , there being less minimum wage jobs in the city . It all ... * kinda * evens out . You 're not likely to live in NYC and have a minimum wage job as an adult . Matter of fact I ca n't think of a single minimum wage job I 've had , and I 've made the rounds as a student . Office worker , busboy , waiter , receptionist ... they all paid around $ 10 . And one last thing to remember : The cost of living does n't * have * to be high in this city . It 's totally doable on less money . The reason it seems higher is that it * can * be higher . Teeth grinding, also known as bruxism, is a common condition that can occur during sleep or while awake. It is when a person clinches, grinds, or gnashes their teeth together. Some people grind their teeth because of stress or anxiety, while others do it because of an abnormal bite or misaligned teeth. It can also be caused by sleep disorders, such as sleep apnea. 
Bruxism can cause tooth damage, such as chips or cracks, and can also cause jaw pain and headaches. If your roommate is grinding their teeth, they may want to see a dentist or doctor to help identify the cause and find a solution. In the meantime, they can try wearing a mouthguard at night to protect their teeth and reduce the grinding noise.

When you pay tuition to go to college, the money is used to cover the cost of providing you with an education. This includes things like hiring professors and other staff, maintaining buildings and classrooms, providing resources like books and computers, and offering extracurricular activities and sports programs. 
Colleges can offer scholarships to students as a way to attract talented students to their school. Scholarships are often funded by donations from individuals, businesses, or foundations that want to support education and help students afford the cost of tuition. Scholarships can also be funded by the college itself, using money from its budget or from tuition paid by other students. 
So, when you receive a scholarship to go to college, you are not taking money away from anyone else. Instead, the scholarship is helping you pay for your own education, and the money for the scholarship may come from a variety of sources.

There are many reasons why pedestrians might be hit by trains. One reason is that some people are not paying attention when they are near train tracks. They might be using their phones, listening to music, or otherwise distracted and not aware of their surroundings. Another reason is that some people may not be familiar with the area and might not realize that there are train tracks nearby. Some people might also intentionally ignore warning signs or try to cross the tracks when they are not supposed to. In some cases, people might simply misjudge the speed or distance of an oncoming train and try to cross the tracks at the wrong time. Finally, it's important to remember that trains are very large and can be very quiet, so it can be difficult for people to hear or see them coming until it's too late.

It's also possible that you have a structural issue with your nasal passages that is causing one side to feel more congested. Yes, we do reside in the Milky Way Galaxy! The Milky Way is a barred spiral galaxy, which means it is a type of galaxy that has a flat, disk-like shape and a central bar-shaped structure, as well as spiral arms. It is called the "Milky Way" because it appears as a milky band of light in the night sky when we look up. 
To take pictures of the Milky Way, you need a few things: 
1. A camera: Any camera with manual settings will work, but a DSLR (digital single-lens reflex) camera is generally better for astrophotography because it allows you to control the exposure, focus, and other settings more easily. 
2. A lens: A wide-angle lens with a focal length of 18-35mm is generally best for capturing the Milky Way. 
3. A tripod: A tripod is essential for keeping your camera steady while taking long exposures. 
4. A clear, dark sky: The Milky Way is most visible on clear, dark nights away from light pollution (bright lights in cities can make it harder to see the stars). 
To take a picture of the Milky Way, you will need to set your camera to manual mode and adjust the settings to let in more light (this is called a "long exposure"). You will also need to focus your lens on infinity (a setting that allows you to take sharp photos of distant objects). Finally, you will need to take the picture using a long exposure time (around 10-30 seconds).

If that money was in an IRA or another tax deferred account , your parents never paid taxes on it in the first place . They would be required to pay taxes on it as it is withdrawn , and so are you . The deceased 's IRA rolls over into what is called a beneficiary IRA . You have to make required withdrawals from it when the deceased turns 70 , just as they would ... but the withdrawal amount is based on your life expectancy , so if you are young , the amount would be very small , and it would continue to grow tax free , likely in excess of the withdrawal amount . Or you could liquidate it immediately , and pay about half of it to taxes and penalties . Option # 1 is better for most people , I recommend consulting with a financial planner .

This is coming from someone who is a conservative republican - For the most part , It 's not quite that simple - Issues involving Taxation are FAR too complex for a true ELI5 The Current tax system is 1 ) Too severely weighted toward a progressive tax 2 ) Too Complex for anyone to understand as a whole Right now , almost 50 % of the US either pays no income taxes , or pays a net negative income tax . ( They get money given to them that they never paid into the system in the first place because of tax policies that amount to income redistribution . ) [ To show how weighted the system is right now - The top 1 % pays more in taxes than the bottom 90 % ] ( URL_0 ) . This leads to a situation where a significant portion of the population has no skin in the game . They have no reason not to insist on a larger government , with twice as much taxation . You can double , triple or quadruple taxes overall - they do n't care . It does n't affect them , cause they pay nothing to start with . It 's what economists call a Moral Hazard - you know whatever happens will cause someone else to pay the cost , not you . However , the answer we favor is not simply raise taxes on the poor and lower taxes on the rich . That by itself will not fix the fundamental problem . That problem is the insane complexity of the US tax system . It costs a lot of money to comply with the tax code - In many cases the costs of making sure you are in compliance outweigh the actual taxes paid , especially for businesses . Some of those costs include * Additional Record Keeping * Accountants * Lawyers * Lost Revenue , as your time and efforts could be spent on profitable activities instead of tax compliance * Economic Distortions - Tax loopholes and penalties give you an incentive to change your behaviors in ways that would not make business sense without them * Lobbying - If the tax system allows some groups to get preferential treatment , almost every business or interest group out there is going to lobby for them to get their own special treatment * Tax Evasion * Underground or Black Market Economy Simple tweaks of the rates here and there are not going to solve any of the major issues with the tax code - I want to see damn near everything we have wiped off the slate , and replaced with a much more straightforward tax plan . My personal preference would be something like the " Fair Tax " plan - Something that wipes out ALL income taxes , and replaces it with a across the board sales tax ( Along with a " Prebate " that would cover any sales taxes for a level up to the poverty level ) I 'm not necessarily married to that one specific proposal - but something of that nature that radically simplifies the current system , and puts millions of tax lawyers , lobbyists and accountants out of work while making it easier on the taxpayer would be good for me .

There are a few reasons why English dubs of foreign films can sometimes be perceived as comically atrocious. One reason is that it can be difficult to match the lip movements of the actors in the original version of the film with the English dialogue. This can lead to situations where the characters' mouths are moving in a way that doesn't match up with the words being spoken in the English version, which can be distracting for viewers. 
Another reason is that it can be challenging to find voice actors who are able to effectively convey the emotions and intentions of the characters in the original version of the film. This is especially true if the foreign film uses a lot of nonverbal communication, such as facial expressions and body language, to convey meaning. 
Finally, the quality of the English dub may also be affected by the translation of the original dialogue into English. It can be difficult to accurately convey the nuances and subtleties of the original language in another language, and this can lead to dialogue that feels awkward or unnatural. 
Overall, while there are certainly some well-done English dubs of foreign films out there, it is not uncommon for English dubs to be perceived as inferior to the original versions of the films.

Mixing different colors of light actually does result in a different color, just like mixing paint does. The difference is in how our eyes and brains interpret the colors. 
When you mix two colors of paint together, the pigments in the paint absorb certain wavelengths of light and reflect others. For example, red paint reflects red light and absorbs other colors. When you mix red and green paint together, the resulting color is a brownish color because the pigments in the paint are absorbing all of the colors of light except for brown. 
On the other hand, when you mix two colors of light together, like red and green, the resulting color is a bright yellow because the light is actually adding together. This is because light is made up of different wavelengths of energy, and when you mix two colors of light together, you are adding the wavelengths of energy together. 
So, the reason why mixing paint results in a different color than mixing light is because paint absorbs certain wavelengths of light, while light adds different wavelengths of energy together.

Marriage is a legally recognized relationship between two people that brings with it certain rights and responsibilities. These rights and responsibilities can include things like how property is shared, how financial assets are managed, and how decisions are made about the care and upbringing of children. 
The government recognizes marriages because it has an interest in promoting stable relationships and supporting families. By providing legal recognition to marriages, the government can help to ensure that couples have access to certain rights and protections that can help to strengthen their relationship and provide a more stable environment for any children they might have. 
If the government stopped recognizing marriages, it would be much more difficult for couples to access these rights and protections. For example, if a couple were not legally married, they might not have the same rights to make medical decisions for each other in an emergency, or to inherit from each other if one of them dies. 
Overall, the legal recognition of marriage helps to support strong and stable relationships, which can be good for individuals, families, and society as a whole.

China is a country in Asia that is governed by the Communist Party of China, which is a political party that has been in power since 1949. The Communist Party of China is the highest authority in the government and the country's political system. 
The President of China is the head of state and is elected by the National People's Congress, which is the highest state body in China. The Premier of China is the head of government and is appointed by the President with the approval of the National People's Congress. The Vice Premier of China is also appointed by the President with the approval of the National People's Congress. 
The President, Premier, and Vice Premier are responsible for running the government and making important decisions that affect the country. They work with other government officials and departments to make sure that the government is working well and that the needs of the people are being met. 
The Communist Party of China has a lot of influence over the government and the country's political system. The Party has control over who is elected or appointed to important positions in the government, and it also has the final say on important decisions that are made by the government. 
The legislature in China is the National People's Congress, which is made up of representatives from all parts of the country. The National People's Congress is responsible for making laws and overseeing the work of the government. 
There are also other governing bodies in China, such as the State Council, which is made up of the Premier and other government officials, and the Supreme People's Court, which is the highest court in the country. 
Overall, China is a country that is governed by the Communist Party of China, with the President, Premier, and Vice Premier leading the government and making important decisions. The National People's Congress and other governing bodies also play important roles in the country's political system.

In a solar system, the star is the central body around which all the other objects, such as planets, asteroids, and comets, orbit. The star is usually much more massive than any of the other objects in the system. The gravitational force of the star is what keeps all the other objects in their orbits. 
The reason why there aren't any solar systems with a huge planet in the middle instead of a star is because planets are not massive enough to have the gravitational force needed to keep all the other objects in orbit. A planet's gravity is much weaker than a star's gravity, so it wouldn't be able to hold onto all the other objects in the system. 
Imagine trying to swing a bucket of water around your head. If you swing it too slowly, the water will slosh out and the bucket will fall to the ground. But if you swing it fast enough, the water will stay in the bucket and it will stay in the air. It's the same way with a solar system. If the central body is not massive enough, it won't have enough gravity to keep all the other objects in orbit. But if it's massive enough, like a star, it will be able to hold onto all the other objects in the system.

The two most common causes of death are crushing and trampling . Crushing happens when someone is stuck up against an immovable object and the crowd surge from behind presses them so hard that they are crushed / suffocated . Trampling occurs when someone falls , no one sees / hears , and they are simply stepped on until they are injured enough to be killed . Sometimes , due to the pressure of the crowd , even if people are trying to pull the person up , they are forced over them lest they fall and be trampled as well . In both scenarios , the problem is the back pressure from the people near the rear who ca n't see the dangerous conditions that are occurring at the front of the stampede . They keep pressing forward , causing problems . This is a particular problem when there is a dangerous situation to the rear ( such as in a fire , trying to escape through narrow doors ) as everyone wants to haul ass out of there , but there is an obstruction up front .

When you browse the internet, your computer stores information about the websites you visit in a temporary storage area called a "cache." This helps the websites load faster when you visit them again because your computer can access the information it needs more quickly from the cache instead of downloading everything again from the internet. 
Sometimes, when you've been browsing the internet for a long time, your cache can get full. When this happens, your computer might have trouble loading new information from the internet, and you might see an error message like "There doesn't seem to be anything here." This can happen on any website, not just Reddit. 
To fix this problem, you can try clearing your cache. This will remove the stored information from your computer, freeing up space in the cache and allowing it to store new information. To clear your cache, you can follow the instructions for your specific web browser. For example, in Google Chrome, you can click on the "More" button (the three vertical dots) in the top right corner of the window, then select "More tools" and "Clear browsing data." You can then choose to delete the cache and other stored data, and click "Clear data" to confirm. This will clear your cache and allow your computer to store new information from the internet.

In many languages, including English, words for objects, animals, and other nouns are not inherently male or female. Instead, the gender of a noun is often assigned based on the gender of the person or being to which the noun refers. For example, the word "girl" refers to a female person, so in English, the word "girl" is considered a feminine noun. Similarly, the word "boy" refers to a male person, so it is considered a masculine noun in English. 
However, not all languages assign gender to nouns in this way. In some languages, the gender of a noun is based on the meaning of the word or its ending. For example, in Spanish, nouns that end in "-o" are usually masculine and nouns that end in "-a" are usually feminine. 
It's not entirely clear why some languages have developed systems for assigning gender to nouns, but it is thought that the origins of gendered language may be related to the way that people think about and conceptualize the world around them. Some linguists believe that gendered language may have evolved as a way to categorize and organize the objects and beings in the world in a way that is easier for people to understand and communicate about. 
Regardless of its origins, gendered language is an important feature of many languages and is used in a variety of contexts, including grammar, vocabulary, and even social interactions. While it can be confusing for learners of a new language, understanding and using gendered language is an important part of speaking and writing in many languages around the world.

However, live TV can also have a sense of excitement and immediacy that can be difficult to capture in a pre-filmed production.

The concept you are referring to is called time dilation, which is a phenomenon that occurs when two objects are in relative motion to each other and one object experiences time at a different rate than the other. This can happen because time and space are connected, and the presence of massive objects or high speeds can distort the fabric of space-time. 
To understand time dilation, imagine that you are standing on one planet and your friend is standing on another planet. You both have clocks that are perfectly synchronized, so at a given moment, both of your clocks read the same time. However, if your friend's planet is moving very fast relative to yours, or if it has a very strong gravitational field, the fabric of space-time around your friend's planet will be distorted, and time will pass more slowly there compared to on your planet. This means that if your friend spends 10 hours on their planet, from their perspective, it will feel like 10 hours have passed. However, from your perspective, it might feel like more than 10 hours have passed because time is passing more quickly on your planet. 
This phenomenon is usually only noticeable when the relative speed between the two objects is very high, or when one object is much more massive than the other. In everyday life, time dilation is not noticeable because the relative speeds and masses of objects are usually not enough to cause a significant difference in the flow of time.

They are really quite different . HTML is the language that websites are written in . It describes what elements are in the page and how they 're organized ( elements such as text , images , buttons and links ) . However , HTML originally did n't include the ability to show graphics , animations or videos ( other than pre - rendered images and animated gifs ) . HTML did provide the ability to include embedded objects , which would require the user to install a plugin that could display them . One such common plugin was Flash , which was basically another small program that runs inside your browser ( another such plugin is Java which can display Java Applets , but they 're hardly in use anymore ) . So developers could use Flash to create things like games ( such as the ones you 'll find in URL_0 ) and video players ( such as YouTube ) . HTML5 introduces new features to HTML , mainly the support for the < canvas > and < video > elements . These tags could be used to display graphics and videos without the need for an external plugin , and were handled by the browser and not by the plugin . HTML5 introduces many other new features , but these are the ones that are most relevant to this discussion ( HTML5 vs Flash ) . The main advantage of HTML5 is that it 's a standard that 's supported ( or should be supported ) by every browser . Drinking only salt water can be very dangerous and can lead to serious health problems. When you drink salt water, your body tries to get rid of the excess salt by producing more urine. This can lead to dehydration because your body is losing more fluids than it is taking in. 
Your body needs a balance of fluids and electrolytes (like sodium and potassium) to function properly. When you drink too much salt water, the excess salt can cause your body's electrolyte balance to become imbalanced, which can lead to a condition called hyponatremia. 
Symptoms of hyponatremia include headache, nausea, vomiting, seizures, and in severe cases, coma and death. It's important to drink fluids that contain the right balance of electrolytes, such as water or sports drinks, to stay hydrated and healthy. 
It's also important to note that drinking salt water is not a good way to hydrate your body. In fact, it can make your dehydration worse. If you are thirsty and need to hydrate your body, it's best to drink water or other beverages that are low in salt.

A PC cleanup is a process that involves removing unnecessary files and programs from your computer to free up storage space and improve its performance. Here are the steps that are typically involved in a standard PC cleaning: 
1. Uninstalling unnecessary programs: You can go to the "Control Panel" on your PC and look for programs that you no longer use or need. You can then select these programs and uninstall them to free up space on your hard drive. 
2. Removing temporary files: Temporary files are created by your operating system and various programs when they run. These files are not needed after the program has finished running, and they can take up a lot of space on your hard drive. You can use the "Disk Cleanup" tool on your PC to remove these temporary files. 
3. Emptying the recycle bin: The recycle bin is a folder on your computer where deleted files are stored until you decide to permanently delete them. Emptying the recycle bin will free up more space on your hard drive. 
4. Defragmenting your hard drive: When you use your computer, the data on your hard drive gets scattered across different locations. Defragmenting your hard drive involves organizing the data so that it is stored in a more efficient way, which can improve the performance of your computer. 
5. Updating your operating system: Keeping your operating system up to date can help fix any bugs or security vulnerabilities and improve the overall performance of your computer. 
6. Running a virus scan: A virus scan involves using antivirus software to search for and remove any malware (viruses, worms, Trojans, etc.) that may be present on your computer. This can help protect your computer from security threats and improve its performance. 
Doing these things can help make your computer run faster and more smoothly. It's a good idea to do a PC cleanup every once in a while to keep your computer in good shape.

Depression is a medical condition that affects the way you feel. It can be caused by a combination of physical, psychological, and environmental factors.

Symptoms of depression can be both physical and psychological. Physical symptoms may include changes in appetite and sleep patterns, fatigue, and body aches. Psychological symptoms may include feeling sad or hopeless, losing interest in activities you used to enjoy, and difficulty concentrating. 
It's important to note that everyone experiences depression differently, and treatment may involve a combination of therapy, medication, and lifestyle changes. If you think you or someone you know may be experiencing depression, it's important to speak with a healthcare provider for a proper evaluation and treatment plan.

A black hole is an object whose mass is so great that its escape velocity exceeds the speed of light . This means that once you pass a certain point of distance from it ( the event horizon ) not even light can travel fast enough to escape the gravitational pull of the object . When a massive star runs out of fuel to burn in its fusion it collapses under its own weight . That much mass in that little space creates an extremely dense white dwarf , or in the case of super massive stars ( tens or even hundreds of time the mass of the sun ) a black hole . A big misconception about black holes is that they 're like big vacuum cleaners sucking up everything around them . Even with their crazy properties , the area outside the event horizon of a black hole is still subject to the same laws of gravity as the rest of the universe . With enough velocity and distance , something can orbit a black hole without ever getting " sucked in " .
