Entropy is a measure of the disorder or randomness of a system. It can be thought of as a way to describe the amount of uncertainty or randomness in a system. 
Here's an example to help explain entropy: Imagine you have a jar with 10 red marbles and 10 blue marbles. The jar is organized, with all the red marbles on one side and all the blue marbles on the other side. In this case, the jar has low entropy because everything is very organized and there is little uncertainty about where the marbles are. 
Now imagine that you shake the jar up, so that the marbles are mixed together. Now it is difficult to predict where any individual marble will be when you reach into the jar. In this case, the jar has high entropy because there is a lot of randomness and uncertainty about where the marbles are. 
In thermodynamics, entropy is often used to describe the randomness or disorder of a system at the molecular level. For example, when a gas is allowed to expand, the molecules will spread out and become more disordered, resulting in an increase in entropy. Similarly, when a substance is cooled, the molecules will slow down and become more organized, resulting in a decrease in entropy.

The donkey and the elephant are symbols that have been associated with the Democratic and Republican parties, respectively, in the United States for more than a century. The origins of these symbols are not entirely clear, but they have become enduring symbols of the two major political parties in the United States. 
The donkey is typically associated with the Democratic Party and is often used as a symbol of the party in political cartoons and other forms of media. The origin of the donkey symbol is believed to date back to the 1828 presidential campaign of Andrew Jackson, who was the first Democratic Party candidate for president. Jackson's opponents referred to him as a "jackass," and he embraced the term, using the donkey on campaign posters and literature. The donkey has since become a symbol of the Democratic Party, often used to represent the party's commitment to the working class and its willingness to stand up for the rights of ordinary people. 
The elephant is typically associated with the Republican Party and is often used as a symbol of the party in political cartoons and other forms of media. The origin of the elephant symbol is believed to date back to the 1874 midterm elections, when a political cartoon by Thomas Nast appeared in Harper's Weekly magazine. The cartoon depicted the Republican Party as an elephant, and the image stuck. The elephant has since become a symbol of the Republican Party, often used to represent the party's conservative values and its commitment to limited government and individual responsibility.

Sources of light use what 's called an additive color model . Each source adds new wavelengths , thus the collective light is the sum of all sources . Thus when you have a red and green light , your eyes are receiving both red and green bands of light . Paints and pigments use a subtractive color model . In this case , the material absorbs some wavelengths and reflects others . No new wavelengths are added . Thus mixing colored paints , it will absorb more colors , causing fewer wavelengths to be reflected back . If mathematically inclined , think of additive sources as a union and substractive pigments as an intersect .

In some species of animals, such as salmon, the females lay eggs and the males fertilize them. After the eggs are fertilized, the males and females may both die. This is called semelparity. 
Semelparity, also known as "big bang" reproduction, is a reproductive strategy in which an organism reproduces only once and then dies. This is in contrast to iteroparity, in which an organism reproduces multiple times over the course of its lifetime. 
One reason why some species may have evolved to reproduce this way is that it can allow them to put all of their energy into producing a large number of offspring at once, rather than spreading their energy out over multiple reproductive events. This can be especially advantageous in environments where conditions are variable and unpredictable, because it allows the organism to take advantage of good conditions when they occur. 
For example, salmon reproduce in streams and rivers, and the conditions in these habitats can vary significantly from year to year. By reproducing all at once and then dying, salmon can ensure that their offspring will have a good chance of surviving and reproducing, even if conditions become unfavorable in the future. 
Overall, semelparity may not always be the most advantageous reproductive strategy, but it can be beneficial in certain situations.

Infrared thermometers work by measuring the amount of thermal radiation (heat) emitted by an object. When an object is heated, it emits infrared radiation, which is a type of electromagnetic radiation that is not visible to the human eye but can be detected by certain sensors, such as those found in infrared thermometers. 
When an infrared thermometer is pointed at a mirror, the sensor in the thermometer detects the infrared radiation emitted by the surface of the mirror. This is because the mirror is reflecting the infrared radiation emitted by the object that the mirror is facing, so the sensor in the thermometer is able to detect the radiation coming from the surface of the mirror. 
It's important to note that the infrared thermometer is not measuring the temperature of the object that the mirror is reflecting. Instead, it is measuring the temperature of the surface of the mirror itself. This is why an infrared thermometer will measure the temperature of a mirror's surface, rather than the surface that the mirror is reflecting.

Programming bugs, also known as software bugs, are mistakes that are made by programmers while writing code. These mistakes can cause the program to behave unexpectedly or not work at all. 
There are many ways that bugs can be introduced into a program. For example, a programmer might forget to include an important line of code, or might use the wrong type of data in a calculation. Sometimes, bugs are caused by mistakes in logic, where the programmer has written code that doesn't do what they intended it to do. 
It can be difficult to create something without bugs because programming is a complex task that involves many steps and requires attention to detail. It is easy to make mistakes when writing code, especially if the programmer is working on a large and complex program. In addition, it is often difficult to test all possible scenarios and combinations of inputs to a program, so it is possible for bugs to remain hidden until the program is being used in the real world. 
To reduce the number of bugs in a program, programmers often use techniques such as testing, debugging, and code reviews to identify and fix problems before the program is released. However, it is still possible for bugs to slip through and be discovered after the program is in use.

NASA retired the Space Shuttle program in 2011 because it had accomplished its goals and was becoming too expensive to maintain. The Space Shuttle was a reusable spacecraft that was used for many different missions, including transporting astronauts to and from the International Space Station (ISS). It was a very advanced and complex vehicle, but it also had some limitations. 
One of the reasons NASA decided to use rockets instead of a new upgraded type of Space Shuttle for the Orion spacecraft is that rockets are generally more powerful and can reach higher altitudes and faster speeds than the Space Shuttle. This is important for deep space missions, such as going to the Moon or Mars, because these destinations are much farther away from Earth than the ISS. 
Another reason is that the Space Shuttle required a lot of maintenance and preparation between flights, which made it less flexible and efficient than rockets. Rockets can be launched more quickly and can be used for a wider range of missions. 
Finally, NASA is always looking for ways to improve and advance its technology, and developing a new type of Space Shuttle would have been a very expensive and time-consuming project. Instead, NASA chose to focus on developing new rockets and spacecraft that are more suited to deep space exploration.

Great question! The Earth is not a perfect sphere, it is actually slightly flattened at the poles and bulges at the equator. This means that the Earth's rotational axis, which is an imaginary line that runs through the center of the Earth and around which the Earth rotates, is not perpendicular to the surface of the Earth. Instead, it is tilted by about 23.5 degrees. 
The North Pole is the point on the Earth's surface that is directly above the Earth's rotational axis. This is the point that is used to define the direction of "north." 
True North, on the other hand, is the direction of the North Pole relative to the surface of the Earth. It is the direction that a compass needle points towards, which is not the same as the direction of the North Pole. This is because the Earth's magnetic field causes the compass needle to be attracted to the Earth's magnetic north pole, which is not exactly the same as the Earth's rotational north pole. 
So, to sum it up: "North" is the direction that is defined by the Earth's rotational axis, and "True North" is the direction that a compass needle points towards due to the Earth's magnetic field. Both of these directions are important for different reasons, and that's why we have both of them.

Wisdom teeth, also known as third molars, are the last teeth to emerge in the mouth, usually appearing in the late teenage years or early twenties. These teeth were once an important part of the human diet because they helped people chew tough and fibrous foods that were common in our ancestors' diets. 
However, as our diets have changed and become softer over time, wisdom teeth have become less necessary. In many people, there is not enough room in the mouth for the wisdom teeth to emerge properly, which can cause problems such as pain, infection, and crowding of the other teeth. 
It's not entirely clear why wisdom teeth still exist if they can cause so many problems. Some scientists believe that wisdom teeth may have once served a useful purpose but are now vestigial, meaning that they have lost their original function due to evolution. Others think that wisdom teeth may still have some use, but more research is needed to understand this. 
Overall, wisdom teeth are a leftover from our ancestors' times and are not as important as they once were. However, they can still cause problems if they don't have enough room to emerge properly, so it's important to keep an eye on them and visit a dentist if you experience any pain or other issues.

The phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is a common one that people use when interacting with law enforcement officers in the United States. It is a way for people to ask whether they are being held against their will by the police, or if they are free to leave. This phrase has become more commonly used in recent years as people have become more aware of their rights when interacting with law enforcement. 
The phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is not necessarily a bad thing to say to a police officer. In fact, it is often seen as a way for people to assert their rights and to ensure that they are not being detained without cause. 
There is a legal precedent for this phrase in the United States. The Fourth Amendment to the United States Constitution protects people from unreasonable searches and seizures by the government. This means that law enforcement officers must have a valid reason, or "probable cause," to detain someone. If an officer does not have probable cause to detain someone, the person has the right to ask if they are being detained and to ask for the reason for the detention. If the officer does not have a valid reason, the person is usually free to leave. 
It is important to note that while the phrase "Am I being detained, officer?" is a way for people to assert their rights and ensure that they are not being detained without cause, it is not a guarantee that a person will be allowed to leave if they are being detained. It is always a good idea to cooperate with law enforcement officers, even if you believe that your rights are being violated. If you feel that your rights have been violated, you can seek legal advice or file a complaint with the appropriate authorities.

When you roll down a car window slightly and drive down the road, the wind rushing past the window can create a loud, high-pitched sound. This is because the air moving past the window is vibrating the window, and the vibrations are creating sound waves that your ear can hear. 
Imagine you have a piece of paper and you hold it up in front of your mouth and blow. The paper will vibrate back and forth, and you'll be able to hear the sound of the paper vibrating. It's the same principle with the car window. The wind blowing past the window is causing the window to vibrate, and the vibrations are creating a sound that you can hear. 
The sound is usually more noticeable when the window is only open a little bit because there is less space for the air to move through, which makes the window vibrate more. When the window is open all the way, there is more space for the air to move through, so the window doesn't vibrate as much and the sound is not as loud.

Wind is air that moves quickly through the atmosphere, or the layer of gases that surrounds the Earth. It is caused by differences in air pressure. 
Air pressure is the force that air exerts on objects. When the air pressure is higher in one place than another, the air will try to move from the area of higher pressure to the area of lower pressure to balance things out. This movement of air is what we call wind. 
For example, if the air pressure is higher over the land than over the ocean, the air will try to move from the land to the ocean. As the air moves, it picks up speed and creates wind. 
The strength and direction of the wind can be affected by many factors, such as the temperature of the air, the humidity, and the topography of the land. Wind can also be affected by the Earth's rotation, which causes the wind to curve and move in different directions. 
Wind is an important source of energy, and it is used to generate electricity in wind turbines. Wind turbines are tall towers with large blades that spin when the wind blows. The movement of the blades turns a generator, which creates electricity that can be used in homes and businesses.

In many cases, animals can determine who has won a fight for dominance based on body language and other signals. For example, if one animal is clearly submitting to the other by lowering their head, averting their gaze, or making submissive noises, it may be clear to the other animal that they have won the fight. It's also important to note that animals don't always have to fight to determine dominance. In some cases, they may use other forms of communication, such as vocalizations or gestures, to establish their dominance without physically engaging in a fight.

A polygraph test, also known as a lie detector test, is a way for people to try to figure out if someone is telling the truth or lying. During the test, the person being tested is hooked up to a machine that measures things like their heartbeat, how fast they are breathing, and how much they are sweating. The idea is that when someone is telling a lie, their body might react in a certain way that can be measured by the machine. 
There are a few different ways that the test can be done. One way is for the person being tested to answer a series of questions while hooked up to the machine. The person who is administering the test will ask a series of questions, some of which the person being tested will know the answer to and some of which they will not. The idea is that when the person being tested is telling the truth, their body will not react very much, but when they are lying, their body might show some signs of stress. 
There are some people who claim that they can cheat on a polygraph test by using techniques like taking deep breaths, biting their tongue, or thinking about something else while they are being tested. However, experts say that these techniques are not very effective and that it is difficult to cheat on a polygraph test. In general, it is best to just be honest during the test.

Molecular orbital theory is a way of understanding how the electrons in a molecule are arranged and how they behave. It helps us predict things like the shape of the molecule and how stable it is. 
Imagine that you have a bunch of balls that you want to stack on top of each other. Each ball represents an electron. The balls are attracted to each other because of the electrical charge they carry, and they want to be as close together as possible. 
In molecular orbital theory, we try to understand how the balls (electrons) are arranged in the stack (molecule) by creating "orbital" shapes that the balls can fit into. Each orbital can hold a certain number of balls, just like how a bucket can hold a certain number of marbles. 
The shapes of the orbitals are determined by the behavior of the electrons in the molecule. Some orbitals are more stable than others, which means the balls (electrons) are less likely to fall out of them. This can help us understand why some molecules are more stable than others.

Nutrition facts labels are required by law to be on most packaged foods in the United States. The information on these labels is based on scientific research and testing. 
To find out the number of calories in a food, researchers use a special machine called a bomb calorimeter. They put a small amount of the food in the machine and burn it. The heat that is given off is measured, and from this, the number of calories in the food can be calculated. 
To find out the amount of fat, protein, and other nutrients in a food, researchers use a process called proximate analysis. This involves using special chemicals to break down the food into its different parts, such as fat, protein, and carbohydrates. The amounts of these nutrients can then be measured. 
Vitamin and mineral content can also be determined through laboratory testing. Researchers can use special equipment to measure the amounts of different vitamins and minerals in a food. 
Overall, finding out the information that goes on nutrition facts labels requires careful scientific research and testing.

Airplane windows are not aligned with the seats because it would not be structurally sound to have them that way. The windows on an airplane are actually round, and they are placed in the fuselage (the main body of the airplane) in a way that distributes the weight of the plane evenly. If the windows were aligned with the seats, it would create weak points in the fuselage, which could be dangerous. It's important for the fuselage to be strong so that the airplane can withstand the forces it experiences during flight, such as turbulence and changes in altitude. So, even though it might seem more convenient to have the windows aligned with the seats, it's actually safer to have them placed where they are.

Human evolution is the process by which our species, Homo sapiens, has changed over time. It is not over, and it is still ongoing. While humans have developed technology that has allowed us to survive and thrive in a variety of environments, natural selection is still at work. 
Natural selection is the process by which certain traits or characteristics become more or less common in a population over time. These traits can be physical, such as the size and shape of our bodies, or they can be behavioral, such as the way we communicate or the way we interact with others. 
In the past, natural selection played a larger role in shaping human evolution because our survival and reproductive success depended more on our physical abilities and traits. For example, people who were stronger and faster were more likely to survive and have children, which meant that these traits became more common in the population over time. 
Nowadays, technology has allowed us to survive and thrive in a variety of environments, and our physical abilities and traits are not as important for our survival as they were in the past. However, natural selection is still at work, and certain traits and characteristics may still be more or less common in a population based on how well they help us survive and reproduce. 
So, to answer your question, human evolution is not over, and natural selection is still at work. However, the role of natural selection in shaping human evolution has changed over time due to the development of technology and other cultural and societal factors.

There are a few reasons why people around the world might not use the local language to refer to other countries. Here are a few: 
1. History: Some countries have long histories of colonizing or being colonized by other countries. This can lead to the names of those countries being adopted in other languages. For example, "Deutschland" (Germany's name in German) might be called "Germany" in English because English speakers have a long history of interacting with Germany and its people. 
2. Language: English is spoken by a large number of people around the world and is often used as a common language for communication between people who speak different languages. This means that many people might use the English names for countries when they are communicating with someone who speaks a different language. 
3. Convenience: It can be easier to use a country's name in a common language like English because it is a name that is familiar to many people. Using a country's local name might be confusing to some people who are not familiar with the language. 
Overall, there are a variety of reasons why people might not use the local language to refer to countries. It's important to remember that different cultures and languages have their own ways of referring to things, and it's always a good idea to be respectful of these differences.

The unemployment rate measures the percentage of people in the labor force who are looking for work but cannot find a job. It is calculated by dividing the number of unemployed people by the total number of people in the labor force, which includes both the employed and the unemployed. 
Sometimes, the number of people who are unemployed can go up, even if the unemployment rate goes down. This can happen when the number of people in the labor force decreases. For example, if a lot of people stop looking for work because they are discouraged or have given up hope of finding a job, they are no longer counted as part of the labor force. As a result, the number of unemployed people is divided by a smaller number, which can lead to a lower unemployment rate. 
On the other hand, if the number of people who are employed increases, the unemployment rate can go down, even if the number of people who are unemployed does not change. This can happen when more people start looking for work because they are more confident about finding a job. In this case, the number of unemployed people is divided by a larger number, which can lead to a lower unemployment rate. 
It is important to look at both the unemployment rate and the total number of people who are unemployed when evaluating the state of the job market. Together, these two measures can give a more complete picture of the employment situation.

Basically , you can move the lights around between camera setups , and you can color correct . In normal " single camera " shooting ( Like a normal 30 Rock episode ) , you can and do tweak the lighting pretty much every time you move the camera . Shooting live , you need a flatter " general purpose " lighting setup that will work okay for every angle but does n't necessarily look amazing at any angle . You also need all lights above the set and out of the field of view of the cameras in live production , whereas in single camera you can have a light or reflector just off camera for a nice effect . You can get the actors to shift their positions to get better shots . You can refresh makeup between takes . All sorts of little subtle things that you 'd never think of if you 've never been on a set because the end result makes it all look like it happens naturally . In live production , you ca n't change your mind about anything once the show starts . In many cases , you will also use different lenses in live production . In a single camera style shoot , you will have a person dedicated to focusing the camera . ( Focus Puller , or 1st Assistant Camera Operator . ) So , if you want to have nice soft blurry backgrounds that make the subject " pop " in the frame , you can shoot with " wide open " lenses so only the actor is in focus . To do that , you need to rehearse any camera or actor moves so the focus puller knows how to move the focus setting on the lens through the course of the shot . Autofocus is n't good enough because it does n't always know which thing should be in focus , and if it gets confused it may make the whole picture go blurry and useless for a bit which ruins the shot . The 1AC may need to use a measuring tape to check the exact distance between the camera and the actor to make sure things are exactly in focus , which requires walking into the camera 's field of view during shot setup . Obviously during live production you ca n't have somebody stopping the scene to measure , or repeating a take because focus was n't right so you " stop down " the lenses or use different lenses so you do n't get the nice soft background and more of the scene is in focus . When framing shots in single camera , you can put the camera right next to an actor 's face to get an interesting shot . But when shooting live , each camera has to stay out of the field of view of every other camera which limits how interesting and varied the shots can be that you can get . One camera person walking right up to an actor would be visible to the other cameras , ruining their shot in a live production so such gags would have to be * very * carefully planned . Then , in editing you would only pick the nicest looking takes , which is obviously impossible when shooting live . Then you send the project off to color correction where the image gets tweaked and refined until it looks like the director / DOP and colorist want it to . This is probably the biggest shift in the image that you are noticing , but it is the hardest to explain . Look for some colorist demo reels on vimeo , and you 'll see some idea of how much the image sometimes gets changed during coloring .

Bad or sloppy code can make a mobile app drain your battery faster in a few different ways: 
1. Poorly optimized algorithms: If the app is using algorithms that are not optimized for efficiency, it may take longer to complete certain tasks, which can drain the battery faster. 
2. Constant CPU usage: If the app is constantly using the CPU, even when it's not doing anything useful, it can drain the battery faster. 
3. Constant network usage: If the app is constantly making network requests or using data, it can drain the battery faster. 
4. Poorly designed user interface: If the app has a poorly designed user interface that requires the user to perform a lot of unnecessary actions, it can drain the battery faster. 
5. Lack of power management: If the app doesn't properly manage power usage, it may keep the device's power-hungry components, such as the CPU or display, turned on unnecessarily, which can drain the battery faster. 
To summarize, bad or sloppy code can make an app drain your battery faster by causing it to use more CPU and network resources, having a poorly designed user interface, or not properly managing power usage.

A more accurate way to phrase the question , a way that would also more or less answer it , is to ask , " What is the evolutionary disadvantage of wisdom teeth to someone who has not yet procreated ? " The answer is : not much . It 's tempting to think that evolution means useless things should disappear over time , sort of like a genetic atrophy , but they do n't work that way . Something has to specifically select _ against _ the useless feature . If having wisdom teeth made people less attractive , or raised the likelihood of them dying before reproducing , then we could postulate that there would be evolutionary pressure against larger wisdom teeth and that , over time , more and more people with smaller wisdom teeth would be selected , leading to a gradual evolutionary decline in wisdom teeth . Since that 's not the case , they 're here to stay . An analogue to help cement the idea would be the appendix . If it 's so useless and so ( potentially ) lethal , why do we still have it ? Because by the time someone 's appendix becomes inflamed , they 've probably already had kids and passed the genes along . If the median age for appendicitis over the last couple thousand years was more in the range of five - years - old , there would be much greater selective pressure to eliminate it .

4. The baby will have genetic material from three people: the first woman, the second woman, and the man whose sperm was used to fertilize the second woman's egg.

Derivatives and integrals are operations that allow us to find the rate of change of a function or the area under a curve, respectively. The derivative of a function tells us how the function is changing at any given point, and the integral of a function gives us the total amount that the function has changed over a certain interval. 
The derivative of x^2 is 2x because x^2 is increasing at a rate of 2x at every point. In other words, if x is increasing by a small amount, then x^2 is increasing by twice that amount. This relationship is captured by the derivative of x^2, which is 2x. 
The derivative of sin^2 x, on the other hand, is not equal to 2 sin x. The derivative of sin^2 x is actually 2 sin x cos x. This is because sin^2 x is not increasing at a constant rate like x^2. Instead, the rate at which sin^2 x is increasing depends on the value of sin x and cos x at that point. When sin x is large and cos x is small, sin^2 x is increasing quickly. When sin x is small and cos x is large, sin^2 x is increasing more slowly. The derivative of sin^2 x takes this changing rate of change into account. 
Similarly, the integral of x is x^2/2 because the total amount that x has changed over a certain interval can be represented by the area of a rectangle with base x and height x/2. The integral of sin x or cos x, on the other hand, cannot be represented by a simple polynomial like x^2/2. Instead, these functions require more complex mathematical expressions to describe their total change over an interval.

There 's a concept in economics called the money multiplier . Basically , a bank is only required to keep 10 % of the value of its deposits on hand , and loans out the other 90 % . The money that 's loaned out is spent , deposited in a bank , and loaned out again . For example , A bank has $ 100,000 in deposits . It keeps $ 10,000 on hand , and lends $ 90,000 to Bob for a new house . Bob gives that $ 90,000 to the house 's previous owner , who deposits it in the bank . The bank keeps $ 9000 on hand and lends the other $ 81,000 to Jeff for a new sports car . The car dealer gets paid , the bank keeps $ 8100 on hand , and loans out the other 72,900 to Mike . At this point , on an initial deposit of $ 100,000 , the bank has $ 244,000 lent out , and is collecting interest on it .

There are five reasons why animals have two eyes ( rather than one ): 1 . Better depth perception . The image on the two eyes is slightly different and by comparing the locations of the images , an animal can locate objects in depth . It is not the only depth cue but it is an important one for predator or tree dwelling species . 2 . Wider field of view . The animal can see more of the visual world with two eyes than with one . 3 . Each eye has a blind spot , and each eye covers the blind spot of the other . There is a blindspot in each eye where the optic nerve leaves the eye , and in that region of the eye , there are no photoreceptors so you are blind there . However , the other eye covers that region so you still know what is there . 4 . Easier to see dim lights with two eyes than one . Two eyes both catching light particles will have a lower threshold for detecting dim lights than just one eye . 5 . Most importantly : as a spare ! An animal still has a good chance of surviving with one eye , but very little chance of reproducing with none . Now , why do n't we have MORE than two eyes ? Likely because , in mammals , the number of new nerve cells that would have to be supported by an additional eye would not be worth the benefits of having it . Nerve cells are the hungriest cells in the body , and more of the cerebral cortex is devoted to vision than any other function . Having additional eyes ( beyond two ) in mammals would require huge numbers of new nerve cells to support them , and the metabolic cost would not be worth the benefit .

It's important to note that the way animals experience and express emotions is likely to be different from the way humans do. However, the basic functions of emotions and social bonds, like love, are likely to be similar across different species.

To make the game more easy / difficult you could alter the chance of them taking random actions instead of optimal actions . There are a lot of different AI structures that could be used for decision making for things like sports games , but each of them can be skewed to make certain decisions by altering the parameters in the game .

When a speaker plays too loudly and the sound distorts, it means that the speaker is being asked to produce sound waves that are too powerful for it to handle. This can happen for a few different reasons. 
One reason is that the speaker's voice coil, which is a thin wire that moves back and forth very quickly to create sound waves, may not be able to move fast enough to keep up with the demands of the signal being sent to it. This can cause the sound waves to become distorted and fuzzy. 
Another reason is that the speaker's cone, which is the part of the speaker that moves back and forth to create sound waves, may be moving too far in one direction or the other. This can cause the cone to hit the limits of its movement, which can also cause the sound waves to become distorted. 
When a speaker is being asked to play too loudly and the sound distorts, it can be bad for the speaker. The speaker may be damaged if it is asked to produce sound waves that are too powerful for it to handle over an extended period of time. AM and FM radio signals sound different because they use different methods to transmit information. AM stands for "amplitude modulation," and FM stands for "frequency modulation." 
In AM radio, the strength or amplitude of the radio signal is used to carry the information, such as music or speech. The signal is sent out at a specific frequency, and the amplitude (or strength) of the signal is changed to transmit the information. 
In FM radio, the frequency of the radio signal is used to carry the information. The signal is sent out at a specific frequency, and the frequency of the signal is changed slightly to transmit the information. 
Because AM radio uses changes in the amplitude of the signal to transmit information, it can be more prone to interference from things like electrical storms or other sources of interference. This can make the sound quality of AM radio less clear than FM radio. FM radio, on the other hand, uses changes in the frequency of the signal to transmit information, which makes it less prone to interference and generally gives it better sound quality.

Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world, with a peak that reaches an elevation of about 29,029 feet (8,848 meters) above sea level. It is not actually growing in height, but rather, it is being uplifted by the movement of the Earth's crust. This process is called tectonic uplift, and it occurs when the Earth's plates collide and push against each other. 
As for whether the peak of Mount Everest could eventually reach space, the answer is no. The Earth's atmosphere extends about 62 miles (100 kilometers) above the surface of the Earth, and the peak of Mount Everest is much shorter than that. Even if Mount Everest were to continue to be uplifted at a rate of two inches per year, it would take many thousands of years for it to reach the edge of the atmosphere. 
There are also other factors that limit the height that a mountain can reach. For example, the weight of the mountain itself can cause it to sink down into the Earth's crust. Additionally, erosion caused by wind and water can gradually wear away at the mountain, causing it to become shorter over time. 
So, to sum it up, Mount Everest is not going to grow tall enough to reach space. It is being uplifted by the movement of the Earth's plates, but there are other factors that limit its height and prevent it from growing indefinitely.

Let 's say you buy stock at $ 10 . You hold it for 20 years , and now it 's worth $ 100 . Normally , when you sell that asset , you pay capital gains taxes on the $ 90 difference in price . Now , you put that stock into a trust and you drop dead before selling it . Your children crack the trust at the $ 100 level . When they sell it , they do n't pay the capital gains tax on the $ 90 profit , as they obtained it at the $ 100 level . Thus , all that income generated from the stock is never taxed at all . As a result , there 's $ 90 per share worth of taxes [ or , more accurately , 20 years of capital gains taxes ] just getting lost .

Power = Current x Voltage Watts = Amps x Volts Meaning you can have high amps and low volts and get the same number of watts as low amps and high voltage . ( 1 Amp at 100 V is the same amount of power as 100 A at 1 V ) . With the same analogy , if I punched you really hard ( high voltage ) once ( small amps ) , you might feel the same as if I punched you very lightly ( small voltage ) but many times ( high amperage ) . The total ' power ' is still the same .

Libertarians believe that people should be free to make their own choices and decisions, as long as they do not harm others. They generally support a smaller government that does not interfere with people's personal or economic affairs, and believe in the importance of private property and free markets. 
There is no one type of person who is into libertarianism. Some libertarians may be wealthy, while others may not be. Some may be conservative, while others may be liberal. Libertarians come from all walks of life and may have a variety of different beliefs and values. 
In a libertarian society, the government would have a limited role and would not regulate or control many aspects of people's lives. Instead, individuals would be free to make their own choices and decisions about how to live their lives. People would be able to own and control their own property, and the economy would be based on free markets and voluntary exchange. There would be no taxes or other forms of coercion used to force people to do things they do not want to do. 
Overall, the goal of libertarianism is to create a society in which individuals are free to live their lives as they see fit, as long as they do not harm others.

Most electronic devices, including cell phones, use lithium-ion batteries, which have a built-in mechanism for keeping track of their charge level. Inside the battery, there are tiny electrical components called "charge coulometers" that keep track of the amount of charge going in and out of the battery. 
When the battery is being charged, the coulometers measure the amount of charge going into the battery and store this information. When the battery is being used to power the device, the coulometers measure the amount of charge being used and update the stored information accordingly. 
The device's battery meter then uses this stored information to display the current charge level on the device's screen. So, the battery doesn't actually "put off" more energy when it is full. Instead, it is the coulometers inside the battery that keep track of the charge level and let the device know how much battery life is left.

Stars, like our own Sun, are made up of very hot gases, mostly hydrogen and helium. They give off energy in the form of light and heat, which is what we feel here on Earth as sunlight. 
Stars are able to stay in balance and not explode or collapse because of something called nuclear fusion. This is a process that happens in the center of the star, where the temperature and pressure are extremely high. In nuclear fusion, the hydrogen atoms combine to form helium atoms, and this releases a huge amount of energy in the form of light and heat. 
This energy helps to hold the star up and keep it from collapsing in on itself. The energy from nuclear fusion also helps to push outwards against the weight of the outer layers of the star, which would otherwise cause the star to collapse. 
In short, the energy produced by nuclear fusion helps to balance the forces acting on a star and keeps it from collapsing or exploding.

In an electrical circuit, positive and negative charges flow through wires to power devices like lights or appliances. The positive charge flows through the circuit to the negative charge, creating an electrical current. 
Sometimes, circuits are set up with just a positive and negative wire, and these are called "floating" circuits. These types of circuits can be used in simple devices like a flashlight, where the positive and negative charges flow through the circuit and back again, turning on the light. 
However, in many cases, it is important to have a third wire in the circuit, called a "ground" wire. This ground wire provides a safe path for the electrical current to flow to the ground, or Earth, if there is a problem with the circuit. 
For example, if a device has a short circuit or malfunction, the excess electricity may try to flow through the device and could potentially cause a fire or injury. By having a ground wire, the excess electricity can flow safely to the ground instead of flowing through the device or a person. 
Ground wires are often used in circuits that have higher voltages, like those found in buildings or homes, because the risk of injury or damage is greater in these types of circuits. However, ground wires can also be used in smaller circuits to provide an additional layer of safety.

There is another type- the Radio - Controlled Clock , which uses the national [ WWVB Service ] ( URL_0 ) It transmits on a 60KHz carrier wave , which is EXCEPTIONALLY low frequency for radio . It is only 1 second per bit and takes a full minute to transmit a frame of time data . Also , the amplitude - modulated type has no error - correction data , which just is n't done in modern data transmission . It makes it very hard to tell if you actually got data or not , and it 's so slow that you would need to just wait until you see consecutive frames that all make sense and count up . The phase - modulated version does have error correction bits . These clocks do n't rely on a backup battery , they just listen for the time . How long it takes to update themselves varies , sometimes it takes awhile to get a complete frame . GPS and cell phone services also broadcast time , but it 's considerably more complicated to receive . WWVB is commonly used on devices which are only clocks / watches .

" Cleaning " is a very undescriptive term in the computer world . In most cases , it indicates stuff like deleting temporary files ( files programs create while running but do n't need ) , deleting old versions of programs that are left behind after updating , emptying the recycle bin , and otherwise deleting files that accumulate on your hard drive but are not necessary . Windows machines have a built - in tool that can take care of most of that automatically ( called " Disk Cleanup " ) . I 'm less familiar with Mac and Linux , but I would n't be surprised if they both also had similar tools . In general , this sort of cleanup can free up some disk space , though it 's unlikely to give a large amount back . Other than that , it does n't provide any other benefit . As a side note , if you want to free up even more disk space and/or make your computer run faster , I suggest uninstalling any programs you have but do n't use . This includes a lot of the bloatware that gets prepackaged with many computers , which tends to be unnecessary . Any program that starts automatically when you turn on your computer is an especially good target , as those will run constantly and take up some computer resources all the time . If you do n't want to uninstall them , you might consider disabling their " run on startup " feature if you do n't need them running all the time , usually found in a settings menu somewhere .

The lines with red bumps and blue triangles that you see on the weather forecast are called isobars. They are lines on a weather map that connect points with the same atmospheric pressure. The pressure is measured in units called hectopascals (hPa). 
When the isobars are close together, it means that the pressure is changing rapidly over a small distance. This usually indicates that the weather is going to be stormy or windy. 
The red bumps on the isobars represent areas of high pressure. High pressure usually means that the weather will be clear and dry, because the air is sinking towards the surface of the Earth. 
The blue triangles on the isobars represent areas of low pressure. Low pressure usually means that the weather will be cloudy and rainy, because the air is rising up into the atmosphere.

A hostile takeover is when a company tries to take control of another company without the support of the company's management or board of directors. This can happen when the company that is trying to take over, called the "acquirer," makes an offer to buy the other company, called the "target," directly to the target's shareholders. The acquirer usually offers a higher price per share than what the shares are currently worth on the stock market, which can be tempting for the target's shareholders. 
One way that the acquirer can try to take over the target is by buying a large number of the target's shares, usually at least 5%. When an acquirer owns 5% or more of a company's shares, it is required to disclose this information to the public and the company. This is because owning a large percentage of a company's shares can give the acquirer significant influence over the company's decisions. 
If the acquirer is able to buy enough shares to gain control of the target, it can then try to replace the target's management and board of directors with its own people. This can be done through a vote of the shareholders. If the acquirer is successful in replacing the management and board, it can then take control of the company and make decisions about how to run it. 
However, hostile takeovers can be risky and can sometimes lead to legal battles between the acquirer and the target. The target's management and board may also try to fight the takeover by trying to find another company to buy the target or by making it more difficult for the acquirer to buy the necessary shares.

There are several reasons why planes with multiple wings are not typically used. One reason is that they are more complex and expensive to build than planes with fewer wings. They also tend to be heavier and less efficient, which means that they require more power to fly and use more fuel.

Gluten is a protein found in wheat, barley, and rye. Some people have a condition called celiac disease, which means that their bodies are unable to properly digest gluten. When people with celiac disease eat foods that contain gluten, it can damage the lining of their small intestine and cause a range of symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and malnutrition. 
For people who do not have celiac disease, gluten is generally not harmful and can be a part of a healthy diet. However, some people may have a sensitivity to gluten that causes them to experience symptoms such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain after consuming it. 
Some people choose to follow a gluten-free diet because they believe it may help with certain health conditions or because they feel that it helps them to feel better overall. However, it's important to note that a gluten-free diet is not necessarily healthier for everyone and may be more expensive and less nutritionally dense than a diet that includes gluten.

During the Cold War, the city of Berlin was located deep within the territory of the German Democratic Republic (GDR), also known as East Germany. The city was divided into two parts: West Berlin, which was controlled by the Western powers (the United States, the United Kingdom, and France), and East Berlin, which was controlled by the Soviet Union. 
West Berlin was located on the western side of the Berlin Wall, which was a heavily fortified barrier that separated the two parts of the city. The wall was built in 1961 to prevent people from East Germany from fleeing to the West. It was guarded by East German soldiers, and anyone who tried to cross the wall illegally could be shot. 
Despite being surrounded by East Germany, West Berlin was not controlled by the GDR or the Soviet Union. Instead, it was a free and democratic city, similar to other cities in Western Europe. The Western powers provided the city with food, fuel, and other supplies to help it survive. They also allowed people from West Berlin to travel freely to other parts of Western Europe. 
East Berlin, on the other hand, was a communist city and was controlled by the GDR and the Soviet Union. It was not as open or free as West Berlin, and people who lived there had fewer freedoms and rights. 
The situation in Berlin remained like this until the end of the Cold War in the 1990s, when the Berlin Wall was finally taken down and the city was reunited.

There are several ways that planes can be prevented from impersonating other airplanes and entering a country's airspace without proper authorization. Here are a few examples:

If an aircraft is behaving suspiciously or is not following the proper flight procedures, it can be detected and investigated. 
2. Air traffic control: Air traffic controllers are responsible for managing the flow of aircraft within a country's airspace. They communicate with pilots and provide them with instructions on where to fly and how to safely navigate around other aircraft. If an aircraft does not respond to their communications or appears to be behaving suspiciously, air traffic controllers can alert the appropriate authorities. 
3. Military aircraft: Many countries also have military aircraft that are specifically designed to patrol their airspace and protect against unauthorized intrusions. These aircraft can intercept and investigate any aircraft that appears to be behaving suspiciously. 
4. International agreements: There are also international agreements in place that outline the rules for aircraft entering another country's airspace. For example, if an aircraft wants to enter another country's airspace, it must first request permission and provide information about its intended flight path and the type of aircraft it is. If the request is not granted, the aircraft is not allowed to enter the airspace. 
Overall, there are many measures in place to prevent planes from impersonating other airplanes and entering a country's airspace without proper authorization.

Surveys try to account for bias and lying in a few different ways. Here are a few examples: 
1. Random sampling: Survey researchers try to select a representative sample of people to participate in the survey. By randomly selecting participants, researchers aim to minimize the chance that certain groups are overrepresented or underrepresented in the sample. 
2. Anonymity: Surveys may be designed to be anonymous, which means that the researchers do not know who filled out the survey. This can help reduce bias because people may feel more comfortable being honest when they don't have to worry about being identified. 
3. Neutral wording: Survey questions should be worded in a neutral way, without implying a certain answer. For example, a question that asks "Do you think it is wrong to drink and drive?" might bias people towards saying "yes" because of the use of the word "wrong." Instead, a better question might be "Do you think it is safe to drink and drive?" 
4. Pilot testing: Before a survey is given to a large group of people, it is often tested with a smaller group to see if there are any problems with the questions or how they are worded. This can help researchers identify and fix any potential biases in the survey.

When you delete a file from your computer, phone, or other device, the data is not immediately erased. Instead, the space that the file occupies on the storage device is marked as available for use, and the data is not actually removed until it is overwritten by new data. 
Think of it like this: Imagine you have a piece of paper with writing on it, and you want to delete the writing. You can't just make the writing disappear, but you can mark the paper as blank and ready to be used again. If you then write something new on the paper, the old writing will be covered up and effectively "deleted." 
When you delete a file, the operating system marks the space on the storage device as available for reuse. The data is still there, but the system treats it as if it's not. If you save a new file, the system will probably use the space that the old file was occupying to store the new one. As a result, the old data will be overwritten and effectively erased it. 
There are some cases where deleted data can be recovered, but it requires special software and is not something that most people can do on their own. In general, once a file has been overwritten, it is very difficult, if not impossible, to recover the original data.

Snow melters can be a useful tool for removing snow, but they are not a universally applicable solution. There are several reasons why they may not be used more widely: 
1. Cost: Snow melters can be expensive to purchase and maintain, especially if they are large enough to handle the amount of snow that accumulates in a city. 
2. Energy consumption: Snow melters require a lot of energy to operate, which can be costly and may not be environmentally friendly. 
3. Limited capacity: Snow melters can only melt a certain amount of snow at a time, so they may not be able to keep up with heavy snowfall or large accumulations of snow. 
4. Infrastructure: In order to use a snow melter, a city would need to have the necessary infrastructure in place to transport the melted snow to a drain or other disposal location. This can be costly and logistically challenging. 
5. Environmental concerns: Melting snow can produce runoff that contains pollutants and other contaminants that can end up in rivers and streams. This can be harmful to the environment and may require additional treatment before the runoff can be safely released. 
Overall, while snow melters can be a useful tool in certain situations, they are not always the most practical or cost-effective solution for snow removal.

Key Existential Concepts The meaninglessness of life , the absence of God , the loneliness of being a thinking individual — it sounds like the existentialists were n't the happiest group of folks , right ? Not necessarily true . Read on to get an idea of what existentialism is all about . Absurdity : What human beings encounter when they come into contact with the world . Absurdity is brought about because the human instinct to seek order and meaning is frustrated by the refusal of the world to be orderly or meaningful . Anxiety : Kierkegaard said , " Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom . " You feel anxiety because you recognize that you and you alone are responsible for your actions . This produces the two - sided feeling of simultaneous dread and exhilaration . Alienation : The sense that you 're a stranger in the world , or a stranger to yourself . Many aspects of existence can be alienating . One of the primary sources is absurdity . Ironically , the stories and systems developed by philosophy and religion to address that absurdity can be just as alienating . Existence precedes essence : Sartre 's phrase to describe the existential situation humans find themselves in . It refers to the fact that when you 're born , you have no meaning , no purpose , no definition . Human beings exist first , and only later define themselves . The Übermensch : The word Nietzsche uses to refer to his ideal human being . Literally " overman , " the word reflects the importance in his philosophy of overcoming — overcoming traditional values , overcoming the herd mentality , and , most importantly , overcoming yourself . You overcome these things so that you might attain something greater . Nietzsche 's Übermensch is an unconventional creator of values , a joyous free spirit , and one who embraces the earth instead of pining away for heaven . The death of God : The death of the notion that belief in God alone , or belief in any religious or philosophical system , is sufficient to provide human beings with the meaning , purpose , and definition they crave . It 's the recognition that , because no external system can provide you with the answers , you must take responsibility for providing them yourself . Subjectivity : Your first - person perspective on the world , including the needs , desires , and emotions that accompany that perspective . The existentialists take this as a valid and important starting point for genuinely human endeavors . This can be contrasted with the scientific mindset , which always starts with objectivity — seeing people in impersonal , objective terms without emotion or appreciation for their individual point of view .

A hypoallergenic animal is one that is less likely to cause an allergic reaction in people who are sensitive to pets. The term "hypoallergenic" does not mean that the animal is completely allergy-free, but it usually means that it produces fewer allergens, which are proteins that can cause an allergic reaction. 
Dogs and cats have both hair and fur. The main difference between the two is that hair is usually longer and coarser, while fur is shorter and softer. Hair also tends to shed less than fur. Some people believe that animals with hair, rather than fur, are less likely to cause allergies because they produce fewer allergens. However, this is not necessarily true for all hypoallergenic pets. 
There are several breeds of dogs and cats that are considered hypoallergenic because they produce fewer allergens than other breeds. These breeds may have hair rather than fur, or they may have a different type of fur that is less likely to cause an allergic reaction. Some examples of hypoallergenic dog breeds include the Bichon Frise, Poodle, and Schnauzer. Some examples of hypoallergenic cat breeds include the Devon Rex, Sphynx, and Siamese. 
It's important to note that while these breeds may be less likely to cause an allergic reaction, they are not completely allergy-free. If you are allergic to pets and want to get one, it's a good idea to spend some time with the animal before you make a commitment to ensure that you are not allergic to it.

Traditionally, new seasons of TV shows have premiered in the fall because that is when the major networks (like ABC, CBS, NBC, and Fox) have their "upfronts," which are presentations to advertisers about the new shows that they will be airing in the upcoming television season. The upfronts usually take place in May, and the new TV seasons begin in September. This allows the networks to promote their new shows to a large audience over the summer, so that people will be more likely to watch when the new seasons begin. 
There are a few reasons why it might make sense for some shows to come out in the spring or summer instead. For one thing, there is typically less competition for viewership during these times of year, since there are fewer new shows premiering. This can be especially helpful for shows that are trying to establish a strong following and might get lost in the shuffle during the crowded fall season. Additionally, the weather is generally nicer in the spring and summer, which can make it a more appealing time for people to sit down and watch TV. 
Overall, it really depends on the show and what the creators and networks think will be the best time for it to be released. Some shows might be more successful in the fall, while others might do better in the spring or summer.

A stawman argument is when you misrepresents your opponents views and argue against that misrepresentation rather than your opponents real points . Example from wikipedia : > A : We should relax the laws on beer . > B : No , any society with unrestricted access to intoxicants loses its work ethic and goes only for immediate gratification . > The proposal was to relax laws on beer . Person B has exaggerated this to a position harder to defend , i.e. , " unrestricted access to intoxicants " . It is a logical fallacy because Person A never made that claim .

Emissivity is a measure of how well an object is able to emit or radiate heat. Some materials are better at emitting heat than others. A material's emissivity can be increased by making its surface rougher, or by painting it a darker color. 
Flat black paint is often used to increase an object's emissivity because it absorbs a large amount of light and heat. When an object is painted black, it absorbs more of the energy that hits its surface, rather than reflecting it back. This absorbed energy can then be radiated back out as heat, which can help to cool the object. 
Heat sinks are designed to dissipate or spread out heat to help cool electronic devices. While painting a heat sink flat black could increase its emissivity, it might not be the most effective way to cool the device. This is because heat sinks rely on air flow to dissipate heat, and a flat black paint job could interfere with this air flow. In addition, the heat sink might get too hot to touch if it is painted black, which could be a safety issue. 
Overall, painting something flat black can increase its emissivity, but it might not always be the best solution for cooling an object.

Developers make "better" compression algorithms by finding ways to compress data more efficiently. This means that they are able to reduce the size of the data while still maintaining the same quality. There are a few different ways that they can do this. 
One way is by finding more efficient ways to represent the data. For example, instead of representing each pixel in an image as a separate piece of information, they might use patterns or colors to represent groups of pixels. This can help to reduce the amount of data that needs to be stored. 
Another way is by finding more efficient ways to compress the data. This might involve using algorithms that are more efficient at finding patterns in the data, or using techniques like predictive coding to better estimate what data is most important to keep. 
Finally, developers can also make new codecs (compression algorithms for audio and video) better by improving the way that they handle different types of data. For example, they might create codecs that are better at compressing video with a lot of fast-moving action, or codecs that are better at compressing audio with a wide range of frequencies. 
Overall, the goal of compression algorithms is to reduce the amount of data that needs to be stored or transmitted, while still maintaining the quality of the original data. As technology and our understanding of data compression improve, we are able to create more efficient algorithms that can compress data even more effectively.

In movies, television shows, and other media, the camera is usually hidden or placed in such a way that it is not visible in the mirror. This is done through a technique called "shooting through a two-way mirror." 
A two-way mirror is a special type of mirror that allows people on one side to see through it while people on the other side see their own reflection. The camera is placed on the side where the people can see through the mirror, and the actors are on the other side, looking into the mirror. This creates the illusion that the camera is not there and the actors are looking at themselves in a regular mirror. 
Another way to hide the camera in a shot like this is to use a special kind of camera called a "reflex camera." A reflex camera has a mirror inside it that reflects the image being captured up into the viewfinder, so the person taking the picture can see what they are photographing. When the picture is taken, the mirror flips up out of the way, allowing the image to be captured on film or digitally. 
In both of these cases, the camera is hidden from view, so it appears as if the character is looking at themselves in a regular mirror.

In a criminal trial, the defendant is considered innocent until proven guilty. The prosecution, or the side that is trying to prove that the defendant is guilty, is responsible for presenting evidence and witness testimony to show that the defendant committed the crime. The defense, or the side representing the defendant, is responsible for challenging the prosecution's evidence and presenting their own evidence and arguments to try to show that the defendant is not guilty. 
As part of this process, the defense lawyer is not responsible for asking the defendant whether they committed the crime. Instead, the lawyer's job is to present a defense for the defendant, which may include questioning the prosecution's evidence and witnesses, presenting evidence and witness testimony on behalf of the defendant, and making legal arguments to try to show that the defendant is not guilty. 
This process is in place to ensure that the defendant receives a fair trial. It allows the defendant to have an advocate who can present a defense on their behalf, and it also helps to protect against wrongful convictions by requiring the prosecution to prove their case beyond a reasonable doubt.

Internet download speeds are typically faster than upload speeds because most people use the internet to download more than they use it to upload. For example, when you are browsing the internet, you are downloading information from websites onto your device. When you watch a video on YouTube, you are downloading the video from YouTube's servers onto your device. When you send an email or message, you are uploading information from your device to the internet. 
Internet service providers (ISPs) usually focus on providing fast download speeds because that is what most people need. They also need to balance the amount of bandwidth they provide for download speeds with the amount of bandwidth they provide for upload speeds. If they provide too much bandwidth for downloads, it could cause problems for people uploading information. 
So, to sum it up, internet download speeds are typically faster than upload speeds because most people use the internet to download more than they use it to upload, and ISPs need to balance the amount of bandwidth they provide for both types of internet traffic.

In the British Army and the US Army, an NCO (Non-Commissioned Officer) is a member of the military who has a rank above a private but below a commissioned officer. NCOs are responsible for leading small groups of soldiers and helping commissioned officers manage and train their troops. Some examples of NCO ranks in the British Army include Corporal, Sergeant, and Warrant Officer. 
Commissioned officers, on the other hand, are leaders who have been given the authority to command groups of soldiers and make decisions on behalf of the military. They hold ranks such as Second Lieutenant, Captain, and Major. Commissioned officers are responsible for leading and directing their troops and making strategic decisions. 
In both the British Army and the US Army, NCOs and commissioned officers have different roles and responsibilities. NCOs focus on leading and training soldiers at the ground level, while commissioned officers have a more strategic and managerial role.

Photos of other planets often look different from photos of Earth because they are taken by spacecraft that are far away from the planets and are using special cameras to capture images. These cameras are designed to work in the harsh conditions of space, where there is no air, and can take pictures with a very high level of detail. Tilting your rearview mirror up at night allows you to see a dimmer version of what's behind you because the mirror is designed to reflect light from the rear of your vehicle. When you tilt the mirror up, the light from the headlights of the vehicles behind you is reflected off the mirror and into your eyes. This can be helpful at night because it allows you to see what's behind you without being blinded by the bright headlights. 
The mirror is not just tilting up to hit the ceiling inside your car. It is still reflecting light, just at a different angle. When you tilt the mirror up, you are changing the angle at which the light is reflected off the mirror and into your eyes. This change in angle causes the light to appear dimmer, which can make it easier to see what's behind you at night.

Software developer here , > It seems to me that since every computer will be made out of pre - fed algorithms That 's not accurate . I would n't necessarily call it wrong , but too simplistic . There are algorithms called " genetic algorithms . " The key of these algorithms is that a data stream that is exactly analogous to DNA dictates the behavior of the system , while the algorithm is static and controls mutation , breeding , and fitness . These equations can be used for just about anything . You can literally breed more fuel efficient engines , more aerodynamic cars and planes , you can teach robots to do their job , virtual creatures to walk , or survive in their virtual environments ; these algorithms do make their way into physical robots in industrial production and research labs . The DNA data can be arbitrarily long and represent arbitrarily complicated behavior . To some degree , complexity can be so high that humans basically ca n't explain how it 's working . One famous demonstration used genetic algorithms to configure a programmable processor to recognize human voice . After 40k generations , in software , over the course of a couple weeks , the hardware device was able to identify a human voice perfectly , and the scientists had absolutely no clue how it worked . Parts of the circuit were wired in weird ways and did n't connect to anything . When they removed that part of the circuit , the device stopped working . They could only reason that the algorithm implicitly took advantage of some electrical resonance or bleeding across circuits of that exact , particular , specific processor ( there are always manufacture flaws and variations between chips in a batch ) . There are software and now some fantastic hardware that represents neurons of the brain . These virtual neurons can be wired into a network , usually virtually . They 're allowed to rewire themselves with static algorithms that determine when a change is necessary , and each neuron can " learn " ; they use little equations to determine if they 're supposed to signal their outputs depending on their inputs , and this builds neural path ways . These algorithms are used in cochlear ( hearing ) implants , they 're used in stock market trading , they 're used in robots and vision systems , they 're popular in " learning AI " . Robots and virtual creatures can learn to walk and navigate a maze . They can simulate learning and memory . You 're basically making a little brain . In fact , Google finished a research project a few months ago where they simulated a small brain . The neural net was configured to model the physical structures of real animal brains , since we have a good understanding these days of that . The brain was allowed to consume information from a selection of internet data and guess what ? It really likes cats . No , I 'm not kidding . --- There are plenty more examples and different technologies . The basic algorithm may be static but it may also give rise to astounding complexity . Evolution itself is principally simple and our DNA sequences , when decoded , fill DVDs . We are not driven by simple algorithms of nature , but the astounding complexity ( data , DNA ) that comes from it . Computers are already doing this on a much smaller scale , but there 's no reason a computer ca n't achieve similar complexity . --- Briefly , you have to argue what you consider consciousness and self awareness even is . Does a single sell organism have consciousness ? I say a Furby has consciousness . For example , it is self aware when it is upside down , and it does n't like that . At a very low , primitive level , like that of a single celled organism , it is conscious .

When you buy capital assets ( think of things like stocks ) , you have what is called a " basis " in that asset . Your basis is usually , with some exceptions , what you paid to purchase the asset . So for example , if you bought a share of stock for $ 100 , your basis in that share of stock is $ 100 . When you sell the stock later , if it is worth more than it was when you bought it , you have " capital gain . " The capital gain is the increase in value between your basis and the amount you sold it for . So in this example , lets say that 5 years later , the stock is worth $ 150 . When you sell this stock , you have $ 50 of capital gain . Here is the part that Obama calls the " Trust Fund Loophole . " When a person dies , then can give their capital assets to a relative . When they do this , the basis in the assets given is automatically changed to same value as the current value of the asset . So in our example , if the person did n't sell the stock , and instead gave it to their daughter when they died , the daughter now owns the stock and the basis is $ 150 . This is important because it allows the daughter to sell the stock immediately , and pay no capital gains taxes . Had the father sold it before he died , he would have paid capital gains taxes on the $ 50 of capital gains .

The ocean is endless but interesting places are not . I 'll assume here that the sail is n't on the horizon and we 're starting from scratch . Write a letter to everyone you know to every possible port and ship your target might encounter . Then make copies . Leave the letters for the packets to pick up . Packets were small , robust , but fairly fast ships that were the stagecoaches of the day . Part bus , part mail service , and always smelly and cramped . The packets would run a route picking up your letters and dropping them off at the destinations . Some packets would n't make it or the mail would simply be destroyed enroute . Which is why you made copies . Other times the packet would leave a letter in every port intended for a specific ship . Which is why you made copies . With luck and few weeks or a few months later the letters would be read . Your friends write back and describe the condition of the ship , where it was seen , and what activity it may be up to . Which you 'll read weeks or months later . However now you have a reasonable idea where to start the search . If the ship has weed on the hull then it will be slow and easier to catch . If the ship 's rigging is old and frayed then it will be slow and easier to catch . If the ship 's sails are thin and patched then it will be slow and easier to catch . So now you have an estimate of the speed . Then does the ship have enough water ? Is the ship using gunpowder excessively or conservatively ? Now you can start to guess how well supplied the ship is . This gives an idea of intent . Once the search region is known then it 's all about guessing where the ship will head next . Does the ship head for fresh water ? Does the ship head to a port for food or supplies ? Does the ship head to sea for mischief ? There are only so many interesting places to go . The most direct routes between these places form routes . Guess the destination and either get there first or attempt to intercept the ship at sea between destinations . Wait for days or weeks . Guess right and the sail should appear on the horizon . Now the chase begins . The winner is the faster ship or the one that does n't make the mistake . Luck , such as with the weather gauge , plays a huge factor on who chases whom . Guess wrong and spend the next few weeks or months repeating the process .

Rockets are launched from the ground, not from the top of a mountain, because it is easier and more practical to build the infrastructure needed for launching rockets on the ground. This infrastructure includes things like the launch pad, which is a platform that the rocket sits on before it takes off, and the control center, which is where the people who are in charge of the launch work. 
Launching a rocket from the top of a mountain would be very difficult because the mountain would need to be tall enough and stable enough to support the weight of the rocket and all of its equipment. It would also be hard to build the infrastructure needed for the launch at the top of a mountain. 
Additionally, launching a rocket from sea level, where the air is denser, can actually be more efficient because it allows the rocket to take advantage of the Earth's atmosphere to gain additional speed. This can reduce the amount of fuel that the rocket needs to carry with it, which makes it lighter and allows it to carry more payload (such as satellites or astronauts). 
Finally, it is generally easier and safer to launch rockets from the ground because it is easier to evacuate people and protect property if something goes wrong during the launch.
