When you save your game to the PS2 's memory card , a file is written to some flash memory - just like saving a text document to a USB flash drive . The data in the file is structured - it has specific fields in it that indicate different parts of the saved state of the game . For instance , there might be a section of the file that indicates the maximum health of your character , or whether or not you 've completed a specific quest . When the game goes to load this file off your card , if the data is wrong somehow - the maximum health is off the charts , or your inventory is full of items that do n't exist - then the save file is considered corrupt and ca n't be loaded . Corruption can occur for many reasons . One is that memory is n't always foolproof . Hard drives fail , flash memory can go bad , etc . It was especially bad in the earlier days of flash memory , so GameCube , Playstation , PS2 , etc memory cards were more error prone than the USB keys we use today . The technology has improved somewhat to be more fault tolerant and to work for longer . It 's also possible that the game had a glitch or was turned off while saving , thus writing an invalid or incomplete file to the card . It 's also possible that external conditions could damage the device ( and thus , the file stored on it ) over time . Since there 's typically nothing players can do to recover a corrupted save , the game typically just gives you a generic error and refuses to load the game .

Commercial planes only carry as much fuel as they need for a particular flight because carrying extra fuel can be heavy and costly. When a plane is carrying more weight, it uses more fuel to fly, which means it costs more money to operate the flight. Additionally, the weight of the fuel itself takes up space in the plane that could be used for other things, such as cargo or passengers. 
For example, imagine that you are driving a car and you need to drive 100 miles. If you filled up your gas tank all the way, you would have more gas than you need for the trip. This extra gas would make your car heavier, which would cause it to use more fuel as you drove, and it would also take up space in your car that could be used for other things. It's the same with a plane. The pilots and airline staff calculate exactly how much fuel they need based on factors such as the distance of the flight, the weight of the plane, and the weather conditions, and they only carry that much fuel. This helps to keep the plane as lightweight and efficient as possible.

One factor is training. Men and women may be trained differently in these sports, and as a result, they may develop different muscle groups and techniques for shooting and throwing.

The common core is designed to teach math in a manner consistant with how people think . For instance , 17 from 52 . The way I was taught to so this problem was to write 52 17 And then substract 7 from 2 , which is n't helpful , so you ' borrow ' a 10 from 50 ' and substract 7 from 12 . Cross out the 5 , write a 4 in its place , and write ' 5 ' below the 7 ( 12 - 7 ) . And so on . But that 's not how we subtract naturally . We think " well 52 is close to 50 and 17 is close to 20 . 50 - 20 is easy : 30 . Then add the two you ignored from 52 : 32 , and the 3 you ignored going from 17 to 20 : 35 . " Each of us may choose arbitrary places to put those values ( " 52 is close to 50 ' 17 is close to 15 " ) but the process is the same . We break hard problems down into easy problems and then add back the bits we skipped . That 's part of how the common core teaches math . The old way works , but students came out the other end not knowing why . It produced generations of students who did math purely by cargo cult thinking .

Balloons are made of thin rubber or other stretchy materials that can be filled with air or other gases. When a balloon is filled with air or another gas and then released, it will float up into the air because the gas inside the balloon is less dense than the air outside the balloon.

Eventually, the balloon will reach a point where the air is so thin and cold that the rubber or other material that the balloon is made of becomes brittle and starts to break. When this happens, the balloon will pop, and all the gas inside it will escape. 
After the balloon pops, the remains of the balloon will fall back down to the ground. The remains of the balloon may be small pieces of rubber or other materials, or it may be one larger piece that falls to the ground. The remains of the balloon will usually land on the ground, but they could also land on a roof or other structure if the balloon was floating high enough. 
It is difficult to predict exactly how high a balloon will float before it pops because it depends on a lot of different factors, such as the size of the balloon, the type of gas inside it, and the weather conditions. Some balloons may pop at a relatively low altitude, while others may float much higher before they pop. In general, though, balloons that are released outdoors will eventually pop and fall back to the ground.

Humans are Trichomic , which is a fancy way to say that we see three primary colors . This is because we have three types of color - sensitive cone cells in the eye- one red , one green , and one blue . There is no way for us to see or perceive another primary color because it would require another type of cone cell to be present in our eyes . There are many animals that have a different number types of cone cells in their eyes . This allows them to see different primary colors than us . Dogs for example are Dichromic ( 2 colors ) , and birds are Tetrochromic ( 4 colors ) . Some species have even have more cone types than that . Fun fact : Goldfish are tetrochromic . They can see red , green , blue , and ultraviolet as their primary colors . Exactly how this is any use to them is unknown to me .

Your example is an exception to what I 'm about to say , but typically the passive voice is less clear and less efficient , so all around worse . Take : " The bear was viewed by Stacy " vs. " Stacy viewed the bear . " Passive voice needs more words , yet it does n't convey any extra meaning . So it 's less efficient . Passive voice also attaches the action ( was viewed ) to the passive subject ( the bear ) rather than the active subject ( Stacy ) . We tend to assume that in the phrase " The ( Noun ) ( Verb ) " , the noun is * doing * something . But in passive voice , the noun is having something * done to it * . Which can be confusing . You have to think more to decode the sentence . So it 's less clear . Active writing is more succinct and clearer , more forceful . Plus , most student just use passive voice to pad out their essays and make them longer .
